blocks.text: Nel mezzo del cammin di nostra vita
blocks.mandelbaum: When I had journeyed half of our life’s way,

blocks.text: mi ritrovai per una selva oscura,
blocks.mandelbaum: I found myself within a shadowed forest,

blocks.text: ché la diritta via era smarrita.
blocks.mandelbaum: for I had lost the path that does not stray.

blocks.text: Ahi quanto a dir qual era è cosa dura
blocks.mandelbaum: Ah, it is hard to speak of what it was,

blocks.text: esta selva selvaggia e aspra e forte
blocks.mandelbaum: that savage forest, dense and difficult,

blocks.text: che nel pensier rinova la paura!
blocks.mandelbaum: which even in recall renews my fear:

blocks.text: Tant’ è amara che poco è più morte;
blocks.mandelbaum: so bitter — death is hardly more severe!

blocks.text: ma per trattar del ben ch’i’ vi trovai,
blocks.mandelbaum: But to retell the good discovered there,

blocks.text: dirò de l’altre cose ch’i’ v’ho scorte.
blocks.mandelbaum: I’ll also tell the other things I saw.

blocks.text: Io non so ben ridir com’ i’ v’intrai,
blocks.mandelbaum: I cannot clearly say how I had entered

blocks.text: tant’ era pien di sonno a quel punto
blocks.mandelbaum: the wood; I was so full of sleep just at

blocks.text: che la verace via abbandonai.
blocks.mandelbaum: the point where I abandoned the true path.

blocks.text: Ma poi ch’i’ fui al piè d’un colle giunto,
blocks.mandelbaum: But when I’d reached the bottom of a hill —

blocks.text: là dove terminava quella valle
blocks.mandelbaum: it rose along the boundary of the valley

blocks.text: che m’avea di paura il cor compunto,
blocks.mandelbaum: that had harassed my heart with so much fear —

blocks.text: guardai in alto e vidi le sue spalle
blocks.mandelbaum: I looked on high and saw its shoulders clothed

blocks.text: vestite già de’ raggi del pianeta
blocks.mandelbaum: already by the rays of that same planet

blocks.text: che mena dritto altrui per ogne calle.
blocks.mandelbaum: which serves to lead men straight along all roads.

blocks.text: Allor fu la paura un poco queta,
blocks.mandelbaum: At this my fear was somewhat quieted;

blocks.text: che nel lago del cor m’era durata
blocks.mandelbaum: for through the night of sorrow I had spent,

blocks.text: la notte ch’i’ passai con tanta pieta.
blocks.mandelbaum: the lake within my heart felt terror present.

blocks.text: E come quei che con lena affannata,
blocks.mandelbaum: And just as he who, with exhausted breath,

blocks.text: uscito fuor del pelago a la riva,
blocks.mandelbaum: having escaped from sea to shore, turns back

blocks.text: si volge a l’acqua perigliosa e guata,
blocks.mandelbaum: to watch the dangerous waters he has quit,

blocks.text: così l’animo mio, ch’ancor fuggiva,
blocks.mandelbaum: so did my spirit, still a fugitive,

blocks.text: si volse a retro a rimirar lo passo
blocks.mandelbaum: turn back to look intently at the pass

blocks.text: che non lasciò già mai persona viva.
blocks.mandelbaum: that never has let any man survive.

blocks.text: Poi ch’èi posato un poco il corpo lasso,
blocks.mandelbaum: I let my tired body rest awhile.

blocks.text: ripresi via per la piaggia diserta,
blocks.mandelbaum: Moving again, I tried the lonely slope —

blocks.text: sì che ’l piè fermo sempre era ’l più basso.
blocks.mandelbaum: my firm foot always was the one below.

blocks.text: Ed ecco, quasi al cominciar de l’erta,
blocks.mandelbaum: And almost where the hillside starts to rise —

blocks.text: una lonza leggera e presta molto,
blocks.mandelbaum: look there! — a leopard, very quick and lithe,

blocks.text: che di pel macolato era coverta;
blocks.mandelbaum: a leopard covered with a spotted hide.

blocks.text: e non mi si partia dinanzi al volto,
blocks.mandelbaum: He did not disappear from sight, but stayed;

blocks.text: anzi ’mpediva tanto il mio cammino,
blocks.mandelbaum: indeed, he so impeded my ascent

blocks.text: ch’i’ fui per ritornar più volte vòlto.
blocks.mandelbaum: that I had often to turn back again.

blocks.text: Temp’ era dal principio del mattino,
blocks.mandelbaum: The time was the beginning of the morning;

blocks.text: e ’l sol montava ’n sù con quelle stelle
blocks.mandelbaum: the sun was rising now in fellowship

blocks.text: ch’eran con lui quando l’amor divino
blocks.mandelbaum: with the same stars that had escorted it

blocks.text: mosse di prima quelle cose belle;
blocks.mandelbaum: when Divine Love first moved those things of beauty;

blocks.text: sì ch’a bene sperar m’era cagione
blocks.mandelbaum: so that the hour and the gentle season

blocks.text: di quella fiera a la gaetta pelle
blocks.mandelbaum: gave me good cause for hopefulness on seeing

blocks.text: l’ora del tempo e la dolce stagione;
blocks.mandelbaum: that beast before me with his speckled skin;

blocks.text: ma non sì che paura non mi desse
blocks.mandelbaum: but hope was hardly able to prevent

blocks.text: la vista che m’apparve d’un leone.
blocks.mandelbaum: the fear I felt when I beheld a lion.

blocks.text: Questi parea che contra me venisse
blocks.mandelbaum: His head held high and ravenous with hunger —

blocks.text: con la test’ alta e con rabbiosa fame,
blocks.mandelbaum: even the air around him seemed to shudder —

blocks.text: sì che parea che l’aere ne tremesse.
blocks.mandelbaum: this lion seemed to make his way against me.

blocks.text: Ed una lupa, che di tutte brame
blocks.mandelbaum: And then a she-wolf showed herself; she seemed

blocks.text: sembiava carca ne la sua magrezza,
blocks.mandelbaum: to carry every craving in her leanness;

blocks.text: e molte genti fé già viver grame,
blocks.mandelbaum: she had already brought despair to many.

blocks.text: questa mi porse tanto di gravezza
blocks.mandelbaum: The very sight of her so weighted me

blocks.text: con la paura ch’uscia di sua vista,
blocks.mandelbaum: with fearfulness that I abandoned hope

blocks.text: ch’io perdei la speranza de l’altezza.
blocks.mandelbaum: of ever climbing up that mountain slope.

blocks.text: E qual è quei che volontieri acquista,
blocks.mandelbaum: Even as he who glories while he gains

blocks.text: e giugne ’l tempo che perder lo face,
blocks.mandelbaum: will, when the time has come to tally loss,

blocks.text: che ’n tutti suoi pensier piange e s’attrista;
blocks.mandelbaum: lament with every thought and turn despondent,

blocks.text: tal mi fece la bestia sanza pace,
blocks.mandelbaum: so was I when I faced that restless beast

blocks.text: che, venendomi ’ncontro, a poco a poco
blocks.mandelbaum: which, even as she stalked me, step by step

blocks.text: mi ripigneva là dove ’l sol tace.
blocks.mandelbaum: had thrust me back to where the sun is speechless.

blocks.text: Mentre ch’i’ rovinava in basso loco,
blocks.mandelbaum: While I retreated down to lower ground,

blocks.text: dinanzi a li occhi mi si fu offerto
blocks.mandelbaum: before my eyes there suddenly appeared

blocks.text: chi per lungo silenzio parea fioco.
blocks.mandelbaum: one who seemed faint because of the long silence.

blocks.text: Quando vidi costui nel gran diserto,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I saw him in that vast wilderness,

blocks.text: «Miserere di me», gridai a lui,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Have pity on me,” were the words I cried,

blocks.text: «qual che tu sii, od ombra od omo certo!».
blocks.mandelbaum: “whatever you may be — a shade, a man.”

blocks.text: Rispuosemi: «Non omo, omo già fui,
blocks.mandelbaum: He answered me: “Not man; I once was man.

blocks.text: e li parenti miei furon lombardi,
blocks.mandelbaum: Both of my parents came from Lombardy,

blocks.text: mantoani per patrïa ambedui.
blocks.mandelbaum: and both claimed Mantua as native city.

blocks.text: Nacqui sub Iulio, ancor che fosse tardi,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I was born, though late, sub Julio,

blocks.text: e vissi a Roma sotto ’l buono Augusto
blocks.mandelbaum: and lived in Rome under the good Augustus —

blocks.text: nel tempo de li dèi falsi e bugiardi.
blocks.mandelbaum: the season of the false and lying gods.

blocks.text: Poeta fui, e cantai di quel giusto
blocks.mandelbaum: I was a poet, and I sang the righteous

blocks.text: figliuol d’Anchise che venne di Troia,
blocks.mandelbaum: son of Anchises who had come from Troy

blocks.text: poi che ’l superbo Ilïón fu combusto.
blocks.mandelbaum: when flames destroyed the pride of Ilium.

blocks.text: Ma tu perché ritorni a tanta noia?
blocks.mandelbaum: But why do you return to wretchedness?

blocks.text: perché non sali il dilettoso monte
blocks.mandelbaum: Why not climb up the mountain of delight,

blocks.text: ch’è principio e cagion di tutta gioia?».
blocks.mandelbaum: the origin and cause of every joy?”

blocks.text: «Or se’ tu quel Virgilio e quella fonte
blocks.mandelbaum: “And are you then that Virgil, you the fountain

blocks.text: che spandi di parlar sì largo fiume?»,
blocks.mandelbaum: that freely pours so rich a stream of speech?”

blocks.text: rispuos’ io lui con vergognosa fronte.
blocks.mandelbaum: I answered him with shame upon my brow.

blocks.text: «O de li altri poeti onore e lume,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O light and honor of all other poets,

blocks.text: vagliami ’l lungo studio e ’l grande amore
blocks.mandelbaum: may my long study and the intense love

blocks.text: che m’ha fatto cercar lo tuo volume.
blocks.mandelbaum: that made me search your volume serve me now.

blocks.text: Tu se’ lo mio maestro e ’l mio autore,
blocks.mandelbaum: You are my master and my author, you —

blocks.text: tu se’ solo colui da cu’ io tolsi
blocks.mandelbaum: the only one from whom my writing drew

blocks.text: lo bello stilo che m’ha fatto onore.
blocks.mandelbaum: the noble style for which I have been honored.

blocks.text: Vedi la bestia per cu’ io mi volsi;
blocks.mandelbaum: You see the beast that made me turn aside;

blocks.text: aiutami da lei, famoso saggio,
blocks.mandelbaum: help me, o famous sage, to stand against her,

blocks.text: ch’ella mi fa tremar le vene e i polsi».
blocks.mandelbaum: for she has made my blood and pulses shudder,”

blocks.text: «A te convien tenere altro vïaggio»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “It is another path that you must take,”

blocks.text: rispuose, poi che lagrimar mi vide,
blocks.mandelbaum: he answered when he saw my tearfulness,

blocks.text: «se vuo’ campar d’esto loco selvaggio;
blocks.mandelbaum: “if you would leave this savage wilderness;

blocks.text: ché questa bestia, per la qual tu gride,
blocks.mandelbaum: the beast that is the cause of your outcry

blocks.text: non lascia altrui passar per la sua via,
blocks.mandelbaum: allows no man to pass along her track,

blocks.text: ma tanto lo ’mpedisce che l’uccide;
blocks.mandelbaum: but blocks him even to the point of death;

blocks.text: e ha natura sì malvagia e ria,
blocks.mandelbaum: her nature is so squalid, so malicious

blocks.text: che mai non empie la bramosa voglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: that she can never sate her greedy will;

blocks.text: e dopo ’l pasto ha più fame che pria.
blocks.mandelbaum: when she has fed, she’s hungrier than ever.

blocks.text: Molti son li animali a cui s’ammoglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: She mates with many living souls and shall

blocks.text: e più saranno ancora, infin che ’l veltro
blocks.mandelbaum: yet mate with many more, until the Greyhound

blocks.text: verrà, che la farà morir con doglia.
blocks.mandelbaum: arrives, inflicting painful death on her.

blocks.text: Questi non ciberà terra né peltro,
blocks.mandelbaum: That Hound will never feed on land or pewter,

blocks.text: ma sapïenza, amore e virtute,
blocks.mandelbaum: but find his fare in wisdom, love, and virtue;

blocks.text: e sua nazion sarà tra feltro e feltro.
blocks.mandelbaum: his place of birth shall be between two felts.

blocks.text: Di quella umile Italia fia salute
blocks.mandelbaum: He will restore low-lying Italy for which

blocks.text: per cui morì la vergine Cammilla,
blocks.mandelbaum: the maid Camilla died of wounds,

blocks.text: Eurialo e Turno e Niso di ferute.
blocks.mandelbaum: and Nisus, Turnus, and Euryalus.

blocks.text: Questi la caccerà per ogne villa,
blocks.mandelbaum: And he will hunt that beast through every city

blocks.text: fin che l’avrà rimessa ne lo ’nferno,
blocks.mandelbaum: until he thrusts her back again to Hell,

blocks.text: là onde ’nvidia prima dipartilla.
blocks.mandelbaum: for which she was first sent above by envy.

blocks.text: Ond’ io per lo tuo me’ penso e discerno
blocks.mandelbaum: Therefore, I think and judge it best for you

blocks.text: che tu mi segui, e io sarò tua guida,
blocks.mandelbaum: to follow me, and I shall guide you, taking

blocks.text: e trarrotti di qui per loco etterno;
blocks.mandelbaum: you from this place through an eternal place,

blocks.text: ove udirai le disperate strida,
blocks.mandelbaum: where you shall hear the howls of desperation

blocks.text: vedrai li antichi spiriti dolenti,
blocks.mandelbaum: and see the ancient spirits in their pain,

blocks.text: ch’a la seconda morte ciascun grida;
blocks.mandelbaum: as each of them laments his second death;

blocks.text: e vederai color che son contenti
blocks.mandelbaum: and you shall see those souls who are content

blocks.text: nel foco, perché speran di venire
blocks.mandelbaum: within the fire, for they hope to reach —

blocks.text: quando che sia a le beate genti.
blocks.mandelbaum: whenever that may be — the blessed people.

blocks.text: A le quai poi se tu vorrai salire,
blocks.mandelbaum: If you would then ascend as high as these,

blocks.text: anima fia a ciò più di me degna:
blocks.mandelbaum: a soul more worthy than I am will guide you;

blocks.text: con lei ti lascerò nel mio partire;
blocks.mandelbaum: I’ll leave you in her care when I depart,

blocks.text: ché quello imperador che là sù regna,
blocks.mandelbaum: because that Emperor who reigns above,

blocks.text: perch’ i’ fu’ ribellante a la sua legge,
blocks.mandelbaum: since I have been rebellious to His law,

blocks.text: non vuol che ’n sua città per me si vegna.
blocks.mandelbaum: will not allow me entry to His city.

blocks.text: In tutte parti impera e quivi regge;
blocks.mandelbaum: He governs everywhere, but rules from there;

blocks.text: quivi è la sua città e l’alto seggio:
blocks.mandelbaum: there is His city, His high capital:

blocks.text: oh felice colui cu’ ivi elegge!».
blocks.mandelbaum: o happy those He chooses to be there!”

blocks.text: E io a lui: «Poeta, io ti richeggio
blocks.mandelbaum: And I replied: “O poet — by that God

blocks.text: per quello Dio che tu non conoscesti,
blocks.mandelbaum: whom you had never come to know — I beg you,

blocks.text: acciò ch’io fugga questo male e peggio,
blocks.mandelbaum: that I may flee this evil and worse evils,

blocks.text: che tu mi meni là dov’ or dicesti,
blocks.mandelbaum: to lead me to the place of which you spoke,

blocks.text: sì ch’io veggia la porta di san Pietro
blocks.mandelbaum: that I may see the gateway of Saint Peter

blocks.text: e color cui tu fai cotanto mesti».
blocks.mandelbaum: and those whom you describe as sorrowful.”

blocks.text: Allor si mosse, e io li tenni dietro.
blocks.mandelbaum: Then he set out, and I moved on behind him.

blocks.text: Lo giorno se n’andava, e l’aere bruno
blocks.mandelbaum: The day was now departing; the dark air

blocks.text: toglieva li animai che sono in terra
blocks.mandelbaum: released the living beings of the earth

blocks.text: da le fatiche loro; e io sol uno
blocks.mandelbaum: from work and weariness; and I myself

blocks.text: m’apparecchiava a sostener la guerra
blocks.mandelbaum: alone prepared to undergo the battle

blocks.text: sì del cammino e sì de la pietate,
blocks.mandelbaum: both of the journeying and of the pity,

blocks.text: che ritrarrà la mente che non erra.
blocks.mandelbaum: which memory, mistaking not, shall show.

blocks.text: O muse, o alto ingegno, or m’aiutate;
blocks.mandelbaum: O Muses, o high genius, help me now;

blocks.text: o mente che scrivesti ciò ch’io vidi,
blocks.mandelbaum: o memory that set down what I saw,

blocks.text: qui si parrà la tua nobilitate.
blocks.mandelbaum: here shall your excellence reveal itself!

blocks.text: Io cominciai: «Poeta che mi guidi,
blocks.mandelbaum: I started: “Poet, you who are my guide,

blocks.text: guarda la mia virtù s’ell’ è possente,
blocks.mandelbaum: see if the force in me is strong enough

blocks.text: prima ch’a l’alto passo tu mi fidi.
blocks.mandelbaum: before you let me face that rugged pass.

blocks.text: Tu dici che di Silvïo il parente,
blocks.mandelbaum: You say that he who fathered Sylvius,

blocks.text: corruttibile ancora, ad immortale
blocks.mandelbaum: while he was still corruptible, had journeyed

blocks.text: secolo andò, e fu sensibilmente.
blocks.mandelbaum: into the deathless world with his live body.

blocks.text: Però, se l’avversario d’ogne male
blocks.mandelbaum: For, if the Enemy of every evil

blocks.text: cortese i fu, pensando l’alto effetto
blocks.mandelbaum: was courteous to him, considering

blocks.text: ch’uscir dovea di lui, e ’l chi e ’l quale
blocks.mandelbaum: all he would cause and who and what he was,

blocks.text: non pare indegno ad omo d’intelletto;
blocks.mandelbaum: that does not seem incomprehensible,

blocks.text: ch’e’ fu de l’alma Roma e di suo impero
blocks.mandelbaum: since in the empyrean heaven he was chosen

blocks.text: ne l’empireo ciel per padre eletto:
blocks.mandelbaum: to father honored Rome and her empire;

blocks.text: la quale e ’l quale, a voler dir lo vero,
blocks.mandelbaum: and if the truth be told, Rome and her realm

blocks.text: fu stabilita per lo loco santo
blocks.mandelbaum: were destined to become the sacred place,

blocks.text: u’ siede il successor del maggior Piero.
blocks.mandelbaum: the seat of the successor of great Peter.

blocks.text: Per quest’ andata onde li dai tu vanto,
blocks.mandelbaum: And through the journey you ascribe to him,

blocks.text: intese cose che furon cagione
blocks.mandelbaum: he came to learn of things that were to bring

blocks.text: di sua vittoria e del papale ammanto.
blocks.mandelbaum: his victory and, too, the papal mantle.

blocks.text: Andovvi poi lo Vas d’elezïone,
blocks.mandelbaum: Later the Chosen Vessel travelled there,

blocks.text: per recarne conforto a quella fede
blocks.mandelbaum: to bring us back assurance of that faith

blocks.text: ch’è principio a la via di salvazione.
blocks.mandelbaum: with which the way to our salvation starts.

blocks.text: Ma io, perché venirvi? o chi ’l concede?
blocks.mandelbaum: But why should I go there? Who sanctions it?

blocks.text: Io non Enëa, io non Paulo sono;
blocks.mandelbaum: For I am not Aeneas, am not Paul;

blocks.text: me degno a ciò né io né altri ’l crede.
blocks.mandelbaum: nor I nor others think myself so worthy.

blocks.text: Per che, se del venire io m’abbandono,
blocks.mandelbaum: Therefore, if I consent to start this journey,

blocks.text: temo che la venuta non sia folle.
blocks.mandelbaum: I fear my venture may be wild and empty.

blocks.text: Se’ savio; intendi me’ ch’i’ non ragiono».
blocks.mandelbaum: You’re wise; you know far more than what I say.”

blocks.text: E qual è quei che disvuol ciò che volle
blocks.mandelbaum: And just as he who unwills what he wills

blocks.text: e per novi pensier cangia proposta,
blocks.mandelbaum: and shifts what he intends to seek new ends

blocks.text: sì che dal cominciar tutto si tolle,
blocks.mandelbaum: so that he’s drawn from what he had begun,

blocks.text: tal mi fec’ ïo ’n quella oscura costa,
blocks.mandelbaum: so was I in the midst of that dark land,

blocks.text: perché, pensando, consumai la ’mpresa
blocks.mandelbaum: because, with all my thinking, I annulled

blocks.text: che fu nel cominciar cotanto tosta.
blocks.mandelbaum: the task I had so quickly undertaken.

blocks.text: «S’i’ ho ben la parola tua intesa»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “If I have understood what you have said,”

blocks.text: rispuose del magnanimo quell’ ombra,
blocks.mandelbaum: replied the shade of that great-hearted one,

blocks.text: «l’anima tua è da viltade offesa;
blocks.mandelbaum: “your soul has been assailed by cowardice,

blocks.text: la qual molte fïate l’omo ingombra
blocks.mandelbaum: which often weighs so heavily on a man—

blocks.text: sì che d’onrata impresa lo rivolve,
blocks.mandelbaum: distracting him from honorable trials—

blocks.text: come falso veder bestia quand’ ombra.
blocks.mandelbaum: as phantoms frighten beasts when shadows fall.

blocks.text: Da questa tema acciò che tu ti solve,
blocks.mandelbaum: That you may be delivered from this fear,

blocks.text: dirotti perch’ io venni e quel ch’io ’ntesi
blocks.mandelbaum: I’ll tell you why I came and what I heard

blocks.text: nel primo punto che di te mi dolve.
blocks.mandelbaum: when I first felt compassion for your pain.

blocks.text: Io era tra color che son sospesi,
blocks.mandelbaum: I was among those souls who are suspended;

blocks.text: e donna mi chiamò beata e bella,
blocks.mandelbaum: a lady called to me, so blessed, so lovely

blocks.text: tal che di comandare io la richiesi.
blocks.mandelbaum: that I implored to serve at her command.

blocks.text: Lucevan li occhi suoi più che la stella;
blocks.mandelbaum: Her eyes surpassed the splendor of the star’s;

blocks.text: e cominciommi a dir soave e piana,
blocks.mandelbaum: and she began to speak to me—so gently

blocks.text: con angelica voce, in sua favella:
blocks.mandelbaum: and softly—with angelic voice. She said:

blocks.text: “O anima cortese mantoana,
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘O spirit of the courteous Mantuan,

blocks.text: di cui la fama ancor nel mondo dura,
blocks.mandelbaum: whose fame is still a presence in the world

blocks.text: e durerà quanto ’l mondo lontana,
blocks.mandelbaum: and shall endure as long as the world lasts,

blocks.text: l’amico mio, e non de la ventura,
blocks.mandelbaum: my friend, who has not been the friend of fortune,

blocks.text: ne la diserta piaggia è impedito
blocks.mandelbaum: is hindered in his path along that lonely

blocks.text: sì nel cammin, che vòlt’ è per paura;
blocks.mandelbaum: hillside; he has been turned aside by terror.

blocks.text: e temo che non sia già sì smarrito,
blocks.mandelbaum: From all that I have heard of him in Heaven,

blocks.text: ch’io mi sia tardi al soccorso levata,
blocks.mandelbaum: he is, I fear, already so astray

blocks.text: per quel ch’i’ ho di lui nel cielo udito.
blocks.mandelbaum: that I have come to help him much too late.

blocks.text: Or movi, e con la tua parola ornata
blocks.mandelbaum: Go now; with your persuasive word, with all

blocks.text: e con ciò c’ha mestieri al suo campare,
blocks.mandelbaum: that is required to see that he escapes,

blocks.text: l’aiuta sì ch’i’ ne sia consolata.
blocks.mandelbaum: bring help to him, that I may be consoled.

blocks.text: I’ son Beatrice che ti faccio andare;
blocks.mandelbaum: For I am Beatrice who send you on;

blocks.text: vegno del loco ove tornar disio;
blocks.mandelbaum: I come from where I most long to return;

blocks.text: amor mi mosse, che mi fa parlare.
blocks.mandelbaum: Love prompted me, that Love which makes me speak.

blocks.text: Quando sarò dinanzi al segnor mio,
blocks.mandelbaum: When once again I stand before my Lord,

blocks.text: di te mi loderò sovente a lui”.
blocks.mandelbaum: then I shall often let Him hear your praises.’

blocks.text: Tacette allora, e poi comincia’ io:
blocks.mandelbaum: Now Beatrice was silent. I began:

blocks.text: “O donna di virtù sola per cui
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘O Lady of virtue, the sole reason why

blocks.text: l’umana spezie eccede ogne contento
blocks.mandelbaum: the human race surpasses all that lies

blocks.text: di quel ciel c’ha minor li cerchi sui,
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath the heaven with the smallest spheres,

blocks.text: tanto m’aggrada il tuo comandamento,
blocks.mandelbaum: so welcome is your wish, that even if

blocks.text: che l’ubidir, se già fosse, m’è tardi;
blocks.mandelbaum: it were already done, it would seem tardy;

blocks.text: più non t’è uo’ ch’aprirmi il tuo talento.
blocks.mandelbaum: all you need do is let me know your will.

blocks.text: Ma dimmi la cagion che non ti guardi
blocks.mandelbaum: But tell me why you have not been more prudent—

blocks.text: de lo scender qua giuso in questo centro
blocks.mandelbaum: descending to this center, moving from

blocks.text: de l’ampio loco ove tornar tu ardi”.
blocks.mandelbaum: that spacious place where you long to return?’

blocks.text: “Da che tu vuo’ saver cotanto a dentro,
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘Because you want to fathom things so deeply,

blocks.text: dirotti brievemente”, mi rispuose,
blocks.mandelbaum: I now shall tell you promptly,’ she replied,

blocks.text: “perch’ i’ non temo di venir qua entro.
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘why I am not afraid to enter here.

blocks.text: Temer si dee di sole quelle cose
blocks.mandelbaum: One ought to be afraid of nothing other

blocks.text: c’hanno potenza di fare altrui male;
blocks.mandelbaum: than things possessed of power to do us harm,

blocks.text: de l’altre no, ché non son paurose.
blocks.mandelbaum: but things innocuous need not be feared.

blocks.text: I’ son fatta da Dio, sua mercé, tale,
blocks.mandelbaum: God, in His graciousness, has made me so

blocks.text: che la vostra miseria non mi tange,
blocks.mandelbaum: that this, your misery, cannot touch me;

blocks.text: né fiamma d’esto ’ncendio non m’assale.
blocks.mandelbaum: I can withstand the fires flaming here.

blocks.text: Donna è gentil nel ciel che si compiange
blocks.mandelbaum: In Heaven there’s a gentle lady—one

blocks.text: di questo ’mpedimento ov’ io ti mando,
blocks.mandelbaum: who weeps for the distress toward which I send you,

blocks.text: sì che duro giudicio là sù frange.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that stern judgment up above is shattered.

blocks.text: Questa chiese Lucia in suo dimando
blocks.mandelbaum: And it was she who called upon Lucia,

blocks.text: e disse:—Or ha bisogno il tuo fedele
blocks.mandelbaum: requesting of her: “Now your faithful one

blocks.text: di te, e io a te lo raccomando—.
blocks.mandelbaum: has need of you, and I commend him to you.”

blocks.text: Lucia, nimica di ciascun crudele,
blocks.mandelbaum: Lucia, enemy of every cruelty,

blocks.text: si mosse, e venne al loco dov’ i’ era,
blocks.mandelbaum: arose and made her way to where I was,

blocks.text: che mi sedea con l’antica Rachele.
blocks.mandelbaum: sitting beside the venerable Rachel.

blocks.text: Disse:—Beatrice, loda di Dio vera,
blocks.mandelbaum: She said: “You, Beatrice, true praise of God,

blocks.text: ché non soccorri quei che t’amò tanto,
blocks.mandelbaum: why have you not helped him who loves you so

blocks.text: ch’uscì per te de la volgare schiera?
blocks.mandelbaum: that—for your sake—he’s left the vulgar crowd?

blocks.text: Non odi tu la pieta del suo pianto,
blocks.mandelbaum: Do you not hear the anguish in his cry?

blocks.text: non vedi tu la morte che ’l combatte
blocks.mandelbaum: Do you not see the death he wars against

blocks.text: su la fiumana ove ’l mar non ha vanto?—.
blocks.mandelbaum: upon that river ruthless as the sea?”

blocks.text: Al mondo non fur mai persone ratte
blocks.mandelbaum: No one within this world has ever been

blocks.text: a far lor pro o a fuggir lor danno,
blocks.mandelbaum: so quick to seek his good or flee his harm

blocks.text: com’ io, dopo cotai parole fatte,
blocks.mandelbaum: as I—when she had finished speaking thus—

blocks.text: venni qua giù del mio beato scanno,
blocks.mandelbaum: to come below, down from my blessed station;

blocks.text: fidandomi del tuo parlare onesto,
blocks.mandelbaum: I trusted in your honest utterance,

blocks.text: ch’onora te e quei ch’udito l’hanno”.
blocks.mandelbaum: which honors you and those who’ve listened to you.’

blocks.text: Poscia che m’ebbe ragionato questo,
blocks.mandelbaum: When she had finished with her words to me,

blocks.text: li occhi lucenti lagrimando volse,
blocks.mandelbaum: she turned aside her gleaming, tearful eyes,

blocks.text: per che mi fece del venir più presto.
blocks.mandelbaum: which only made me hurry all the more.

blocks.text: E venni a te così com’ ella volse:
blocks.mandelbaum: And, just as she had wished, I came to you:

blocks.text: d’inanzi a quella fiera ti levai
blocks.mandelbaum: I snatched you from the path of the fierce beast

blocks.text: che del bel monte il corto andar ti tolse.
blocks.mandelbaum: that barred the shortest way up the fair mountain.

blocks.text: Dunque: che è? perché, perché restai,
blocks.mandelbaum: What is it then? Why, why do you resist?

blocks.text: perché tanta viltà nel core allette,
blocks.mandelbaum: Why does your heart host so much cowardice?

blocks.text: perché ardire e franchezza non hai,
blocks.mandelbaum: Where are your daring and your openness

blocks.text: poscia che tai tre donne benedette
blocks.mandelbaum: as long as there are three such blessed women

blocks.text: curan di te ne la corte del cielo,
blocks.mandelbaum: concerned for you within the court of Heaven

blocks.text: e ’l mio parlar tanto ben ti promette?».
blocks.mandelbaum: and my words promise you so great a good?”

blocks.text: Quali fioretti dal notturno gelo
blocks.mandelbaum: As little flowers, which the chill of night

blocks.text: chinati e chiusi, poi che ’l sol li ’mbianca,
blocks.mandelbaum: has bent and huddled, when the white sun strikes

blocks.text: si drizzan tutti aperti in loro stelo,
blocks.mandelbaum: grow straight and open fully on their stems,

blocks.text: tal mi fec’ io di mia virtude stanca,
blocks.mandelbaum: so did I, too, with my exhausted force;

blocks.text: e tanto buono ardire al cor mi corse,
blocks.mandelbaum: and such warm daring rushed into my heart

blocks.text: ch’i’ cominciai come persona franca:
blocks.mandelbaum: that I—as one who has been freed—began:

blocks.text: «Oh pietosa colei che mi soccorse!
blocks.mandelbaum: “O she, compassionate, who has helped me!

blocks.text: e te cortese ch’ubidisti tosto
blocks.mandelbaum: And you who, courteous, obeyed so quickly

blocks.text: a le vere parole che ti porse!
blocks.mandelbaum: the true words that she had addressed to you!

blocks.text: Tu m’hai con disiderio il cor disposto
blocks.mandelbaum: You, with your words, have so disposed my heart

blocks.text: sì al venir con le parole tue,
blocks.mandelbaum: to longing for this journey—I return

blocks.text: ch’i’ son tornato nel primo proposto.
blocks.mandelbaum: to what I was at first prepared to do.

blocks.text: Or va, ch’un sol volere è d’ambedue:
blocks.mandelbaum: Now go; a single will fills both of us:

blocks.text: tu duca, tu segnore e tu maestro».
blocks.mandelbaum: you are my guide, my governor, my master.”

blocks.text: Così li dissi; e poi che mosso fue,
blocks.mandelbaum: These were my words to him; when he advanced

blocks.text: intrai per lo cammino alto e silvestro.
blocks.mandelbaum: I entered on the steep and savage path.

blocks.text: ‘Per me si va ne la città dolente,
blocks.mandelbaum: THROUGH ME THE WAY INTO THE SUFFERING CITY,

blocks.text: per me si va ne l’etterno dolore,
blocks.mandelbaum: THROUGH ME THE WAY TO THE ETERNAL PAIN,

blocks.text: per me si va tra la perduta gente.
blocks.mandelbaum: THROUGH ME THE WAY THAT RUNS AMONG THE LOST.

blocks.text: Giustizia mosse il mio alto fattore;
blocks.mandelbaum: JUSTICE URGED ON MY HIGH ARTIFICER;

blocks.text: fecemi la divina podestate,
blocks.mandelbaum: MY MAKER WAS DIVINE AUTHORITY,

blocks.text: la somma sapïenza e ’l primo amore.
blocks.mandelbaum: THE HIGHEST WISDOM, AND THE PRIMAL LOVE.

blocks.text: Dinanzi a me non fuor cose create
blocks.mandelbaum: BEFORE ME NOTHING BUT ETERNAL THINGS

blocks.text: se non etterne, e io etterno duro.
blocks.mandelbaum: WERE MADE, AND I ENDURE ETERNALLY.

blocks.text: Lasciate ogne speranza, voi ch’intrate’.
blocks.mandelbaum: ABANDON EVERY HOPE, WHO ENTER HERE.

blocks.text: Queste parole di colore oscuro
blocks.mandelbaum: These words—their aspect was obscure—I read

blocks.text: vid’ ïo scritte al sommo d’una porta;
blocks.mandelbaum: inscribed above a gateway, and I said:

blocks.text: per ch’io: «Maestro, il senso lor m’è duro».
blocks.mandelbaum: “Master, their meaning is difficult for me.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me, come persona accorta:
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me, as one who comprehends:

blocks.text: «Qui si convien lasciare ogne sospetto;
blocks.mandelbaum: “Here one must leave behind all hesitation;

blocks.text: ogne viltà convien che qui sia morta.
blocks.mandelbaum: here every cowardice must meet its death.

blocks.text: Noi siam venuti al loco ov’ i’ t’ho detto
blocks.mandelbaum: For we have reached the place of which I spoke,

blocks.text: che tu vedrai le genti dolorose
blocks.mandelbaum: where you will see the miserable people,

blocks.text: c’hanno perduto il ben de l’intelletto».
blocks.mandelbaum: those who have lost the good of the intellect.”

blocks.text: E poi che la sua mano a la mia puose
blocks.mandelbaum: And when, with gladness in his face, he placed

blocks.text: con lieto volto, ond’ io mi confortai,
blocks.mandelbaum: his hand upon my own, to comfort me,

blocks.text: mi mise dentro a le segrete cose.
blocks.mandelbaum: he drew me in among the hidden things.

blocks.text: Quivi sospiri, pianti e alti guai
blocks.mandelbaum: Here sighs and lamentations and loud cries

blocks.text: risonavan per l’aere sanza stelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: were echoing across the starless air,

blocks.text: per ch’io al cominciar ne lagrimai.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that, as soon as I set out, I wept.

blocks.text: Diverse lingue, orribili favelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: Strange utterances, horrible pronouncements,

blocks.text: parole di dolore, accenti d’ira,
blocks.mandelbaum: accents of anger, words of suffering,

blocks.text: voci alte e fioche, e suon di man con elle
blocks.mandelbaum: and voices shrill and faint, and beating hands—

blocks.text: facevano un tumulto, il qual s’aggira
blocks.mandelbaum: all went to make a tumult that will whirl

blocks.text: sempre in quell’ aura sanza tempo tinta,
blocks.mandelbaum: forever through that turbid, timeless air,

blocks.text: come la rena quando turbo spira.
blocks.mandelbaum: like sand that eddies when a whirlwind swirls.

blocks.text: E io ch’avea d’error la testa cinta,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I—my head oppressed by horror—said:

blocks.text: dissi: «Maestro, che è quel ch’i’ odo?
blocks.mandelbaum: “Master, what is it that I hear? Who are

blocks.text: e che gent’ è che par nel duol sì vinta?».
blocks.mandelbaum: those people so defeated by their pain?”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Questo misero modo
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “This miserable way

blocks.text: tegnon l’anime triste di coloro
blocks.mandelbaum: is taken by the sorry souls of those

blocks.text: che visser sanza ’nfamia e sanza lodo.
blocks.mandelbaum: who lived without disgrace and without praise.

blocks.text: Mischiate sono a quel cattivo coro
blocks.mandelbaum: They now commingle with the coward angels,

blocks.text: de li angeli che non furon ribelli
blocks.mandelbaum: the company of those who were not rebels

blocks.text: né fur fedeli a Dio, ma per sé fuoro.
blocks.mandelbaum: nor faithful to their God, but stood apart.

blocks.text: Caccianli i ciel per non esser men belli,
blocks.mandelbaum: The heavens, that their beauty not be lessened,

blocks.text: né lo profondo inferno li riceve,
blocks.mandelbaum: have cast them out, nor will deep Hell receive them—

blocks.text: ch’alcuna gloria i rei avrebber d’elli».
blocks.mandelbaum: even the wicked cannot glory in them.”

blocks.text: E io: «Maestro, che è tanto greve
blocks.mandelbaum: And I: “What is it, master, that oppresses

blocks.text: a lor che lamentar li fa sì forte?».
blocks.mandelbaum: these souls, compelling them to wail so loud?”

blocks.text: Rispuose: «Dicerolti molto breve.
blocks.mandelbaum: He answered: “I shall tell you in few words.

blocks.text: Questi non hanno speranza di morte,
blocks.mandelbaum: Those who are here can place no hope in death,

blocks.text: e la lor cieca vita è tanto bassa,
blocks.mandelbaum: and their blind life is so abject that they

blocks.text: che ’nvidïosi son d’ogne altra sorte.
blocks.mandelbaum: are envious of every other fate.

blocks.text: Fama di loro il mondo esser non lassa;
blocks.mandelbaum: The world will let no fame of theirs endure;

blocks.text: misericordia e giustizia li sdegna:
blocks.mandelbaum: both justice and compassion must disdain them;

blocks.text: non ragioniam di lor, ma guarda e passa».
blocks.mandelbaum: let us not talk of them, but look and pass.”

blocks.text: E io, che riguardai, vidi una ’nsegna
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, looking more closely, saw a banner

blocks.text: che girando correva tanto ratta,
blocks.mandelbaum: that, as it wheeled about, raced on—so quick

blocks.text: che d’ogne posa mi parea indegna;
blocks.mandelbaum: that any respite seemed unsuited to it.

blocks.text: e dietro le venìa sì lunga tratta
blocks.mandelbaum: Behind that banner trailed so long a file

blocks.text: di gente, ch’i’ non averei creduto
blocks.mandelbaum: of people—I should never have believed

blocks.text: che morte tanta n’avesse disfatta.
blocks.mandelbaum: that death could have unmade so many souls.

blocks.text: Poscia ch’io v’ebbi alcun riconosciuto,
blocks.mandelbaum: After I had identified a few,

blocks.text: vidi e conobbi l’ombra di colui
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw and recognized the shade of him

blocks.text: che fece per viltade il gran rifiuto.
blocks.mandelbaum: who made, through cowardice, the great refusal.

blocks.text: Incontanente intesi e certo fui
blocks.mandelbaum: At once I understood with certainty:

blocks.text: che questa era la setta d’i cattivi,
blocks.mandelbaum: this company contained the cowardly,

blocks.text: a Dio spiacenti e a’ nemici sui.
blocks.mandelbaum: hateful to God and to His enemies.

blocks.text: Questi sciaurati, che mai non fur vivi,
blocks.mandelbaum: These wretched ones, who never were alive,

blocks.text: erano ignudi e stimolati molto
blocks.mandelbaum: went naked and were stung again, again

blocks.text: da mosconi e da vespe ch’eran ivi.
blocks.mandelbaum: by horseflies and by wasps that circled them.

blocks.text: Elle rigavan lor di sangue il volto,
blocks.mandelbaum: The insects streaked their faces with their blood,

blocks.text: che, mischiato di lagrime, a’ lor piedi
blocks.mandelbaum: which, mingled with their tears, fell at their feet,

blocks.text: da fastidiosi vermi era ricolto.
blocks.mandelbaum: where it was gathered up by sickening worms.

blocks.text: E poi ch’a riguardar oltre mi diedi,
blocks.mandelbaum: And then, looking beyond them, I could see

blocks.text: vidi genti a la riva d’un gran fiume;
blocks.mandelbaum: a crowd along the bank of a great river;

blocks.text: per ch’io dissi: «Maestro, or mi concedi
blocks.mandelbaum: at which I said: “Allow me now to know

blocks.text: ch’i’ sappia quali sono, e qual costume
blocks.mandelbaum: who are these people—master—and what law

blocks.text: le fa di trapassar parer sì pronte,
blocks.mandelbaum: has made them seem so eager for the crossing,

blocks.text: com’ i’ discerno per lo fioco lume».
blocks.mandelbaum: as I can see despite the feeble light.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Le cose ti fier conte
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “When we have stopped along

blocks.text: quando noi fermerem li nostri passi
blocks.mandelbaum: the melancholy shore of Acheron,

blocks.text: su la trista riviera d’Acheronte».
blocks.mandelbaum: then all these matters will be plain to you.”

blocks.text: Allor con li occhi vergognosi e bassi,
blocks.mandelbaum: At that, with eyes ashamed, downcast, and fearing

blocks.text: temendo no ’l mio dir li fosse grave,
blocks.mandelbaum: that what I said had given him offense,

blocks.text: infino al fiume del parlar mi trassi.
blocks.mandelbaum: I did not speak until we reached the river.

blocks.text: Ed ecco verso noi venir per nave
blocks.mandelbaum: And here, advancing toward us, in a boat,

blocks.text: un vecchio, bianco per antico pelo,
blocks.mandelbaum: an aged man—his hair was white with years —

blocks.text: gridando: «Guai a voi, anime prave!
blocks.mandelbaum: was shouting: “Woe to you, corrupted souls!

blocks.text: Non isperate mai veder lo cielo:
blocks.mandelbaum: Forget your hope of ever seeing Heaven:

blocks.text: i’ vegno per menarvi a l’altra riva
blocks.mandelbaum: I come to lead you to the other shore,

blocks.text: ne le tenebre etterne, in caldo e ’n gelo.
blocks.mandelbaum: to the eternal dark, to fire and frost.

blocks.text: E tu che se’ costì, anima viva,
blocks.mandelbaum: And you approaching there, you living soul,

blocks.text: pàrtiti da cotesti che son morti».
blocks.mandelbaum: keep well away from these—they are the dead.”

blocks.text: Ma poi che vide ch’io non mi partiva,
blocks.mandelbaum: But when he saw I made no move to go,

blocks.text: disse: «Per altra via, per altri porti
blocks.mandelbaum: he said: “Another way and other harbors—

blocks.text: verrai a piaggia, non qui, per passare:
blocks.mandelbaum: not here—will bring you passage to your shore:

blocks.text: più lieve legno convien che ti porti».
blocks.mandelbaum: a lighter craft will have to carry you.”

blocks.text: E ’l duca lui: «Caron, non ti crucciare:
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide then: “Charon, don’t torment yourself:

blocks.text: vuolsi così colà dove si puote
blocks.mandelbaum: our passage has been willed above, where One

blocks.text: ciò che si vuole, e più non dimandare».
blocks.mandelbaum: can do what He has willed; and ask no more.”

blocks.text: Quinci fuor quete le lanose gote
blocks.mandelbaum: Now silence fell upon the wooly cheeks

blocks.text: al nocchier de la livida palude,
blocks.mandelbaum: of Charon, pilot of the livid marsh,

blocks.text: che ’ntorno a li occhi avea di fiamme rote.
blocks.mandelbaum: whose eyes were ringed about with wheels of flame.

blocks.text: Ma quell’ anime, ch’eran lasse e nude,
blocks.mandelbaum: But all those spirits, naked and exhausted,

blocks.text: cangiar colore e dibattero i denti,
blocks.mandelbaum: had lost their color, and they gnashed their teeth

blocks.text: ratto che ’nteser le parole crude.
blocks.mandelbaum: as soon as they heard Charon’s cruel words;

blocks.text: Bestemmiavano Dio e lor parenti,
blocks.mandelbaum: they execrated God and their own parents

blocks.text: l’umana spezie e ’l loco e ’l tempo e ’l seme
blocks.mandelbaum: and humankind, and then the place and time

blocks.text: di lor semenza e di lor nascimenti.
blocks.mandelbaum: of their conception’s seed and of their birth.

blocks.text: Poi si ritrasser tutte quante insieme,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then they forgathered, huddled in one throng,

blocks.text: forte piangendo, a la riva malvagia
blocks.mandelbaum: weeping aloud along that wretched shore

blocks.text: ch’attende ciascun uom che Dio non teme.
blocks.mandelbaum: which waits for all who have no fear of God.

blocks.text: Caron dimonio, con occhi di bragia
blocks.mandelbaum: The demon Charon, with his eyes like embers,

blocks.text: loro accennando, tutte le raccoglie;
blocks.mandelbaum: by signaling to them, has all embark;

blocks.text: batte col remo qualunque s’adagia.
blocks.mandelbaum: his oar strikes anyone who stretches out.

blocks.text: Come d’autunno si levan le foglie
blocks.mandelbaum: As, in the autumn, leaves detach themselves,

blocks.text: l’una appresso de l’altra, fin che ’l ramo
blocks.mandelbaum: first one and then the other, till the bough

blocks.text: vede a la terra tutte le sue spoglie,
blocks.mandelbaum: sees all its fallen garments on the ground,

blocks.text: similemente il mal seme d’Adamo
blocks.mandelbaum: similarly, the evil seed of Adam

blocks.text: gittansi di quel lito ad una ad una,
blocks.mandelbaum: descended from the shoreline one by one,

blocks.text: per cenni come augel per suo richiamo.
blocks.mandelbaum: when signaled, as a falcon—called—will come.

blocks.text: Così sen vanno su per l’onda bruna,
blocks.mandelbaum: So do they move across the darkened waters;

blocks.text: e avanti che sien di là discese,
blocks.mandelbaum: even before they reach the farther shore,

blocks.text: anche di qua nuova schiera s’auna.
blocks.mandelbaum: new ranks already gather on this bank.

blocks.text: «Figliuol mio», disse ’l maestro cortese,
blocks.mandelbaum: “My son,” the gracious master said to me,

blocks.text: «quelli che muoion ne l’ira di Dio
blocks.mandelbaum: “those who have died beneath the wrath of God,

blocks.text: tutti convegnon qui d’ogne paese;
blocks.mandelbaum: all these assemble here from every country;

blocks.text: e pronti sono a trapassar lo rio,
blocks.mandelbaum: and they are eager for the river crossing

blocks.text: ché la divina giustizia li sprona,
blocks.mandelbaum: because celestial justice spurs them on,

blocks.text: sì che la tema si volve in disio.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that their fear is turned into desire.

blocks.text: Quinci non passa mai anima buona;
blocks.mandelbaum: No good soul ever takes its passage here;

blocks.text: e però, se Caron di te si lagna,
blocks.mandelbaum: therefore, if Charon has complained of you,

blocks.text: ben puoi sapere omai che ’l suo dir suona».
blocks.mandelbaum: by now you can be sure what his words mean.”

blocks.text: Finito questo, la buia campagna
blocks.mandelbaum: And after this was said, the darkened plain

blocks.text: tremò sì forte, che de lo spavento
blocks.mandelbaum: quaked so tremendously—the memory

blocks.text: la mente di sudore ancor mi bagna.
blocks.mandelbaum: of terror then, bathes me in sweat again.

blocks.text: La terra lagrimosa diede vento,
blocks.mandelbaum: A whirlwind burst out of the tear-drenched earth,

blocks.text: che balenò una luce vermiglia
blocks.mandelbaum: a wind that crackled with a bloodred light,

blocks.text: la qual mi vinse ciascun sentimento;
blocks.mandelbaum: a light that overcame all of my senses;

blocks.text: e caddi come l’uom cui sonno piglia.
blocks.mandelbaum: and like a man whom sleep has seized, I fell.

blocks.text: Ruppemi l’alto sonno ne la testa
blocks.mandelbaum: The heavy sleep within my head was smashed

blocks.text: un greve truono, sì ch’io mi riscossi
blocks.mandelbaum: by an enormous thunderclap, so that

blocks.text: come persona ch’è per forza desta;
blocks.mandelbaum: I started up as one whom force awakens;

blocks.text: e l’occhio riposato intorno mossi,
blocks.mandelbaum: I stood erect and turned my rested eyes

blocks.text: dritto levato, e fiso riguardai
blocks.mandelbaum: from side to side, and I stared steadily

blocks.text: per conoscer lo loco dov’ io fossi.
blocks.mandelbaum: to learn what place it was surrounding me.

blocks.text: Vero è che ’n su la proda mi trovai
blocks.mandelbaum: In truth I found myself upon the brink

blocks.text: de la valle d’abisso dolorosa
blocks.mandelbaum: of an abyss, the melancholy valley

blocks.text: che ’ntrono accoglie d’infiniti guai.
blocks.mandelbaum: containing thundering, unending wailings.

blocks.text: Oscura e profonda era e nebulosa
blocks.mandelbaum: That valley, dark and deep and filled with mist,

blocks.text: tanto che, per ficcar lo viso a fondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: is such that, though I gazed into its pit,

blocks.text: io non vi discernea alcuna cosa.
blocks.mandelbaum: I was unable to discern a thing.

blocks.text: «Or discendiam qua giù nel cieco mondo»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Let us descend into the blind world now,”

blocks.text: cominciò il poeta tutto smorto.
blocks.mandelbaum: the poet, who was deathly pale, began;

blocks.text: «Io sarò primo, e tu sarai secondo».
blocks.mandelbaum: “I shall go first and you will follow me.”

blocks.text: E io, che del color mi fui accorto,
blocks.mandelbaum: But I, who’d seen the change in his complexion,

blocks.text: dissi: «Come verrò, se tu paventi
blocks.mandelbaum: said: “How shall I go on if you are frightened,

blocks.text: che suoli al mio dubbiare esser conforto?».
blocks.mandelbaum: you who have always helped dispel my doubts?”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «L’angoscia de le genti
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “The anguish of the people

blocks.text: che son qua giù, nel viso mi dipigne
blocks.mandelbaum: whose place is here below, has touched my face

blocks.text: quella pietà che tu per tema senti.
blocks.mandelbaum: with the compassion you mistake for fear.

blocks.text: Andiam, ché la via lunga ne sospigne».
blocks.mandelbaum: Let us go on, the way that waits is long.”

blocks.text: Così si mise e così mi fé intrare
blocks.mandelbaum: So he set out, and so he had me enter

blocks.text: nel primo cerchio che l’abisso cigne.
blocks.mandelbaum: on that first circle girdling the abyss.

blocks.text: Quivi, secondo che per ascoltare,
blocks.mandelbaum: Here, for as much as hearing could discover,

blocks.text: non avea pianto mai che di sospiri
blocks.mandelbaum: there was no outcry louder than the sighs

blocks.text: che l’aura etterna facevan tremare;
blocks.mandelbaum: that caused the everlasting air to tremble.

blocks.text: ciò avvenia di duol sanza martìri,
blocks.mandelbaum: The sighs arose from sorrow without torments,

blocks.text: ch’avean le turbe, ch’eran molte e grandi,
blocks.mandelbaum: out of the crowds—the many multitudes—

blocks.text: d’infanti e di femmine e di viri.
blocks.mandelbaum: of infants and of women and of men.

blocks.text: Lo buon maestro a me: «Tu non dimandi
blocks.mandelbaum: The kindly master said: “Do you not ask

blocks.text: che spiriti son questi che tu vedi?
blocks.mandelbaum: who are these spirits whom you see before you?

blocks.text: Or vo’ che sappi, innanzi che più andi,
blocks.mandelbaum: I’d have you know, before you go ahead,

blocks.text: ch’ei non peccaro; e s’elli hanno mercedi,
blocks.mandelbaum: they did not sin; and yet, though they have merits,

blocks.text: non basta, perché non ebber battesmo,
blocks.mandelbaum: that’s not enough, because they lacked baptism,

blocks.text: ch’è porta de la fede che tu credi;
blocks.mandelbaum: the portal of the faith that you embrace.

blocks.text: e s’e’ furon dinanzi al cristianesmo,
blocks.mandelbaum: And if they lived before Christianity,

blocks.text: non adorar debitamente a Dio:
blocks.mandelbaum: they did not worship God in fitting ways;

blocks.text: e di questi cotai son io medesmo.
blocks.mandelbaum: and of such spirits I myself am one.

blocks.text: Per tai difetti, non per altro rio,
blocks.mandelbaum: For these defects, and for no other evil,

blocks.text: semo perduti, e sol di tanto offesi
blocks.mandelbaum: we now are lost and punished just with this:

blocks.text: che sanza speme vivemo in disio».
blocks.mandelbaum: we have no hope and yet we live in longing.”

blocks.text: Gran duol mi prese al cor quando lo ’ntesi,
blocks.mandelbaum: Great sorrow seized my heart on hearing him,

blocks.text: però che gente di molto valore
blocks.mandelbaum: for I had seen some estimable men

blocks.text: conobbi che ’n quel limbo eran sospesi.
blocks.mandelbaum: among the souls suspended in that limbo.

blocks.text: «Dimmi, maestro mio, dimmi, segnore»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Tell me, my master, tell me, lord.” I then

blocks.text: comincia’ io per voler esser certo
blocks.mandelbaum: began because I wanted to be certain

blocks.text: di quella fede che vince ogne errore:
blocks.mandelbaum: of that belief which vanquishes all errors,

blocks.text: «uscicci mai alcuno, o per suo merto
blocks.mandelbaum: “did any ever go—by his own merit

blocks.text: o per altrui, che poi fosse beato?».
blocks.mandelbaum: or others’—from this place toward blessedness?”

blocks.text: E quei che ’ntese il mio parlar coverto,
blocks.mandelbaum: And he, who understood my covert speech,

blocks.text: rispuose: «Io era nuovo in questo stato,
blocks.mandelbaum: replied: “I was new—entered on this state

blocks.text: quando ci vidi venire un possente,
blocks.mandelbaum: when I beheld a Great Lord enter here;

blocks.text: con segno di vittoria coronato.
blocks.mandelbaum: the crown he wore, a sign of victory.

blocks.text: Trasseci l’ombra del primo parente,
blocks.mandelbaum: He carried off the shade of our first father,

blocks.text: d’Abèl suo figlio e quella di Noè,
blocks.mandelbaum: of his son Abel, and the shade of Noah,

blocks.text: di Moïsè legista e ubidente;
blocks.mandelbaum: of Moses, the obedient legislator,

blocks.text: Abraàm patrïarca e Davìd re,
blocks.mandelbaum: of father Abraham, David the king,

blocks.text: Israèl con lo padre e co’ suoi nati
blocks.mandelbaum: of Israel, his father, and his sons,

blocks.text: e con Rachele, per cui tanto fé,
blocks.mandelbaum: and Rachel, she for whom he worked so long,

blocks.text: e altri molti, e feceli beati.
blocks.mandelbaum: and many others—and He made them blessed;

blocks.text: E vo’ che sappi che, dinanzi ad essi,
blocks.mandelbaum: and I should have you know that, before them,

blocks.text: spiriti umani non eran salvati».
blocks.mandelbaum: there were no human souls that had been saved.”

blocks.text: Non lasciavam l’andar perch’ ei dicessi,
blocks.mandelbaum: We did not stay our steps although he spoke;

blocks.text: ma passavam la selva tuttavia,
blocks.mandelbaum: we still continued onward through the wood—

blocks.text: la selva, dico, di spiriti spessi.
blocks.mandelbaum: the wood, I say, where many spirits thronged.

blocks.text: Non era lunga ancor la nostra via
blocks.mandelbaum: Our path had not gone far beyond the point

blocks.text: di qua dal sonno, quand’ io vidi un foco
blocks.mandelbaum: where I had slept, when I beheld a fire

blocks.text: ch’emisperio di tenebre vincia.
blocks.mandelbaum: win out against a hemisphere of shadows.

blocks.text: Di lungi n’eravamo ancora un poco,
blocks.mandelbaum: We still were at a little distance from it,

blocks.text: ma non sì ch’io non discernessi in parte
blocks.mandelbaum: but not so far I could not see in part

blocks.text: ch’orrevol gente possedea quel loco.
blocks.mandelbaum: that honorable men possessed that place.

blocks.text: «O tu ch’onori scïenzïa e arte,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O you who honor art and science both,

blocks.text: questi chi son c’hanno cotanta onranza,
blocks.mandelbaum: who are these souls whose dignity has kept

blocks.text: che dal modo de li altri li diparte?».
blocks.mandelbaum: their way of being, separate from the rest?”

blocks.text: E quelli a me: «L’onrata nominanza
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “The honor of their name,

blocks.text: che di lor suona sù ne la tua vita,
blocks.mandelbaum: which echoes up above within your life,

blocks.text: grazïa acquista in ciel che sì li avanza».
blocks.mandelbaum: gains Heaven’s grace, and that advances them.”

blocks.text: Intanto voce fu per me udita:
blocks.mandelbaum: Meanwhile there was a voice that I could hear:

blocks.text: «Onorate l’altissimo poeta;
blocks.mandelbaum: “Pay honor to the estimable poet;

blocks.text: l’ombra sua torna, ch’era dipartita».
blocks.mandelbaum: his shadow, which had left us, now returns.”

blocks.text: Poi che la voce fu restata e queta,
blocks.mandelbaum: After that voice was done, when there was silence,

blocks.text: vidi quattro grand’ ombre a noi venire:
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw four giant shades approaching us;

blocks.text: sembianz’ avevan né trista né lieta.
blocks.mandelbaum: in aspect, they were neither sad nor joyous.

blocks.text: Lo buon maestro cominciò a dire:
blocks.mandelbaum: My kindly master then began by saying:

blocks.text: «Mira colui con quella spada in mano,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Look well at him who holds that sword in hand

blocks.text: che vien dinanzi ai tre sì come sire:
blocks.mandelbaum: who moves before the other three as lord.

blocks.text: quelli è Omero poeta sovrano;
blocks.mandelbaum: That shade is Homer, the consummate poet;

blocks.text: l’altro è Orazio satiro che vene;
blocks.mandelbaum: the other one is Horace, satirist;

blocks.text: Ovidio è ’l terzo, e l’ultimo Lucano.
blocks.mandelbaum: the third is Ovid, and the last is Lucan.

blocks.text: Però che ciascun meco si convene
blocks.mandelbaum: Because each of these spirits shares with me

blocks.text: nel nome che sonò la voce sola,
blocks.mandelbaum: the name called out before by the lone voice,

blocks.text: fannomi onore, e di ciò fanno bene».
blocks.mandelbaum: they welcome me—and, doing that, do well.”

blocks.text: Così vid’ i’ adunar la bella scola
blocks.mandelbaum: And so I saw that splendid school assembled

blocks.text: di quel segnor de l’altissimo canto
blocks.mandelbaum: led by the lord of song incomparable,

blocks.text: che sovra li altri com’ aquila vola.
blocks.mandelbaum: who like an eagle soars above the rest.

blocks.text: Da ch’ebber ragionato insieme alquanto,
blocks.mandelbaum: Soon after they had talked a while together,

blocks.text: volsersi a me con salutevol cenno,
blocks.mandelbaum: they turned to me, saluting cordially;

blocks.text: e ’l mio maestro sorrise di tanto;
blocks.mandelbaum: and having witnessed this, my master smiled;

blocks.text: e più d’onore ancora assai mi fenno,
blocks.mandelbaum: and even greater honor then was mine,

blocks.text: ch’e’ sì mi fecer de la loro schiera,
blocks.mandelbaum: for they invited me to join their ranks—

blocks.text: sì ch’io fui sesto tra cotanto senno.
blocks.mandelbaum: I was the sixth among such intellects.

blocks.text: Così andammo infino a la lumera,
blocks.mandelbaum: So did we move along and toward the light,

blocks.text: parlando cose che ’l tacere è bello,
blocks.mandelbaum: talking of things about which silence here

blocks.text: sì com’ era ’l parlar colà dov’ era.
blocks.mandelbaum: is just as seemly as our speech was there.

blocks.text: Venimmo al piè d’un nobile castello,
blocks.mandelbaum: We reached the base of an exalted castle,

blocks.text: sette volte cerchiato d’alte mura,
blocks.mandelbaum: encircled seven times by towering walls,

blocks.text: difeso intorno d’un bel fiumicello.
blocks.mandelbaum: defended all around by a fair stream.

blocks.text: Questo passammo come terra dura;
blocks.mandelbaum: We forded this as if upon hard ground;

blocks.text: per sette porte intrai con questi savi:
blocks.mandelbaum: I entered seven portals with these sages;

blocks.text: giugnemmo in prato di fresca verdura.
blocks.mandelbaum: we reached a meadow of green flowering plants.

blocks.text: Genti v’eran con occhi tardi e gravi,
blocks.mandelbaum: The people here had eyes both grave and slow;

blocks.text: di grande autorità ne’ lor sembianti:
blocks.mandelbaum: their features carried great authority;

blocks.text: parlavan rado, con voci soavi.
blocks.mandelbaum: they spoke infrequently, with gentle voices.

blocks.text: Traemmoci così da l’un de’ canti,
blocks.mandelbaum: We drew aside to one part of the meadow,

blocks.text: in loco aperto, luminoso e alto,
blocks.mandelbaum: an open place both high and filled with light,

blocks.text: sì che veder si potien tutti quanti.
blocks.mandelbaum: and we could see all those who were assembled.

blocks.text: Colà diritto, sovra ’l verde smalto,
blocks.mandelbaum: Facing me there, on the enameled green,

blocks.text: mi fuor mostrati li spiriti magni,
blocks.mandelbaum: great—hearted souls were shown to me and I

blocks.text: che del vedere in me stesso m’essalto.
blocks.mandelbaum: still glory in my having witnessed them.

blocks.text: I’ vidi Eletra con molti compagni,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw Electra with her many comrades,

blocks.text: tra ’ quai conobbi Ettòr ed Enea,
blocks.mandelbaum: among whom I knew Hector and Aeneas,

blocks.text: Cesare armato con li occhi grifagni.
blocks.mandelbaum: and Caesar, in his armor, falcon-eyed.

blocks.text: Vidi Cammilla e la Pantasilea;
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw Camilla and Penthesilea

blocks.text: da l’altra parte vidi ’l re Latino
blocks.mandelbaum: and, on the other side, saw King Latinus,

blocks.text: che con Lavina sua figlia sedea.
blocks.mandelbaum: who sat beside Lavinia, his daughter.

blocks.text: Vidi quel Bruto che cacciò Tarquino,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw that Brutus who drove Tarquin out,

blocks.text: Lucrezia, Iulia, Marzïa e Corniglia;
blocks.mandelbaum: Lucretia, Julia, Marcia, and Cornelia,

blocks.text: e solo, in parte, vidi ’l Saladino.
blocks.mandelbaum: and, solitary, set apart, Saladin.

blocks.text: Poi ch’innalzai un poco più le ciglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I had raised my eyes a little higher,

blocks.text: vidi ’l maestro di color che sanno
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw the master of the men who know

blocks.text: seder tra filosofica famiglia.
blocks.mandelbaum: seated in philosophic family.

blocks.text: Tutti lo miran, tutti onor li fanno:
blocks.mandelbaum: There all look up to him, all do him honor:

blocks.text: quivi vid’ ïo Socrate e Platone,
blocks.mandelbaum: there I beheld both Socrates and Plato,

blocks.text: che ’nnanzi a li altri più presso li stanno;
blocks.mandelbaum: closest to him, in front of all the rest;

blocks.text: Democrito che ’l mondo a caso pone,
blocks.mandelbaum: Democritus, who ascribes the world to chance,

blocks.text: Dïogenès, Anassagora e Tale,
blocks.mandelbaum: Diogenes, Empedocles, and Zeno,

blocks.text: Empedoclès, Eraclito e Zenone;
blocks.mandelbaum: and Thales, Anaxagoras, Heraclitus;

blocks.text: e vidi il buono accoglitor del quale,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw the good collector of medicinals,

blocks.text: Dïascoride dico; e vidi Orfeo,
blocks.mandelbaum: I mean Dioscorides; and I saw Orpheus,

blocks.text: Tulïo e Lino e Seneca morale;
blocks.mandelbaum: and Tully, Linus, moral Seneca;

blocks.text: Euclide geomètra e Tolomeo,
blocks.mandelbaum: and Euclid the geometer, and Ptolemy,

blocks.text: Ipocràte, Avicenna e Galïeno,
blocks.mandelbaum: Hippocrates and Galen, Avicenna,

blocks.text: Averoìs, che ’l gran comento feo.
blocks.mandelbaum: Averroes, of the great Commentary.

blocks.text: Io non posso ritrar di tutti a pieno,
blocks.mandelbaum: I cannot here describe them all in full;

blocks.text: però che sì mi caccia il lungo tema,
blocks.mandelbaum: my ample theme impels me onward so:

blocks.text: che molte volte al fatto il dir vien meno.
blocks.mandelbaum: what’s told is often less than the event.

blocks.text: La sesta compagnia in due si scema:
blocks.mandelbaum: The company of six divides in two;

blocks.text: per altra via mi mena il savio duca,
blocks.mandelbaum: my knowing guide leads me another way,

blocks.text: fuor de la queta, ne l’aura che trema.
blocks.mandelbaum: beyond the quiet, into trembling air.

blocks.text: E vegno in parte ove non è che luca.
blocks.mandelbaum: And I have reached a part where no thing gleams.

blocks.text: Così discesi del cerchio primaio
blocks.mandelbaum: So I descended from the first enclosure

blocks.text: giù nel secondo, che men loco cinghia
blocks.mandelbaum: down to the second circle, that which girdles

blocks.text: e tanto più dolor, che punge a guaio.
blocks.mandelbaum: less space but grief more great, that goads to weeping.

blocks.text: Stavvi Minòs orribilmente, e ringhia:
blocks.mandelbaum: There dreadful Minos stands, gnashing his teeth:

blocks.text: essamina le colpe ne l’intrata;
blocks.mandelbaum: examining the sins of those who enter,

blocks.text: giudica e manda secondo ch’avvinghia.
blocks.mandelbaum: he judges and assigns as his tail twines.

blocks.text: Dico che quando l’anima mal nata
blocks.mandelbaum: I mean that when the spirit born to evil

blocks.text: li vien dinanzi, tutta si confessa;
blocks.mandelbaum: appears before him, it confesses all;

blocks.text: e quel conoscitor de le peccata
blocks.mandelbaum: and he, the connoisseur of sin, can tell

blocks.text: vede qual loco d’inferno è da essa;
blocks.mandelbaum: the depth in Hell appropriate to it;

blocks.text: cignesi con la coda tante volte
blocks.mandelbaum: as many times as Minos wraps his tail

blocks.text: quantunque gradi vuol che giù sia messa.
blocks.mandelbaum: around himself, that marks the sinner’s level.

blocks.text: Sempre dinanzi a lui ne stanno molte:
blocks.mandelbaum: Always there is a crowd that stands before him:

blocks.text: vanno a vicenda ciascuna al giudizio,
blocks.mandelbaum: each soul in turn advances toward that judgment;

blocks.text: dicono e odono e poi son giù volte.
blocks.mandelbaum: they speak and hear, then they are cast below.

blocks.text: «O tu che vieni al doloroso ospizio»,
blocks.mandelbaum: Arresting his extraordinary task,

blocks.text: disse Minòs a me quando mi vide,
blocks.mandelbaum: Minos, as soon as he had seen me, said:

blocks.text: lasciando l’atto di cotanto offizio,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O you who reach this house of suffering,

blocks.text: «guarda com’ entri e di cui tu ti fide;
blocks.mandelbaum: be careful how you enter, whom you trust;

blocks.text: non t’inganni l’ampiezza de l’intrare!».
blocks.mandelbaum: the gate is wide, but do not be deceived!”

blocks.text: E ’l duca mio a lui: «Perché pur gride?
blocks.mandelbaum: To which my guide replied: “But why protest?

blocks.text: Non impedir lo suo fatale andare:
blocks.mandelbaum: Do not attempt to block his fated path:

blocks.text: vuolsi così colà dove si puote
blocks.mandelbaum: our passage has been willed above, where One

blocks.text: ciò che si vuole, e più non dimandare».
blocks.mandelbaum: can do what He has willed; and ask no more.”

blocks.text: Or incomincian le dolenti note
blocks.mandelbaum: Now notes of desperation have begun

blocks.text: a farmisi sentire; or son venuto
blocks.mandelbaum: to overtake my hearing; now I come

blocks.text: là dove molto pianto mi percuote.
blocks.mandelbaum: where mighty lamentation beats against me.

blocks.text: Io venni in loco d’ogne luce muto,
blocks.mandelbaum: I reached a place where every light is muted,

blocks.text: che mugghia come fa mar per tempesta,
blocks.mandelbaum: which bellows like the sea beneath a tempest,

blocks.text: se da contrari venti è combattuto.
blocks.mandelbaum: when it is battered by opposing winds.

blocks.text: La bufera infernal, che mai non resta,
blocks.mandelbaum: The hellish hurricane, which never rests,

blocks.text: mena li spirti con la sua rapina;
blocks.mandelbaum: drives on the spirits with its violence:

blocks.text: voltando e percotendo li molesta.
blocks.mandelbaum: wheeling and pounding, it harasses them.

blocks.text: Quando giungon davanti a la ruina,
blocks.mandelbaum: When they come up against the ruined slope,

blocks.text: quivi le strida, il compianto, il lamento;
blocks.mandelbaum: then there are cries and wailing and lament,

blocks.text: bestemmian quivi la virtù divina.
blocks.mandelbaum: and there they curse the force of the divine.

blocks.text: Intesi ch’a così fatto tormento
blocks.mandelbaum: I learned that those who undergo this torment

blocks.text: enno dannati i peccator carnali,
blocks.mandelbaum: are damned because they sinned within the flesh,

blocks.text: che la ragion sommettono al talento.
blocks.mandelbaum: subjecting reason to the rule of lust.

blocks.text: E come li stornei ne portan l’ali
blocks.mandelbaum: And as, in the cold season, starlings’ wings

blocks.text: nel freddo tempo, a schiera larga e piena,
blocks.mandelbaum: bear them along in broad and crowded ranks

blocks.text: così quel fiato li spiriti mali
blocks.mandelbaum: so does that blast bear on the guilty spirits:

blocks.text: di qua, di là, di giù, di sù li mena;
blocks.mandelbaum: now here, now there, now down, now up, it drives them.

blocks.text: nulla speranza li conforta mai,
blocks.mandelbaum: There is no hope that ever comforts them—

blocks.text: non che di posa, ma di minor pena.
blocks.mandelbaum: no hope for rest and none for lesser pain.

blocks.text: E come i gru van cantando lor lai,
blocks.mandelbaum: And just as cranes in flight will chant their lays,

blocks.text: faccendo in aere di sé lunga riga,
blocks.mandelbaum: arraying their long file across the air,

blocks.text: così vid’ io venir, traendo guai,
blocks.mandelbaum: so did the shades I saw approaching, borne

blocks.text: ombre portate da la detta briga;
blocks.mandelbaum: by that assailing wind, lament and moan;

blocks.text: per ch’i’ dissi: «Maestro, chi son quelle
blocks.mandelbaum: so that I asked him: “Master, who are those

blocks.text: genti che l’aura nera sì gastiga?».
blocks.mandelbaum: who suffer punishment in this dark air?”

blocks.text: «La prima di color di cui novelle
blocks.mandelbaum: “The first of those about whose history

blocks.text: tu vuo’ saper», mi disse quelli allotta,
blocks.mandelbaum: you want to know,” my master then told me

blocks.text: «fu imperadrice di molte favelle.
blocks.mandelbaum: “once ruled as empress over many nations.

blocks.text: A vizio di lussuria fu sì rotta,
blocks.mandelbaum: Her vice of lust became so customary

blocks.text: che libito fé licito in sua legge,
blocks.mandelbaum: that she made license licit in her laws

blocks.text: per tòrre il biasmo in che era condotta.
blocks.mandelbaum: to free her from the scandal she had caused.

blocks.text: Ell’ è Semiramìs, di cui si legge
blocks.mandelbaum: She is Semiramis, of whom we read

blocks.text: che succedette a Nino e fu sua sposa:
blocks.mandelbaum: that she was Ninus’ wife and his successor:

blocks.text: tenne la terra che ’l Soldan corregge.
blocks.mandelbaum: she held the land the Sultan now commands.

blocks.text: L’altra è colei che s’ancise amorosa,
blocks.mandelbaum: That other spirit killed herself for love,

blocks.text: e ruppe fede al cener di Sicheo;
blocks.mandelbaum: and she betrayed the ashes of Sychaeus;

blocks.text: poi è Cleopatràs lussurïosa.
blocks.mandelbaum: the wanton Cleopatra follows next.

blocks.text: Elena vedi, per cui tanto reo
blocks.mandelbaum: See Helen, for whose sake so many years

blocks.text: tempo si volse, e vedi ’l grande Achille,
blocks.mandelbaum: of evil had to pass; see great Achilles,

blocks.text: che con amore al fine combatteo.
blocks.mandelbaum: who finally met love-in his last battle.

blocks.text: Vedi Parìs, Tristano»; e più di mille
blocks.mandelbaum: See Paris, Tristan . . .”—and he pointed out

blocks.text: ombre mostrommi e nominommi a dito,
blocks.mandelbaum: and named to me more than a thousand shades

blocks.text: ch’amor di nostra vita dipartille.
blocks.mandelbaum: departed from our life because of love.

blocks.text: Poscia ch’io ebbi ’l mio dottore udito
blocks.mandelbaum: No sooner had I heard my teacher name

blocks.text: nomar le donne antiche e ’ cavalieri,
blocks.mandelbaum: the ancient ladies and the knights, than pity

blocks.text: pietà mi giunse, e fui quasi smarrito.
blocks.mandelbaum: seized me, and I was like a man astray.

blocks.text: I’ cominciai: «Poeta, volontieri
blocks.mandelbaum: My first words: “Poet, I should willingly

blocks.text: parlerei a quei due che ’nsieme vanno,
blocks.mandelbaum: speak with those two who go together there

blocks.text: e paion sì al vento esser leggeri».
blocks.mandelbaum: and seem so lightly carried by the wind.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Vedrai quando saranno
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “You’ll see when they draw closer

blocks.text: più presso a noi; e tu allor li priega
blocks.mandelbaum: to us, and then you may appeal to them

blocks.text: per quello amor che i mena, ed ei verranno».
blocks.mandelbaum: by that love which impels them. They will come.”

blocks.text: Sì tosto come il vento a noi li piega,
blocks.mandelbaum: No sooner had the wind bent them toward us

blocks.text: mossi la voce: «O anime affannate,
blocks.mandelbaum: than I urged on my voice: “O battered souls

blocks.text: venite a noi parlar, s’altri nol niega!».
blocks.mandelbaum: if One does not forbid it, speak with us.”

blocks.text: Quali colombe dal disio chiamate
blocks.mandelbaum: Even as doves when summoned by desire,

blocks.text: con l’ali alzate e ferme al dolce nido
blocks.mandelbaum: borne forward by their will, move through the air

blocks.text: vegnon per l’aere, dal voler portate;
blocks.mandelbaum: with wings uplifted, still, to their sweet nest,

blocks.text: cotali uscir de la schiera ov’ è Dido,
blocks.mandelbaum: those spirits left the ranks where Dido suffers

blocks.text: a noi venendo per l’aere maligno,
blocks.mandelbaum: approaching us through the malignant air;

blocks.text: sì forte fu l’affettüoso grido.
blocks.mandelbaum: so powerful had been my loving cry.

blocks.text: «O animal grazïoso e benigno
blocks.mandelbaum: “O living being, gracious and benign,

blocks.text: che visitando vai per l’aere perso
blocks.mandelbaum: who through the darkened air have come to visit

blocks.text: noi che tignemmo il mondo di sanguigno,
blocks.mandelbaum: our souls that stained the world with blood, if He

blocks.text: se fosse amico il re de l’universo,
blocks.mandelbaum: who rules the universe were friend to us

blocks.text: noi pregheremmo lui de la tua pace,
blocks.mandelbaum: then we should pray to Him to give you peace

blocks.text: poi c’hai pietà del nostro mal perverso.
blocks.mandelbaum: for you have pitied our atrocious state.

blocks.text: Di quel che udire e che parlar vi piace,
blocks.mandelbaum: Whatever pleases you to hear and speak

blocks.text: noi udiremo e parleremo a voi,
blocks.mandelbaum: will please us, too, to hear and speak with you,

blocks.text: mentre che ’l vento, come fa, ci tace.
blocks.mandelbaum: now while the wind is silent, in this place.

blocks.text: Siede la terra dove nata fui
blocks.mandelbaum: The land where I was born lies on that shore

blocks.text: su la marina dove ’l Po discende
blocks.mandelbaum: to which the Po together with the waters

blocks.text: per aver pace co’ seguaci sui.
blocks.mandelbaum: that follow it descends to final rest.

blocks.text: Amor, ch’al cor gentil ratto s’apprende,
blocks.mandelbaum: Love, that can quickly seize the gentle heart,

blocks.text: prese costui de la bella persona
blocks.mandelbaum: took hold of him because of the fair body

blocks.text: che mi fu tolta; e ’l modo ancor m’offende.
blocks.mandelbaum: taken from me—how that was done still wounds me.

blocks.text: Amor, ch’a nullo amato amar perdona,
blocks.mandelbaum: Love, that releases no beloved from loving,

blocks.text: mi prese del costui piacer sì forte,
blocks.mandelbaum: took hold of me so strongly through his beauty

blocks.text: che, come vedi, ancor non m’abbandona.
blocks.mandelbaum: that, as you see, it has not left me yet.

blocks.text: Amor condusse noi ad una morte.
blocks.mandelbaum: Love led the two of us unto one death.

blocks.text: Caina attende chi a vita ci spense».
blocks.mandelbaum: Caina waits for him who took our life.”

blocks.text: Queste parole da lor ci fuor porte.
blocks.mandelbaum: These words were borne across from them to us.

blocks.text: Quand’ io intesi quell’ anime offense,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I had listened to those injured souls,

blocks.text: china’ il viso, e tanto il tenni basso,
blocks.mandelbaum: I bent my head and held it low until

blocks.text: fin che ’l poeta mi disse: «Che pense?».
blocks.mandelbaum: the poet asked of me: “What are you thinking?”

blocks.text: Quando rispuosi, cominciai: «Oh lasso,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I replied, my words began: “Alas,

blocks.text: quanti dolci pensier, quanto disio
blocks.mandelbaum: how many gentle thoughts, how deep a longing,

blocks.text: menò costoro al doloroso passo!».
blocks.mandelbaum: had led them to the agonizing pass!”

blocks.text: Poi mi rivolsi a loro e parla’ io,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then I addressed my speech again to them,

blocks.text: e cominciai: «Francesca, i tuoi martìri
blocks.mandelbaum: and I began: “Francesca, your afflictions

blocks.text: a lagrimar mi fanno tristo e pio.
blocks.mandelbaum: move me to tears of sorrow and of pity.

blocks.text: Ma dimmi: al tempo d’i dolci sospiri,
blocks.mandelbaum: But tell me, in the time of gentle sighs,

blocks.text: a che e come concedette amore
blocks.mandelbaum: with what and in what way did Love allow you

blocks.text: che conosceste i dubbiosi disiri?».
blocks.mandelbaum: to recognize your still uncertain longings?”

blocks.text: E quella a me: «Nessun maggior dolore
blocks.mandelbaum: And she to me: “There is no greater sorrow

blocks.text: che ricordarsi del tempo felice
blocks.mandelbaum: than thinking back upon a happy time

blocks.text: nella miseria; e ciò sa ’l tuo dottore.
blocks.mandelbaum: in misery—and this your teacher knows.

blocks.text: Ma s’a conoscer la prima radice
blocks.mandelbaum: Yet if you long so much to understand

blocks.text: del nostro amor tu hai cotanto affetto,
blocks.mandelbaum: the first root of our love, then I shall tell

blocks.text: dirò come colui che piange e dice.
blocks.mandelbaum: my tale to you as one who weeps and speaks.

blocks.text: Noi leggiavamo un giorno per diletto
blocks.mandelbaum: One day, to pass the time away, we read

blocks.text: di Lancialotto come amor lo strinse;
blocks.mandelbaum: of Lancelot—how love had overcome him.

blocks.text: soli eravamo e sanza alcun sospetto.
blocks.mandelbaum: We were alone, and we suspected nothing.

blocks.text: Per più fïate li occhi ci sospinse
blocks.mandelbaum: And time and time again that reading led

blocks.text: quella lettura, e scolorocci il viso;
blocks.mandelbaum: our eyes to meet, and made our faces pale,

blocks.text: ma solo un punto fu quel che ci vinse.
blocks.mandelbaum: and yet one point alone defeated us.

blocks.text: Quando leggemmo il disïato riso
blocks.mandelbaum: When we had read how the desired smile

blocks.text: esser basciato da cotanto amante,
blocks.mandelbaum: was kissed by one who was so true a lover,

blocks.text: questi, che mai da me non fia diviso,
blocks.mandelbaum: this one, who never shall be parted from me,

blocks.text: la bocca mi basciò tutto tremante.
blocks.mandelbaum: while all his body trembled, kissed my mouth.

blocks.text: Galeotto fu ’l libro e chi lo scrisse:
blocks.mandelbaum: A Gallehault indeed, that book and he

blocks.text: quel giorno più non vi leggemmo avante».
blocks.mandelbaum: who wrote it, too; that day we read no more.”

blocks.text: Mentre che l’uno spirto questo disse,
blocks.mandelbaum: And while one spirit said these words to me,

blocks.text: l’altro piangëa; sì che di pietade
blocks.mandelbaum: the other wept, so that—because of pity—

blocks.text: io venni men così com’ io morisse.
blocks.mandelbaum: I fainted, as if I had met my death.

blocks.text: E caddi come corpo morto cade.
blocks.mandelbaum: And then I fell as a dead body falls.

blocks.text: Al tornar de la mente, che si chiuse
blocks.mandelbaum: Upon my mind’s reviving—it had closed

blocks.text: dinanzi a la pietà d’i due cognati,
blocks.mandelbaum: on hearing the lament of those two kindred,

blocks.text: che di trestizia tutto mi confuse,
blocks.mandelbaum: since sorrow had confounded me completely—

blocks.text: novi tormenti e novi tormentati
blocks.mandelbaum: I see new sufferings, new sufferers

blocks.text: mi veggio intorno, come ch’io mi mova
blocks.mandelbaum: surrounding me on every side, wherever

blocks.text: e ch’io mi volga, e come che io guati.
blocks.mandelbaum: I move or turn about or set my eyes.

blocks.text: Io sono al terzo cerchio, de la piova
blocks.mandelbaum: I am in the third circle, filled with cold,

blocks.text: etterna, maladetta, fredda e greve;
blocks.mandelbaum: unending, heavy, and accursed rain;

blocks.text: regola e qualità mai non l’è nova.
blocks.mandelbaum: its measure and its kind are never changed.

blocks.text: Grandine grossa, acqua tinta e neve
blocks.mandelbaum: Gross hailstones, water gray with filth, and snow

blocks.text: per l’aere tenebroso si riversa;
blocks.mandelbaum: come streaking down across the shadowed air;
