blocks.text: pute la terra che questo riceve.
blocks.mandelbaum: the earth, as it receives that shower, stinks.

blocks.text: Cerbero, fiera crudele e diversa,
blocks.mandelbaum: Over the souls of those submerged beneath

blocks.text: con tre gole caninamente latra
blocks.mandelbaum: that mess, is an outlandish, vicious beast,

blocks.text: sovra la gente che quivi è sommersa.
blocks.mandelbaum: his three throats barking, doglike: Cerberus.

blocks.text: Li occhi ha vermigli, la barba unta e atra,
blocks.mandelbaum: His eyes are bloodred; greasy, black, his beard;

blocks.text: e ’l ventre largo, e unghiate le mani;
blocks.mandelbaum: his belly bulges, and his hands are claws;

blocks.text: graffia li spirti ed iscoia ed isquatra.
blocks.mandelbaum: his talons tear and flay and rend the shades.

blocks.text: Urlar li fa la pioggia come cani;
blocks.mandelbaum: That downpour makes the sinners howl like dogs;

blocks.text: de l’un de’ lati fanno a l’altro schermo;
blocks.mandelbaum: they use one of their sides to screen the other—

blocks.text: volgonsi spesso i miseri profani.
blocks.mandelbaum: those miserable wretches turn and turn.

blocks.text: Quando ci scorse Cerbero, il gran vermo,
blocks.mandelbaum: When Cerberus, the great worm, noticed us,

blocks.text: le bocche aperse e mostrocci le sanne;
blocks.mandelbaum: he opened wide his mouths, showed us his fangs;

blocks.text: non avea membro che tenesse fermo.
blocks.mandelbaum: there was no part of him that did not twitch.

blocks.text: E ’l duca mio distese le sue spanne,
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide opened his hands to their full span,

blocks.text: prese la terra, e con piene le pugna
blocks.mandelbaum: plucked up some earth, and with his fists filled full

blocks.text: la gittò dentro a le bramose canne.
blocks.mandelbaum: he hurled it straight into those famished jaws.

blocks.text: Qual è quel cane ch’abbaiando agogna,
blocks.mandelbaum: Just as a dog that barks with greedy hunger

blocks.text: e si racqueta poi che ’l pasto morde,
blocks.mandelbaum: will then fall quiet when he gnaws his food,

blocks.text: ché solo a divorarlo intende e pugna,
blocks.mandelbaum: intent and straining hard to cram it in,

blocks.text: cotai si fecer quelle facce lorde
blocks.mandelbaum: so were the filthy faces of the demon

blocks.text: de lo demonio Cerbero, che ’ntrona
blocks.mandelbaum: Cerberus transformed—after he’d stunned

blocks.text: l’anime sì, ch’esser vorrebber sorde.
blocks.mandelbaum: the spirits so, they wished that they were deaf.

blocks.text: Noi passavam su per l’ombre che adona
blocks.mandelbaum: We walked across the shades on whom there thuds

blocks.text: la greve pioggia, e ponavam le piante
blocks.mandelbaum: that heavy rain, and set our soles upon

blocks.text: sovra lor vanità che par persona.
blocks.mandelbaum: their empty images that seem like persons.

blocks.text: Elle giacean per terra tutte quante,
blocks.mandelbaum: And all those spirits lay upon the ground,

blocks.text: fuor d’una ch’a seder si levò, ratto
blocks.mandelbaum: except for one who sat erect as soon

blocks.text: ch’ella ci vide passarsi davante.
blocks.mandelbaum: as he caught sight of us in front of him.

blocks.text: «O tu che se’ per questo ’nferno tratto»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O you who are conducted through this Hell,”

blocks.text: mi disse, «riconoscimi, se sai:
blocks.mandelbaum: he said to me, “recall me, if you can;

blocks.text: tu fosti, prima ch’io disfatto, fatto».
blocks.mandelbaum: for you, before I was unmade, were made.”

blocks.text: E io a lui: «L’angoscia che tu hai
blocks.mandelbaum: And I to him: “It is perhaps your anguish

blocks.text: forse ti tira fuor de la mia mente,
blocks.mandelbaum: that snatches you out of my memory,

blocks.text: sì che non par ch’i’ ti vedessi mai.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that it seems that I have never seen you.

blocks.text: Ma dimmi chi tu se’ che ’n sì dolente
blocks.mandelbaum: But tell me who you are, you who are set

blocks.text: loco se’ messo, e hai sì fatta pena,
blocks.mandelbaum: in such a dismal place, such punishment—

blocks.text: che, s’altra è maggio, nulla è sì spiacente».
blocks.mandelbaum: if other pains are more, none’s more disgusting.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «La tua città, ch’è piena
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “Your city—one so full

blocks.text: d’invidia sì che già trabocca il sacco,
blocks.mandelbaum: of envy that its sack has always spilled—

blocks.text: seco mi tenne in la vita serena.
blocks.mandelbaum: that city held me in the sunlit life.

blocks.text: Voi cittadini mi chiamaste Ciacco:
blocks.mandelbaum: The name you citizens gave me was Ciacco;

blocks.text: per la dannosa colpa de la gola,
blocks.mandelbaum: and for the damning sin of gluttony,

blocks.text: come tu vedi, a la pioggia mi fiacco.
blocks.mandelbaum: as you can see, I languish in the rain.

blocks.text: E io anima trista non son sola,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, a wretched soul, am not alone,

blocks.text: ché tutte queste a simil pena stanno
blocks.mandelbaum: for all of these have this same penalty

blocks.text: per simil colpa». E più non fé parola.
blocks.mandelbaum: for this same sin.” And he said nothing more.

blocks.text: Io li rispuosi: «Ciacco, il tuo affanno
blocks.mandelbaum: I answered him: “Ciacco, your suffering

blocks.text: mi pesa sì, ch’a lagrimar mi ’nvita;
blocks.mandelbaum: so weights on me that I am forced to weep;

blocks.text: ma dimmi, se tu sai, a che verranno
blocks.mandelbaum: but tell me, if you know, what end awaits

blocks.text: li cittadin de la città partita;
blocks.mandelbaum: the citizens of that divided city;

blocks.text: s’alcun v’è giusto; e dimmi la cagione
blocks.mandelbaum: is any just man there? Tell me the reason

blocks.text: per che l’ha tanta discordia assalita».
blocks.mandelbaum: why it has been assailed by so much schism.”

blocks.text: E quelli a me: «Dopo lunga tencione
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “After long controversy,

blocks.text: verranno al sangue, e la parte selvaggia
blocks.mandelbaum: they’ll come to blood; the party of the woods

blocks.text: caccerà l’altra con molta offensione.
blocks.mandelbaum: will chase the other out with much offense.

blocks.text: Poi appresso convien che questa caggia
blocks.mandelbaum: But then, within three suns, they too must fall;

blocks.text: infra tre soli, e che l’altra sormonti
blocks.mandelbaum: at which the other party will prevail,

blocks.text: con la forza di tal che testé piaggia.
blocks.mandelbaum: using the power of one who tacks his sails.

blocks.text: Alte terrà lungo tempo le fronti,
blocks.mandelbaum: This party will hold high its head for long

blocks.text: tenendo l’altra sotto gravi pesi,
blocks.mandelbaum: and heap great weights upon its enemies,

blocks.text: come che di ciò pianga o che n’aonti.
blocks.mandelbaum: however much they weep indignantly.

blocks.text: Giusti son due, e non vi sono intesi;
blocks.mandelbaum: Two men are just, but no one listens to them.

blocks.text: superbia, invidia e avarizia sono
blocks.mandelbaum: Three sparks that set on fire every heart

blocks.text: le tre faville c’hanno i cuori accesi».
blocks.mandelbaum: are envy, pride, and avariciousness.”

blocks.text: Qui puose fine al lagrimabil suono.
blocks.mandelbaum: With this, his words, inciting tears, were done;

blocks.text: E io a lui: «Ancor vo’ che mi ’nsegni
blocks.mandelbaum: and I to him: “I would learn more from you;

blocks.text: e che di più parlar mi facci dono.
blocks.mandelbaum: I ask you for a gift of further speech:

blocks.text: Farinata e ’l Tegghiaio, che fuor sì degni,
blocks.mandelbaum: Tegghiaio, Farinata, men so worthy,

blocks.text: Iacopo Rusticucci, Arrigo e ’l Mosca
blocks.mandelbaum: Arrigo, Mosca, Jacopo Rusticucci,

blocks.text: e li altri ch’a ben far puoser li ’ngegni,
blocks.mandelbaum: and all the rest whose minds bent toward the good,

blocks.text: dimmi ove sono e fa ch’io li conosca;
blocks.mandelbaum: do tell me where they are and let me meet them;

blocks.text: ché gran disio mi stringe di savere
blocks.mandelbaum: for my great longing drives me on to learn

blocks.text: se ’l ciel li addolcia o lo ’nferno li attosca».
blocks.mandelbaum: if Heaven sweetens or Hell poisons them.”

blocks.text: E quelli: «Ei son tra l’anime più nere;
blocks.mandelbaum: And he: “They are among the blackest souls;

blocks.text: diverse colpe giù li grava al fondo:
blocks.mandelbaum: a different sin has dragged them to the bottom;

blocks.text: se tanto scendi, là i potrai vedere.
blocks.mandelbaum: if you descend so low, there you can see them.

blocks.text: Ma quando tu sarai nel dolce mondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: But when you have returned to the sweet world,

blocks.text: priegoti ch’a la mente altrui mi rechi:
blocks.mandelbaum: I pray, recall me to men’s memory:

blocks.text: più non ti dico e più non ti rispondo».
blocks.mandelbaum: I say no more to you, answer no more.”

blocks.text: Li diritti occhi torse allora in biechi;
blocks.mandelbaum: Then his straight gaze grew twisted and awry;

blocks.text: guardommi un poco e poi chinò la testa:
blocks.mandelbaum: he looked at me awhile, then bent his head;

blocks.text: cadde con essa a par de li altri ciechi.
blocks.mandelbaum: he fell as low as all his blind companions.

blocks.text: E ’l duca disse a me: «Più non si desta
blocks.mandelbaum: And my guide said to me: “He’ll rise no more

blocks.text: di qua dal suon de l’angelica tromba,
blocks.mandelbaum: until the blast of the angelic trumpet

blocks.text: quando verrà la nimica podesta:
blocks.mandelbaum: upon the coming of the hostile Judge:

blocks.text: ciascun rivederà la trista tomba,
blocks.mandelbaum: each one shall see his sorry tomb again

blocks.text: ripiglierà sua carne e sua figura,
blocks.mandelbaum: and once again take on his flesh and form,

blocks.text: udirà quel ch’in etterno rimbomba».
blocks.mandelbaum: and hear what shall resound eternally.”

blocks.text: Sì trapassammo per sozza mistura
blocks.mandelbaum: So did we pass across that squalid mixture

blocks.text: de l’ombre e de la pioggia, a passi lenti,
blocks.mandelbaum: of shadows and of rain, our steps slowed down,

blocks.text: toccando un poco la vita futura;
blocks.mandelbaum: talking awhile about the life to come.

blocks.text: per ch’io dissi: «Maestro, esti tormenti
blocks.mandelbaum: At which I said: “And after the great sentence-

blocks.text: crescerann’ ei dopo la gran sentenza,
blocks.mandelbaum: o master—will these torments grow, or else

blocks.text: o fier minori, o saran sì cocenti?».
blocks.mandelbaum: be less, or will they be just as intense?”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Ritorna a tua scïenza,
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “Remember now your science,

blocks.text: che vuol, quanto la cosa è più perfetta,
blocks.mandelbaum: which says that when a thing has more perfection,

blocks.text: più senta il bene, e così la doglienza.
blocks.mandelbaum: so much the greater is its pain or pleasure.

blocks.text: Tutto che questa gente maladetta
blocks.mandelbaum: Though these accursed sinners never shall

blocks.text: in vera perfezion già mai non vada,
blocks.mandelbaum: attain the true perfection, yet they can

blocks.text: di là più che di qua essere aspetta».
blocks.mandelbaum: expect to be more perfect then than now.”

blocks.text: Noi aggirammo a tondo quella strada,
blocks.mandelbaum: We took the circling way traced by that road;

blocks.text: parlando più assai ch’i’ non ridico;
blocks.mandelbaum: we said much more than I can here recount;

blocks.text: venimmo al punto dove si digrada:
blocks.mandelbaum: we reached the point that marks the downward slope.

blocks.text: quivi trovammo Pluto, il gran nemico.
blocks.mandelbaum: Here we found Plutus, the great enemy.

blocks.text: «Pape Satàn, pape Satàn aleppe!»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Pape Satan, Pape Satan aleppe!”

blocks.text: cominciò Pluto con la voce chioccia;
blocks.mandelbaum: so Plutus, with his grating voice, began.

blocks.text: e quel savio gentil, che tutto seppe,
blocks.mandelbaum: The gentle sage, aware of everything,

blocks.text: disse per confortarmi: «Non ti noccia
blocks.mandelbaum: said reassuringly, “Don’t let your fear

blocks.text: la tua paura; ché, poder ch’elli abbia,
blocks.mandelbaum: defeat you; for whatever power he has,

blocks.text: non ci torrà lo scender questa roccia».
blocks.mandelbaum: he cannot stop our climbing down this crag.”

blocks.text: Poi si rivolse a quella ’nfiata labbia,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then he turned back to Plutus’ swollen face

blocks.text: e disse: «Taci, maladetto lupo!
blocks.mandelbaum: and said to him: “Be quiet, cursed wolf!

blocks.text: consuma dentro te con la tua rabbia.
blocks.mandelbaum: Let your vindictiveness feed on yourself.

blocks.text: Non è sanza cagion l’andare al cupo:
blocks.mandelbaum: His is no random journey to the deep:

blocks.text: vuolsi ne l’alto, là dove Michele
blocks.mandelbaum: it has been willed on high, where Michael took

blocks.text: fé la vendetta del superbo strupo».
blocks.mandelbaum: revenge upon the arrogant rebellion.”

blocks.text: Quali dal vento le gonfiate vele
blocks.mandelbaum: As sails inflated by the wind collapse,

blocks.text: caggiono avvolte, poi che l’alber fiacca,
blocks.mandelbaum: entangled in a heap, when the mast cracks,

blocks.text: tal cadde a terra la fiera crudele.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that ferocious beast fell to the ground.

blocks.text: Così scendemmo ne la quarta lacca,
blocks.mandelbaum: Thus we made our way down to the fourth ditch,

blocks.text: pigliando più de la dolente ripa
blocks.mandelbaum: to take in more of that despondent shore

blocks.text: che ’l mal de l’universo tutto insacca.
blocks.mandelbaum: where all the universe’s ill is stored.

blocks.text: Ahi giustizia di Dio! tante chi stipa
blocks.mandelbaum: Justice of God! Who has amassed as many

blocks.text: nove travaglie e pene quant’ io viddi?
blocks.mandelbaum: strange tortures and travails as I have seen?

blocks.text: e perché nostra colpa sì ne scipa?
blocks.mandelbaum: Why do we let our guilt consume us so?

blocks.text: Come fa l’onda là sovra Cariddi,
blocks.mandelbaum: Even as waves that break above Charybdis,

blocks.text: che si frange con quella in cui s’intoppa,
blocks.mandelbaum: each shattering the other when they meet,

blocks.text: così convien che qui la gente riddi.
blocks.mandelbaum: so must the spirits here dance their round dance.

blocks.text: Qui vid’ i’ gente più ch’altrove troppa,
blocks.mandelbaum: Here, more than elsewhere, I saw multitudes

blocks.text: e d’una parte e d’altra, con grand’ urli,
blocks.mandelbaum: to every side of me; their howls were loud

blocks.text: voltando pesi per forza di poppa.
blocks.mandelbaum: while, wheeling weights, they used their chests to push.

blocks.text: Percotëansi ’ncontro; e poscia pur lì
blocks.mandelbaum: They struck against each other; at that point,

blocks.text: si rivolgea ciascun, voltando a retro,
blocks.mandelbaum: each turned around and, wheeling back those weights,

blocks.text: gridando: «Perché tieni?» e «Perché burli?».
blocks.mandelbaum: cried out: “Why do you hoard?” “Why do you squander?”

blocks.text: Così tornavan per lo cerchio tetro
blocks.mandelbaum: So did they move around the sorry circle

blocks.text: da ogne mano a l’opposito punto,
blocks.mandelbaum: from left and right to the opposing point;

blocks.text: gridandosi anche loro ontoso metro;
blocks.mandelbaum: again, again they cried their chant of scorn;

blocks.text: poi si volgea ciascun, quand’ era giunto,
blocks.mandelbaum: and so, when each of them had changed positions,

blocks.text: per lo suo mezzo cerchio a l’altra giostra.
blocks.mandelbaum: he circled halfway back to his next joust.

blocks.text: E io, ch’avea lo cor quasi compunto,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, who felt my heart almost pierced through,

blocks.text: dissi: «Maestro mio, or mi dimostra
blocks.mandelbaum: requested: “Master, show me now what shades

blocks.text: che gente è questa, e se tutti fuor cherci
blocks.mandelbaum: are these and tell me if they all were clerics—

blocks.text: questi chercuti a la sinistra nostra».
blocks.mandelbaum: those tonsured ones who circle on our left.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Tutti quanti fuor guerci
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “All these, to left and right

blocks.text: sì de la mente in la vita primaia,
blocks.mandelbaum: were so squint-eyed of mind in the first life—

blocks.text: che con misura nullo spendio ferci.
blocks.mandelbaum: no spending that they did was done with measure.

blocks.text: Assai la voce lor chiaro l’abbaia,
blocks.mandelbaum: Their voices bark this out with clarity

blocks.text: quando vegnono a’ due punti del cerchio
blocks.mandelbaum: when they have reached the two points of the circle

blocks.text: dove colpa contraria li dispaia.
blocks.mandelbaum: where their opposing guilts divide their ranks.

blocks.text: Questi fuor cherci, che non han coperchio
blocks.mandelbaum: These to the left—their heads bereft of hair—

blocks.text: veggendo il duca mio tornare in volta,
blocks.mandelbaum: when I saw that my guide was driven back,

blocks.text: più tosto dentro il suo novo ristrinse.
blocks.mandelbaum: made him more quickly mask his own new pallor.

blocks.text: Attento si fermò com’ uom ch’ascolta;
blocks.mandelbaum: He stood alert, like an attentive listener,

blocks.text: ché l’occhio nol potea menare a lunga
blocks.mandelbaum: because his eye could hardly journey far

blocks.text: per l’aere nero e per la nebbia folta.
blocks.mandelbaum: across the black air and the heavy fog.

blocks.text: «Pur a noi converrà vincer la punga»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “We have to win this battle,” he began,

blocks.text: cominciò el, «se non . . . Tal ne s’offerse.
blocks.mandelbaum: “if not. . . But one so great had offered help.

blocks.text: Oh quanto tarda a me ch’altri qui giunga!».
blocks.mandelbaum: How slow that someone’s coming to see me!”

blocks.text: I’ vidi ben sì com’ ei ricoperse
blocks.mandelbaum: But I saw well enough how he had covered

blocks.text: lo cominciar con l’altro che poi venne,
blocks.mandelbaum: his first words with the words that followed after—

blocks.text: che fur parole a le prime diverse;
blocks.mandelbaum: so different from what he had said before;

blocks.text: ma nondimen paura il suo dir dienne,
blocks.mandelbaum: nevertheless, his speech made me afraid,

blocks.text: perch’ io traeva la parola tronca
blocks.mandelbaum: because I drew out from his broken phrase

blocks.text: forse a peggior sentenzia che non tenne.
blocks.mandelbaum: a meaning worse—perhaps—than he’d intended.

blocks.text: «In questo fondo de la trista conca
blocks.mandelbaum: “Does anyone from the first circle, one

blocks.text: discende mai alcun del primo grado,
blocks.mandelbaum: whose only punishment is crippled hope,

blocks.text: che sol per pena ha la speranza cionca?».
blocks.mandelbaum: ever descend so deep in this sad hollow?”

blocks.text: Questa question fec’ io; e quei «Di rado
blocks.mandelbaum: That was my question. And he answered so:

blocks.text: incontra», mi rispuose, «che di noi
blocks.mandelbaum: “It is quite rare for one of us to go

blocks.text: faccia il cammino alcun per qual io vado.
blocks.mandelbaum: along the way that I have taken now.

blocks.text: Ver è ch’altra fïata qua giù fui,
blocks.mandelbaum: But I, in truth, have been here once before:

blocks.text: congiurato da quella Eritón cruda
blocks.mandelbaum: that savage witch Erichtho, she who called

blocks.text: che richiamava l’ombre a’ corpi sui.
blocks.mandelbaum: the shades back to their bodies, summoned me.

blocks.text: Di poco era di me la carne nuda,
blocks.mandelbaum: My flesh had not been long stripped off when she

blocks.text: ch’ella mi fece intrar dentr’ a quel muro,
blocks.mandelbaum: had me descend through all the rings of Hell,

blocks.text: per trarne un spirto del cerchio di Giuda.
blocks.mandelbaum: to draw a spirit back from Judas’ circle.

blocks.text: Quell’ è ’l più basso loco e ’l più oscuro,
blocks.mandelbaum: That is the deepest and the darkest place,

blocks.text: e ’l più lontan dal ciel che tutto gira:
blocks.mandelbaum: the farthest from the heaven that girds all:

blocks.text: ben so ’l cammin; però ti fa sicuro.
blocks.mandelbaum: so rest assured, I know the pathway well.

blocks.text: Questa palude che ’l gran puzzo spira
blocks.mandelbaum: This swamp that breeds and breathes the giant stench

blocks.text: cigne dintorno la città dolente,
blocks.mandelbaum: surrounds the city of the sorrowing,

blocks.text: u’ non potemo intrare omai sanz’ ira».
blocks.mandelbaum: which now we cannot enter without anger.”

blocks.text: E altro disse, ma non l’ho a mente;
blocks.mandelbaum: And he said more, but I cannot remember

blocks.text: però che l’occhio m’avea tutto tratto
blocks.mandelbaum: because my eyes had wholly taken me

blocks.text: ver’ l’alta torre a la cima rovente,
blocks.mandelbaum: to that high tower with the glowing summit

blocks.text: dove in un punto furon dritte ratto
blocks.mandelbaum: where, at one single point, there suddenly

blocks.text: tre furïe infernal di sangue tinte,
blocks.mandelbaum: stood three infernal Furies flecked with blood,

blocks.text: che membra feminine avieno e atto,
blocks.mandelbaum: who had the limbs of women and their ways

blocks.text: e con idre verdissime eran cinte;
blocks.mandelbaum: but wore, as girdles, snakes of deepest green;

blocks.text: serpentelli e ceraste avien per crine,
blocks.mandelbaum: small serpents and horned vipers formed their hairs,

blocks.text: onde le fiere tempie erano avvinte.
blocks.mandelbaum: and these were used to bind their bestial temples.

blocks.text: E quei, che ben conobbe le meschine
blocks.mandelbaum: And he, who knew these handmaids well—they served

blocks.text: de la regina de l’etterno pianto,
blocks.mandelbaum: the Queen of never-ending lamentation—

blocks.text: «Guarda», mi disse, «le feroci Erine.
blocks.mandelbaum: said: “Look at the ferocious Erinyes!

blocks.text: Quest’ è Megera dal sinistro canto;
blocks.mandelbaum: That is Megaera on the left, and she

blocks.text: quella che piange dal destro è Aletto;
blocks.mandelbaum: who weeps upon the right, that is Allecto;

blocks.text: Tesifón è nel mezzo»; e tacque a tanto.
blocks.mandelbaum: Tisiphone’s between them.” He was done.

blocks.text: Con l’unghie si fendea ciascuna il petto;
blocks.mandelbaum: Each Fury tore her breast with taloned nails;

blocks.text: battiensi a palme e gridavan sì alto,
blocks.mandelbaum: each, with her palms, beat on herself and wailed

blocks.text: ch’i’ mi strinsi al poeta per sospetto.
blocks.mandelbaum: so loud that I, in fear, drew near the poet.

blocks.text: «Vegna Medusa: sì ’l farem di smalto»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Just let Medusa come; then we shall turn

blocks.text: dicevan tutte riguardando in giuso;
blocks.mandelbaum: him into stone,” they all cried, looking down;

blocks.text: «mal non vengiammo in Tesëo l’assalto».
blocks.mandelbaum: “we should have punished Theseus’ assault.”

blocks.text: «Volgiti ’n dietro e tien lo viso chiuso;
blocks.mandelbaum: “Turn round and keep your eyes shut fast, for should

blocks.text: ché se ’l Gorgón si mostra e tu ’l vedessi,
blocks.mandelbaum: the Gorgon show herself and you behold her,

blocks.text: nulla sarebbe di tornar mai suso».
blocks.mandelbaum: never again would you return above,”

blocks.text: Così disse ’l maestro; ed elli stessi
blocks.mandelbaum: my master said; and he himself turned me

blocks.text: mi volse, e non si tenne a le mie mani,
blocks.mandelbaum: around and, not content with just my hands,

blocks.text: che con le sue ancor non mi chiudessi.
blocks.mandelbaum: used his as well to cover up my eyes.

blocks.text: O voi ch’avete li ’ntelletti sani,
blocks.mandelbaum: O you possessed of sturdy intellects,

blocks.text: mirate la dottrina che s’asconde
blocks.mandelbaum: observe the teaching that is hidden here

blocks.text: sotto ’l velame de li versi strani.
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath the veil of verses so obscure.

blocks.text: E già venìa su per le torbide onde
blocks.mandelbaum: And now, across the turbid waves, there passed

blocks.text: un fracasso d’un suon, pien di spavento,
blocks.mandelbaum: a reboantic fracas—horrid sound,

blocks.text: per cui tremavano amendue le sponde,
blocks.mandelbaum: enough to make both of the shorelines quake:

blocks.text: non altrimenti fatto che d’un vento
blocks.mandelbaum: a sound not other than a wind’s when, wild

blocks.text: impetüoso per li avversi ardori,
blocks.mandelbaum: because it must contend with warmer currents,

blocks.text: che fier la selva e sanz’ alcun rattento
blocks.mandelbaum: it strikes against the forest without let,

blocks.text: li rami schianta, abbatte e porta fori;
blocks.mandelbaum: shattering, beating down, bearing off branches,

blocks.text: dinanzi polveroso va superbo,
blocks.mandelbaum: as it moves proudly, clouds of dust before it,

blocks.text: e fa fuggir le fiere e li pastori.
blocks.mandelbaum: and puts to flight both animals and shepherds.

blocks.text: Li occhi mi sciolse e disse: «Or drizza il nerbo
blocks.mandelbaum: He freed my eyes and said: “Now let your optic

blocks.text: del viso su per quella schiuma antica
blocks.mandelbaum: nerve turn directly toward that ancient foam,

blocks.text: per indi ove quel fummo è più acerbo».
blocks.mandelbaum: there where the mist is thickest and most acrid.”

blocks.text: Come le rane innanzi a la nimica
blocks.mandelbaum: As frogs confronted by their enemy,

blocks.text: biscia per l’acqua si dileguan tutte,
blocks.mandelbaum: the snake, will scatter underwater till

blocks.text: fin ch’a la terra ciascuna s’abbica,
blocks.mandelbaum: each hunches in a heap along the bottom,

blocks.text: vid’ io più di mille anime distrutte
blocks.mandelbaum: so did the thousand ruined souls I saw

blocks.text: fuggir così dinanzi ad un ch’al passo
blocks.mandelbaum: take flight before a figure crossing Styx

blocks.text: passava Stige con le piante asciutte.
blocks.mandelbaum: who walked as if on land and with dry soles.

blocks.text: Dal volto rimovea quell’ aere grasso,
blocks.mandelbaum: He thrust away the thick air from his face,

blocks.text: menando la sinistra innanzi spesso;
blocks.mandelbaum: waving his left hand frequently before him;

blocks.text: e sol di quell’ angoscia parea lasso.
blocks.mandelbaum: that seemed the only task that wearied him.

blocks.text: Ben m’accorsi ch’elli era da ciel messo,
blocks.mandelbaum: I knew well he was Heaven’s messenger,

blocks.text: e volsimi al maestro; e quei fé segno
blocks.mandelbaum: and I turned toward my master; and he made

blocks.text: ch’i’ stessi queto ed inchinassi ad esso.
blocks.mandelbaum: a sign that I be still and bow before him.

blocks.text: Ahi quanto mi parea pien di disdegno!
blocks.mandelbaum: How full of high disdain he seemed to me!

blocks.text: Venne a la porta e con una verghetta
blocks.mandelbaum: He came up to the gate, and with a wand,

blocks.text: l’aperse, che non v’ebbe alcun ritegno.
blocks.mandelbaum: he opened it, for there was no resistance.

blocks.text: «O cacciati del ciel, gente dispetta»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O you cast out of Heaven, hated crowd,”

blocks.text: cominciò elli in su l’orribil soglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: were his first words upon that horrid threshold,

blocks.text: «ond’ esta oltracotanza in voi s’alletta?
blocks.mandelbaum: “why do you harbor this presumptuousness?

blocks.text: Perché recalcitrate a quella voglia
blocks.mandelbaum: Why are you so reluctant to endure

blocks.text: a cui non puote il fin mai esser mozzo,
blocks.mandelbaum: that Will whose aim can never be cut short,

blocks.text: e che più volte v’ha cresciuta doglia?
blocks.mandelbaum: and which so often added to your hurts?

blocks.text: Che giova ne le fata dar di cozzo?
blocks.mandelbaum: What good is it to thrust against the fates?

blocks.text: Cerbero vostro, se ben vi ricorda,
blocks.mandelbaum: Your Cerberus, if you remember well,

blocks.text: ne porta ancor pelato il mento e ’l gozzo».
blocks.mandelbaum: for that, had both his throat and chin stripped clean.”

blocks.text: Poi si rivolse per la strada lorda,
blocks.mandelbaum: At that he turned and took the filthy road,

blocks.text: e non fé motto a noi, ma fé sembiante
blocks.mandelbaum: and did not speak to us, but had the look

blocks.text: d’omo cui altra cura stringa e morda
blocks.mandelbaum: of one who is obsessed by other cares

blocks.text: che quella di colui che li è davante;
blocks.mandelbaum: than those that press and gnaw at those before him;

blocks.text: e noi movemmo i piedi inver’ la terra,
blocks.mandelbaum: and we moved forward, on into the city,

blocks.text: sicuri appresso le parole sante.
blocks.mandelbaum: in safety, having heard his holy words.

blocks.text: Dentro li ’ntrammo sanz’ alcuna guerra;
blocks.mandelbaum: We made our way inside without a struggle;

blocks.text: e io, ch’avea di riguardar disio
blocks.mandelbaum: and I, who wanted so much to observe

blocks.text: la condizion che tal fortezza serra,
blocks.mandelbaum: the state of things that such a fortress guarded,

blocks.text: com’ io fui dentro, l’occhio intorno invio:
blocks.mandelbaum: as soon as I had entered, looked about.

blocks.text: e veggio ad ogne man grande campagna,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw, on every side, a spreading plain

blocks.text: piena di duolo e di tormento rio.
blocks.mandelbaum: of lamentation and atrocious pain.

blocks.text: Sì come ad Arli, ove Rodano stagna,
blocks.mandelbaum: Just as at Arles, where Rhone becomes a marsh,

blocks.text: sì com’ a Pola, presso del Carnaro
blocks.mandelbaum: just as at Pola, near Quarnero’s gulf,

blocks.text: ch’Italia chiude e suoi termini bagna,
blocks.mandelbaum: that closes Italy and bathes its borders,

blocks.text: fanno i sepulcri tutt’ il loco varo,
blocks.mandelbaum: the sepulchers make all the plain uneven,

blocks.text: così facevan quivi d’ogne parte,
blocks.mandelbaum: so they did here on every side, except

blocks.text: salvo che ’l modo v’era più amaro;
blocks.mandelbaum: that here the sepulchers were much more harsh;

blocks.text: ché tra li avelli fiamme erano sparte,
blocks.mandelbaum: for flames were scattered through the tombs, and these

blocks.text: per le quali eran sì del tutto accesi,
blocks.mandelbaum: had kindled all of them to glowing heat;

blocks.text: che ferro più non chiede verun’ arte.
blocks.mandelbaum: no artisan could ask for hotter iron.

blocks.text: Tutti li lor coperchi eran sospesi,
blocks.mandelbaum: The lid of every tomb was lifted up,

blocks.text: e fuor n’uscivan sì duri lamenti,
blocks.mandelbaum: and from each tomb such sorry cries arose

blocks.text: che ben parean di miseri e d’offesi.
blocks.mandelbaum: as could come only from the sad and hurt.

blocks.text: E io: «Maestro, quai son quelle genti
blocks.mandelbaum: And I: “Master, who can these people be

blocks.text: che, seppellite dentro da quell’ arche,
blocks.mandelbaum: who, buried in great chests of stone like these,

blocks.text: si fan sentir coi sospiri dolenti?».
blocks.mandelbaum: must speak by way of sighs in agony?”

blocks.text: E quelli a me: «Qui son li eresïarche
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “Here are arch-heretics

blocks.text: con lor seguaci, d’ogne setta, e molto
blocks.mandelbaum: and those who followed them, from every sect;

blocks.text: più che non credi son le tombe carche.
blocks.mandelbaum: those tombs are much more crowded than you think.

blocks.text: Simile qui con simile è sepolto,
blocks.mandelbaum: Here, like has been ensepulchered with like;

blocks.text: e i monimenti son più e men caldi».
blocks.mandelbaum: some monuments are heated more, some less.”

blocks.text: E poi ch’a la man destra si fu vòlto,
blocks.mandelbaum: And then he turned around and to his right;

blocks.text: passammo tra i martìri e li alti spaldi.
blocks.mandelbaum: we passed between the torments and high walls.

blocks.text: Ora sen va per un secreto calle,
blocks.mandelbaum: Now, by a narrow path that ran between

blocks.text: tra ’l muro de la terra e li martìri,
blocks.mandelbaum: those torments and the ramparts of the city,

blocks.text: lo mio maestro, e io dopo le spalle.
blocks.mandelbaum: my master moves ahead, I following.

blocks.text: «O virtù somma, che per li empi giri
blocks.mandelbaum: “O highest virtue, you who lead me through

blocks.text: mi volvi», cominciai, «com’ a te piace,
blocks.mandelbaum: these circles of transgression, at your will,

blocks.text: parlami, e sodisfammi a’ miei disiri.
blocks.mandelbaum: do speak to me, and satisfy my longings.

blocks.text: La gente che per li sepolcri giace
blocks.mandelbaum: Can those who lie within the sepulchers

blocks.text: potrebbesi veder? già son levati
blocks.mandelbaum: be seen? The lids—in fact—have all been lifted;

blocks.text: tutt’ i coperchi, e nessun guardia face».
blocks.mandelbaum: no guardian is watching over them.”

blocks.text: E quelli a me: «Tutti saran serrati
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “They’ll all be shuttered up

blocks.text: quando di Iosafàt qui torneranno
blocks.mandelbaum: when they return here from Jehosaphat

blocks.text: coi corpi che là sù hanno lasciati.
blocks.mandelbaum: together with the flesh they left above.

blocks.text: Suo cimitero da questa parte hanno
blocks.mandelbaum: Within this region is the cemetery

blocks.text: con Epicuro tutti suoi seguaci,
blocks.mandelbaum: of Epicurus and his followers,

blocks.text: che l’anima col corpo morta fanno.
blocks.mandelbaum: all those who say the soul dies with the body.

blocks.text: Però a la dimanda che mi faci
blocks.mandelbaum: And so the question you have asked of me

blocks.text: quinc’ entro satisfatto sarà tosto,
blocks.mandelbaum: will soon find satisfaction while we’re here,

blocks.text: e al disio ancor che tu mi taci».
blocks.mandelbaum: as will the longing you have hid from me.”

blocks.text: E io: «Buon duca, non tegno riposto
blocks.mandelbaum: And I: “Good guide, the only reason I

blocks.text: a te mio cuor se non per dicer poco,
blocks.mandelbaum: have hid my heart was that I might speak briefly,

blocks.text: e tu m’hai non pur mo a ciò disposto».
blocks.mandelbaum: and you, long since, encouraged me in this.”

blocks.text: «O Tosco che per la città del foco
blocks.mandelbaum: “O Tuscan, you who pass alive across

blocks.text: vivo ten vai così parlando onesto,
blocks.mandelbaum: the fiery city with such seemly words,

blocks.text: piacciati di restare in questo loco.
blocks.mandelbaum: be kind enough to stay your journey here.

blocks.text: La tua loquela ti fa manifesto
blocks.mandelbaum: Your accent makes it clear that you belong

blocks.text: di quella nobil patrïa natio,
blocks.mandelbaum: among the natives of the noble city

blocks.text: a la qual forse fui troppo molesto».
blocks.mandelbaum: I may have dealt with too vindictively.”

blocks.text: Subitamente questo suono uscìo
blocks.mandelbaum: This sound had burst so unexpectedly

blocks.text: d’una de l’arche; però m’accostai,
blocks.mandelbaum: out of one sepulcher that, trembling, I

blocks.text: temendo, un poco più al duca mio.
blocks.mandelbaum: then drew a little closer to my guide.

blocks.text: Ed el mi disse: «Volgiti! Che fai?
blocks.mandelbaum: But he told me: “Turn round! What are you doing?

blocks.text: Vedi là Farinata che s’è dritto:
blocks.mandelbaum: That’s Farinata who has risen there—

blocks.text: da la cintola in sù tutto ’l vedrai».
blocks.mandelbaum: you will see all of him from the waist up.”

blocks.text: Io avea già il mio viso nel suo fitto;
blocks.mandelbaum: My eyes already were intent on his;

blocks.text: ed el s’ergea col petto e con la fronte
blocks.mandelbaum: and up he rose—his forehead and his chest-

blocks.text: com’ avesse l’inferno a gran dispitto.
blocks.mandelbaum: as if he had tremendous scorn for Hell.

blocks.text: E l’animose man del duca e pronte
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide—his hands encouraging and quick-

blocks.text: mi pinser tra le sepulture a lui,
blocks.mandelbaum: thrust me between the sepulchers toward him,

blocks.text: dicendo: «Le parole tue sien conte».
blocks.mandelbaum: saying: “Your words must be appropriate.”

blocks.text: Com’ io al piè de la sua tomba fui,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I’d drawn closer to his sepulcher,

blocks.text: guardommi un poco, e poi, quasi sdegnoso,
blocks.mandelbaum: he glanced at me, and as if in disdain,

blocks.text: mi dimandò: «Chi fuor li maggior tui?».
blocks.mandelbaum: he asked of me: “Who were your ancestors?”

blocks.text: Io ch’era d’ubidir disideroso,
blocks.mandelbaum: Because I wanted so to be compliant,

blocks.text: non gliel celai, ma tutto gliel’ apersi;
blocks.mandelbaum: I hid no thing from him: I told him all.

blocks.text: ond’ ei levò le ciglia un poco in suso;
blocks.mandelbaum: At this he lifted up his brows a bit,

blocks.text: poi disse: «Fieramente furo avversi
blocks.mandelbaum: then said: “They were ferocious enemies

blocks.text: a me e a miei primi e a mia parte,
blocks.mandelbaum: of mine and of my parents and my party,

blocks.text: sì che per due fïate li dispersi».
blocks.mandelbaum: so that I had to scatter them twice over.”

blocks.text: «S’ei fur cacciati, ei tornar d’ogne parte»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “If they were driven out,” I answered him,

blocks.text: rispuos’ io lui, «l’una e l’altra fïata;
blocks.mandelbaum: “they still returned, both times, from every quarter;

blocks.text: ma i vostri non appreser ben quell’ arte».
blocks.mandelbaum: but yours were never quick to learn that art.”

blocks.text: Allor surse a la vista scoperchiata
blocks.mandelbaum: At this there rose another shade alongside,

blocks.text: un’ombra, lungo questa, infino al mento:
blocks.mandelbaum: uncovered to my sight down to his chin;

blocks.text: credo che s’era in ginocchie levata.
blocks.mandelbaum: I think that he had risen on his knees.

blocks.text: Dintorno mi guardò, come talento
blocks.mandelbaum: He looked around me, just as if he longed

blocks.text: avesse di veder s’altri era meco;
blocks.mandelbaum: to see if I had come with someone else;

blocks.text: e poi che ’l sospecciar fu tutto spento,
blocks.mandelbaum: but then, his expectation spent, he said

blocks.text: piangendo disse: «Se per questo cieco
blocks.mandelbaum: in tears: “If it is your high intellect

blocks.text: carcere vai per altezza d’ingegno,
blocks.mandelbaum: that lets you journey here, through this blind prison,

blocks.text: mio figlio ov’ è? e perché non è teco?».
blocks.mandelbaum: where is my son? Why is he not with you?”

blocks.text: E io a lui: «Da me stesso non vegno:
blocks.mandelbaum: I answered: “My own powers have not brought me;

blocks.text: colui ch’attende là, per qui mi mena
blocks.mandelbaum: he who awaits me there, leads me through here

blocks.text: forse cui Guido vostro ebbe a disdegno».
blocks.mandelbaum: perhaps to one your Guido did disdain.”

blocks.text: Le sue parole e ’l modo de la pena
blocks.mandelbaum: His words, the nature of his punishment—

blocks.text: m’avean di costui già letto il nome;
blocks.mandelbaum: these had already let me read his name;

blocks.text: però fu la risposta così piena.
blocks.mandelbaum: therefore, my answer was so fully made.

blocks.text: Di sùbito drizzato gridò: «Come?
blocks.mandelbaum: Then suddenly erect, he cried: “What’s that:

blocks.text: dicesti “elli ebbe”? non viv’ elli ancora?
blocks.mandelbaum: He ‘did disdain’? He is not still alive?

blocks.text: non fiere li occhi suoi lo dolce lume?».
blocks.mandelbaum: The sweet light does not strike against his eyes?”

blocks.text: Quando s’accorse d’alcuna dimora
blocks.mandelbaum: And when he noticed how I hesitated

blocks.text: ch’io facëa dinanzi a la risposta,
blocks.mandelbaum: a moment in my answer, he fell back—

blocks.text: supin ricadde e più non parve fora.
blocks.mandelbaum: supine—and did not show himself again.

blocks.text: Ma quell’ altro magnanimo, a cui posta
blocks.mandelbaum: But that great—hearted one, the other shade

blocks.text: restato m’era, non mutò aspetto,
blocks.mandelbaum: at whose request I’d stayed, did not change aspect

blocks.text: né mosse collo, né piegò sua costa;
blocks.mandelbaum: or turn aside his head or lean or bend;

blocks.text: e sé continüando al primo detto,
blocks.mandelbaum: and taking up his words where—he’d left off,

blocks.text: «S’elli han quell’ arte», disse, «male appresa,
blocks.mandelbaum: “If they were slow,” he said, “to learn that art,

blocks.text: ciò mi tormenta più che questo letto.
blocks.mandelbaum: that is more torment to me than this bed.

blocks.text: Ma non cinquanta volte fia raccesa
blocks.mandelbaum: And yet the Lady who is ruler here

blocks.text: la faccia de la donna che qui regge,
blocks.mandelbaum: will not have her face kindled fifty times

blocks.text: che tu saprai quanto quell’ arte pesa.
blocks.mandelbaum: before you learn how heavy is that art.

blocks.text: E se tu mai nel dolce mondo regge,
blocks.mandelbaum: And so may you return to the sweet world,
