blocks.text: di sé e d’un cespuglio fece un groppo.
blocks.mandelbaum: he fell into one tangle with a bush.

blocks.text: Di rietro a loro era la selva piena
blocks.mandelbaum: Behind these two, black bitches filled the wood,

blocks.text: di nere cagne, bramose e correnti
blocks.mandelbaum: and they were just as eager and as swift

blocks.text: come veltri ch’uscisser di catena.
blocks.mandelbaum: as greyhounds that have been let off their leash.

blocks.text: In quel che s’appiattò miser li denti,
blocks.mandelbaum: They set their teeth in him where he had crouched;

blocks.text: e quel dilaceraro a brano a brano;
blocks.mandelbaum: and, piece by piece, those dogs dismembered him

blocks.text: poi sen portar quelle membra dolenti.
blocks.mandelbaum: and carried off his miserable limbs.

blocks.text: Presemi allor la mia scorta per mano,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then he who was my escort took my hand;

blocks.text: e menommi al cespuglio che piangea
blocks.mandelbaum: he led me to the lacerated thorn

blocks.text: per le rotture sanguinenti in vano.
blocks.mandelbaum: that wept in vain where it was bleeding, broken.

blocks.text: «O Iacopo», dicea, «da Santo Andrea,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O Jacopo,” it said, “da Santo Andrea,

blocks.text: che t’è giovato di me fare schermo?
blocks.mandelbaum: what have you gained by using me as screen?

blocks.text: che colpa ho io de la tua vita rea?».
blocks.mandelbaum: Am I to blame for your indecent life?”

blocks.text: Quando ’l maestro fu sovr’ esso fermo,
blocks.mandelbaum: When my good master stood beside that bush,

blocks.text: disse: «Chi fosti, che per tante punte
blocks.mandelbaum: he said: “Who were you, who through many wounds

blocks.text: soffi con sangue doloroso sermo?».
blocks.mandelbaum: must breathe with blood your melancholy words?”

blocks.text: Ed elli a noi: «O anime che giunte
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to us: “O spirits who have come

blocks.text: siete a veder lo strazio disonesto
blocks.mandelbaum: to witness the outrageous laceration

blocks.text: c’ha le mie fronde sì da me disgiunte,
blocks.mandelbaum: that leaves so many of my branches torn,

blocks.text: raccoglietele al piè del tristo cesto.
blocks.mandelbaum: collect them at the foot of this sad thorn.

blocks.text: I’ fui de la città che nel Batista
blocks.mandelbaum: My home was in the city whose first patron

blocks.text: mutò ’l primo padrone; ond’ ei per questo
blocks.mandelbaum: gave way to John the Baptist; for this reason,

blocks.text: sempre con l’arte sua la farà trista;
blocks.mandelbaum: he’ll always use his art to make it sorrow;

blocks.text: e se non fosse che ’n sul passo d’Arno
blocks.mandelbaum: and if, along the crossing of the Arno,

blocks.text: rimane ancor di lui alcuna vista,
blocks.mandelbaum: some effigy of Mars had not remained,

blocks.text: que’ cittadin che poi la rifondarno
blocks.mandelbaum: those citizens who afterward rebuilt

blocks.text: sovra ’l cener che d’Attila rimase,
blocks.mandelbaum: their city on the ashes that Attila

blocks.text: avrebber fatto lavorare indarno.
blocks.mandelbaum: had left to them, would have travailed in vain.

blocks.text: Io fei gibetto a me de le mie case».
blocks.mandelbaum: I made—of my own house—my gallows place.”

blocks.text: Poi che la carità del natio loco
blocks.mandelbaum: Love of our native city overcame me;

blocks.text: mi strinse, raunai le fronde sparte
blocks.mandelbaum: I gathered up the scattered boughs and gave

blocks.text: e rende’le a colui, ch’era già fioco.
blocks.mandelbaum: them back to him whose voice was spent already.

blocks.text: Indi venimmo al fine ove si parte
blocks.mandelbaum: From there we reached the boundary that divides

blocks.text: lo secondo giron dal terzo, e dove
blocks.mandelbaum: the second from the third ring—and the sight

blocks.text: si vede di giustizia orribil arte.
blocks.mandelbaum: of a dread work that justice had devised.

blocks.text: A ben manifestar le cose nove,
blocks.mandelbaum: To make these strange things clear, I must explain

blocks.text: dico che arrivammo ad una landa
blocks.mandelbaum: that we had come upon an open plain

blocks.text: che dal suo letto ogne pianta rimove.
blocks.mandelbaum: that banishes all green things from its bed.

blocks.text: La dolorosa selva l’è ghirlanda
blocks.mandelbaum: The wood of sorrow is a garland round it,

blocks.text: intorno, come ’l fosso tristo ad essa;
blocks.mandelbaum: just as that wood is ringed by a sad channel;

blocks.text: quivi fermammo i passi a randa a randa.
blocks.mandelbaum: here, at the very edge, we stayed our steps.

blocks.text: Lo spazzo era una rena arida e spessa,
blocks.mandelbaum: The ground was made of sand, dry and compact,

blocks.text: non d’altra foggia fatta che colei
blocks.mandelbaum: a sand not different in kind from that

blocks.text: che fu da’ piè di Caton già soppressa.
blocks.mandelbaum: on which the feet of Cato had once tramped.

blocks.text: O vendetta di Dio, quanto tu dei
blocks.mandelbaum: O vengeance of the Lord, how you should be

blocks.text: esser temuta da ciascun che legge
blocks.mandelbaum: dreaded by everyone who now can read

blocks.text: ciò che fu manifesto a li occhi mei!
blocks.mandelbaum: whatever was made manifest to me!

blocks.text: D’anime nude vidi molte gregge
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw so many flocks of naked souls,

blocks.text: che piangean tutte assai miseramente,
blocks.mandelbaum: all weeping miserably, and it seemed that

blocks.text: e parea posta lor diversa legge.
blocks.mandelbaum: they were ruled by different decrees.

blocks.text: Supin giacea in terra alcuna gente,
blocks.mandelbaum: Some lay upon the ground, flat on their backs;

blocks.text: alcuna si sedea tutta raccolta,
blocks.mandelbaum: some huddled in a crouch, and there they sat;

blocks.text: e altra andava continüamente.
blocks.mandelbaum: and others moved about incessantly.

blocks.text: Quella che giva ’ntorno era più molta,
blocks.mandelbaum: The largest group was those who walked about,

blocks.text: e quella men che giacëa al tormento,
blocks.mandelbaum: the smallest, those supine in punishment;

blocks.text: ma più al duolo avea la lingua sciolta.
blocks.mandelbaum: but these had looser tongues to tell their torment.

blocks.text: Sovra tutto ’l sabbion, d’un cader lento,
blocks.mandelbaum: Above that plain of sand, distended flakes

blocks.text: piovean di foco dilatate falde,
blocks.mandelbaum: of fire showered down; their fall was slow—

blocks.text: come di neve in alpe sanza vento.
blocks.mandelbaum: as snow descends on alps when no wind blows.

blocks.text: Quali Alessandro in quelle parti calde
blocks.mandelbaum: Just like the flames that Alexander saw

blocks.text: d’Indïa vide sopra ’l süo stuolo
blocks.mandelbaum: in India’s hot zones, when fires fell,

blocks.text: fiamme cadere infino a terra salde,
blocks.mandelbaum: intact and to the ground, on his battalions,

blocks.text: per ch’ei provide a scalpitar lo suolo
blocks.mandelbaum: for which—wisely—he had his soldiers tramp

blocks.text: con le sue schiere, acciò che lo vapore
blocks.mandelbaum: the soil to see that every fire was spent

blocks.text: mei si stingueva mentre ch’era solo:
blocks.mandelbaum: before new flames were added to the old;

blocks.text: tale scendeva l’etternale ardore;
blocks.mandelbaum: so did the never-ending heat descend;

blocks.text: onde la rena s’accendea, com’ esca
blocks.mandelbaum: with this, the sand was kindled just as tinder

blocks.text: sotto focile, a doppiar lo dolore.
blocks.mandelbaum: on meeting flint will flame—doubling the pain.

blocks.text: Sanza riposo mai era la tresca
blocks.mandelbaum: The dance of wretched hands was never done;

blocks.text: de le misere mani, or quindi or quinci
blocks.mandelbaum: now here, now there, they tried to beat aside

blocks.text: escotendo da sé l’arsura fresca.
blocks.mandelbaum: the fresh flames as they fell. And I began

blocks.text: I’ cominciai: «Maestro, tu che vinci
blocks.mandelbaum: to speak: “My master, you who can defeat

blocks.text: tutte le cose, fuor che ’ demon duri
blocks.mandelbaum: all things except for those tenacious demons

blocks.text: ch’a l’intrar de la porta incontra uscinci,
blocks.mandelbaum: who tried to block us at the entryway,

blocks.text: chi è quel grande che non par che curi
blocks.mandelbaum: who is that giant there, who does not seem

blocks.text: lo ’ncendio e giace dispettoso e torto,
blocks.mandelbaum: to heed the singeing—he who lies and scorns

blocks.text: sì che la pioggia non par che ’l marturi?».
blocks.mandelbaum: and scowls, he whom the rains can’t seem to soften?”

blocks.text: E quel medesmo, che si fu accorto
blocks.mandelbaum: And he himself, on noticing that I

blocks.text: ch’io domandava il mio duca di lui,
blocks.mandelbaum: was querying my guide about him, cried:

blocks.text: gridò: «Qual io fui vivo, tal son morto.
blocks.mandelbaum: “That which I was in life, I am in death.

blocks.text: Se Giove stanchi ’l suo fabbro da cui
blocks.mandelbaum: Though Jove wear out the smith from whom he took,

blocks.text: crucciato prese la folgore aguta
blocks.mandelbaum: in wrath, the keen-edged thunderbolt with which

blocks.text: onde l’ultimo dì percosso fui;
blocks.mandelbaum: on my last day I was to be transfixed;

blocks.text: o s’elli stanchi li altri a muta a muta
blocks.mandelbaum: or if he tire the others, one by one,

blocks.text: in Mongibello a la focina negra,
blocks.mandelbaum: in Mongibello, at the sooty forge,

blocks.text: chiamando “Buon Vulcano, aiuta, aiuta!”,
blocks.mandelbaum: while bellowing: ‘O help, good Vulcan, help!’—

blocks.text: sì com’ el fece a la pugna di Flegra,
blocks.mandelbaum: just as he did when there was war at Phlegra—

blocks.text: e me saetti con tutta sua forza:
blocks.mandelbaum: and casts his shafts at me with all his force,

blocks.text: non ne potrebbe aver vendetta allegra».
blocks.mandelbaum: not even then would he have happy vengeance.”

blocks.text: Allora il duca mio parlò di forza
blocks.mandelbaum: Then did my guide speak with such vehemence

blocks.text: tanto, ch’i’ non l’avea sì forte udito:
blocks.mandelbaum: as I had never heard him use before:

blocks.text: «O Capaneo, in ciò che non s’ammorza
blocks.mandelbaum: “O Capaneus, for your arrogance

blocks.text: la tua superbia, se’ tu più punito;
blocks.mandelbaum: that is not quenched, you’re punished all the more:

blocks.text: nullo martiro, fuor che la tua rabbia,
blocks.mandelbaum: no torture other than your own madness

blocks.text: sarebbe al tuo furor dolor compito».
blocks.mandelbaum: could offer pain enough to match your wrath.”

blocks.text: Poi si rivolse a me con miglior labbia,
blocks.mandelbaum: But then, with gentler face he turned to me

blocks.text: dicendo: «Quei fu l’un d’i sette regi
blocks.mandelbaum: and said: “That man was one of seven kings

blocks.text: ch’assiser Tebe; ed ebbe e par ch’elli abbia
blocks.mandelbaum: besieging Thebes; he held—and still, it seems,

blocks.text: Dio in disdegno, e poco par che ’l pregi;
blocks.mandelbaum: holds—God in great disdain, disprizing Him;

blocks.text: ma, com’ io dissi lui, li suoi dispetti
blocks.mandelbaum: but as I told him now, his maledictions

blocks.text: sono al suo petto assai debiti fregi.
blocks.mandelbaum: sit well as ornaments upon his chest.

blocks.text: Or mi vien dietro, e guarda che non metti,
blocks.mandelbaum: Now follow me and—take care—do not set

blocks.text: ancor, li piedi ne la rena arsiccia;
blocks.mandelbaum: your feet upon the sand that’s burning hot,

blocks.text: ma sempre al bosco tien li piedi stretti».
blocks.mandelbaum: but always keep them back, close to the forest.”

blocks.text: Tacendo divenimmo là ’ve spiccia
blocks.mandelbaum: In silence we had reached a place where flowed

blocks.text: fuor de la selva un picciol fiumicello,
blocks.mandelbaum: a slender watercourse out of the wood—

blocks.text: lo cui rossore ancor mi raccapriccia.
blocks.mandelbaum: a stream whose redness makes me shudder still.

blocks.text: Quale del Bulicame esce ruscello
blocks.mandelbaum: As from the Bulicame pours a brook

blocks.text: che parton poi tra lor le peccatrici,
blocks.mandelbaum: whose waters then are shared by prostitutes,

blocks.text: tal per la rena giù sen giva quello.
blocks.mandelbaum: so did this stream run down across the sand.

blocks.text: Lo fondo suo e ambo le pendici
blocks.mandelbaum: Its bed and both its banks were made of stone,

blocks.text: fatt’ era ’n pietra, e ’ margini dallato;
blocks.mandelbaum: together with the slopes along its shores,

blocks.text: per ch’io m’accorsi che ’l passo era lici.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that I saw our passageway lay there.

blocks.text: «Tra tutto l’altro ch’i’ t’ho dimostrato,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Among all other things that I have shown you

blocks.text: poscia che noi intrammo per la porta
blocks.mandelbaum: since we first made our way across the gate

blocks.text: lo cui sogliare a nessuno è negato,
blocks.mandelbaum: whose threshold is forbidden to no one,

blocks.text: cosa non fu da li tuoi occhi scorta
blocks.mandelbaum: no thing has yet been witnessed by your eyes

blocks.text: notabile com’ è ’l presente rio,
blocks.mandelbaum: as notable as this red rivulet,

blocks.text: che sovra sé tutte fiammelle ammorta».
blocks.mandelbaum: which quenches every flame that burns above it.”

blocks.text: Queste parole fuor del duca mio;
blocks.mandelbaum: These words were spoken by my guide; at this,

blocks.text: per ch’io ’l pregai che mi largisse ’l pasto
blocks.mandelbaum: I begged him to bestow the food for which

blocks.text: di cui largito m’avëa il disio.
blocks.mandelbaum: he had already given me the craving.

blocks.text: «In mezzo mar siede un paese guasto»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “A devastated land lies in midsea,

blocks.text: diss’ elli allora, «che s’appella Creta,
blocks.mandelbaum: a land that is called Crete,” he answered me.

blocks.text: sotto ’l cui rege fu già ’l mondo casto.
blocks.mandelbaum: “Under its king the world once lived chastely.

blocks.text: Una montagna v’è che già fu lieta
blocks.mandelbaum: Within that land there was a mountain blessed

blocks.text: d’acqua e di fronde, che si chiamò Ida;
blocks.mandelbaum: with leaves and waters, and they called it Ida;

blocks.text: or è diserta come cosa vieta.
blocks.mandelbaum: but it is withered now like some old thing.

blocks.text: Rëa la scelse già per cuna fida
blocks.mandelbaum: It once was chosen as a trusted cradle

blocks.text: del suo figliuolo, e per celarlo meglio,
blocks.mandelbaum: by Rhea for her son; to hide him better,

blocks.text: quando piangea, vi facea far le grida.
blocks.mandelbaum: when he cried out, she had her servants clamor.

blocks.text: Dentro dal monte sta dritto un gran veglio,
blocks.mandelbaum: Within the mountain is a huge Old Man,

blocks.text: che tien volte le spalle inver’ Dammiata
blocks.mandelbaum: who stands erect—his back turned toward Damietta—

blocks.text: e Roma guarda come süo speglio.
blocks.mandelbaum: and looks at Rome as if it were his mirror.

blocks.text: La sua testa è di fin oro formata,
blocks.mandelbaum: The Old Man’s head is fashioned of fine gold,

blocks.text: e puro argento son le braccia e ’l petto,
blocks.mandelbaum: the purest silver forms his arms and chest,

blocks.text: poi è di rame infino a la forcata;
blocks.mandelbaum: but he is made of brass down to the cleft;

blocks.text: da indi in giuso è tutto ferro eletto,
blocks.mandelbaum: below that point he is of choicest iron

blocks.text: salvo che ’l destro piede è terra cotta;
blocks.mandelbaum: except for his right foot, made of baked clay;

blocks.text: e sta ’n su quel, più che ’n su l’altro, eretto.
blocks.mandelbaum: and he rests more on this than on the left.

blocks.text: Ciascuna parte, fuor che l’oro, è rotta
blocks.mandelbaum: Each part of him, except the gold, is cracked;

blocks.text: d’una fessura che lagrime goccia,
blocks.mandelbaum: and down that fissure there are tears that drip;

blocks.text: le quali, accolte, fóran quella grotta.
blocks.mandelbaum: when gathered, they pierce through that cavern’s floor

blocks.text: Lor corso in questa valle si diroccia;
blocks.mandelbaum: and, crossing rocks into this valley, form

blocks.text: fanno Acheronte, Stige e Flegetonta;
blocks.mandelbaum: the Acheron and Styx and Phlegethon;

blocks.text: poi sen van giù per questa stretta doccia,
blocks.mandelbaum: and then they make their way down this tight channel,

blocks.text: infin, là ove più non si dismonta,
blocks.mandelbaum: and at the point past which there’s no descent,

blocks.text: fanno Cocito; e qual sia quello stagno
blocks.mandelbaum: they form Cocytus; since you are to see

blocks.text: tu lo vedrai, però qui non si conta».
blocks.mandelbaum: what that pool is, I’ll not describe it here.”

blocks.text: E io a lui: «Se ’l presente rigagno
blocks.mandelbaum: And I asked him: “But if the rivulet

blocks.text: si diriva così dal nostro mondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: must follow such a course down from our world,

blocks.text: perché ci appar pur a questo vivagno?».
blocks.mandelbaum: why can we see it only at this boundary?”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Tu sai che ’l loco è tondo;
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “You know this place is round;

blocks.text: e tutto che tu sie venuto molto,
blocks.mandelbaum: and though the way that you have come is long,

blocks.text: pur a sinistra, giù calando al fondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: and always toward the left and toward the bottom,

blocks.text: non se’ ancor per tutto ’l cerchio vòlto;
blocks.mandelbaum: you still have not completed all the circle:

blocks.text: per che, se cosa n’apparisce nova,
blocks.mandelbaum: so that, if something new appears to us,

blocks.text: non de’ addur maraviglia al tuo volto».
blocks.mandelbaum: it need not bring such wonder to your face.”

blocks.text: E io ancor: «Maestro, ove si trova
blocks.mandelbaum: And I again: “Master, where’s Phlegethon

blocks.text: Flegetonta e Letè? ché de l’un taci,
blocks.mandelbaum: and where is Lethe? You omit the second

blocks.text: e l’altro di’ che si fa d’esta piova».
blocks.mandelbaum: and say this rain of tears has formed the first.”

blocks.text: «In tutte tue question certo mi piaci»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “I’m pleased indeed,” he said, “with all your questions;

blocks.text: rispuose, «ma ’l bollor de l’acqua rossa
blocks.mandelbaum: yet one of them might well have found its answer

blocks.text: dovea ben solver l’una che tu faci.
blocks.mandelbaum: already—when you saw the red stream boiling.

blocks.text: Letè vedrai, ma fuor di questa fossa,
blocks.mandelbaum: You shall see Lethe, but past this abyss,

blocks.text: là dove vanno l’anime a lavarsi
blocks.mandelbaum: there where the spirits go to cleanse themselves

blocks.text: quando la colpa pentuta è rimossa».
blocks.mandelbaum: when their repented guilt is set aside.”

blocks.text: Poi disse: «Omai è tempo da scostarsi
blocks.mandelbaum: Then he declared: “The time has come to quit

blocks.text: dal bosco; fa che di retro a me vegne:
blocks.mandelbaum: this wood; see that you follow close behind me;

blocks.text: li margini fan via, che non son arsi,
blocks.mandelbaum: these margins form a path that does not scorch,

blocks.text: e sopra loro ogne vapor si spegne».
blocks.mandelbaum: and over them, all flaming vapor is quenched.”

blocks.text: Ora cen porta l’un de’ duri margini;
blocks.mandelbaum: Now one of the hard borders bears us forward;

blocks.text: e ’l fummo del ruscel di sopra aduggia,
blocks.mandelbaum: the river mist forms shadows overhead

blocks.text: sì che dal foco salva l’acqua e li argini.
blocks.mandelbaum: and shields the shores and water from the fire.

blocks.text: Quali Fiamminghi tra Guizzante e Bruggia,
blocks.mandelbaum: Just as between Wissant and Bruges, the Flemings,

blocks.text: temendo ’l fiotto che ’nver’ lor s’avventa,
blocks.mandelbaum: in terror of the tide that floods toward them,

blocks.text: fanno lo schermo perché ’l mar si fuggia;
blocks.mandelbaum: have built a wall of dykes to daunt the sea;

blocks.text: e quali Padoan lungo la Brenta,
blocks.mandelbaum: and as the Paduans, along the Brenta,

blocks.text: per difender lor ville e lor castelli,
blocks.mandelbaum: build bulwarks to defend their towns and castles

blocks.text: anzi che Carentana il caldo senta:
blocks.mandelbaum: before the dog days fall on Carentana;

blocks.text: a tale imagine eran fatti quelli,
blocks.mandelbaum: just so were these embankments, even though

blocks.text: tutto che né sì alti né sì grossi,
blocks.mandelbaum: they were not built so high and not so broad,

blocks.text: qual che si fosse, lo maestro félli.
blocks.mandelbaum: whoever was the artisan who made them.

blocks.text: Già eravam da la selva rimossi
blocks.mandelbaum: By now we were so distant from the wood

blocks.text: tanto, ch’i’ non avrei visto dov’ era,
blocks.mandelbaum: that I should not have made out where it was—

blocks.text: perch’ io in dietro rivolto mi fossi,
blocks.mandelbaum: not even if I’d turned around to look—

blocks.text: quando incontrammo d’anime una schiera
blocks.mandelbaum: when we came on a company of spirits

blocks.text: che venian lungo l’argine, e ciascuna
blocks.mandelbaum: who made their way along the bank; and each

blocks.text: ci riguardava come suol da sera
blocks.mandelbaum: stared steadily at us, as in the dusk,

blocks.text: guardare uno altro sotto nuova luna;
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath the new moon, men look at each other.

blocks.text: e sì ver’ noi aguzzavan le ciglia
blocks.mandelbaum: They knit their brows and squinted at us—just

blocks.text: come ’l vecchio sartor fa ne la cruna.
blocks.mandelbaum: as an old tailor at his needle’s eye.

blocks.text: Così adocchiato da cotal famiglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: And when that family looked harder, I

blocks.text: fui conosciuto da un, che mi prese
blocks.mandelbaum: was recognized by one, who took me by

blocks.text: per lo lembo e gridò: «Qual maraviglia!».
blocks.mandelbaum: the hem and cried out: “This is marvelous!”

blocks.text: E io, quando ’l suo braccio a me distese,
blocks.mandelbaum: That spirit having stretched his arm toward me,

blocks.text: ficcaï li occhi per lo cotto aspetto,
blocks.mandelbaum: I fixed my eyes upon his baked, brown features,

blocks.text: sì che ’l viso abbrusciato non difese
blocks.mandelbaum: so that the scorching of his face could not

blocks.text: la conoscenza süa al mio ’ntelletto;
blocks.mandelbaum: prevent my mind from recognizing him;

blocks.text: e chinando la mano a la sua faccia,
blocks.mandelbaum: and lowering my face to meet his face,

blocks.text: rispuosi: «Siete voi qui, ser Brunetto?».
blocks.mandelbaum: I answered him: “Are you here, Ser Brunetto?”

blocks.text: E quelli: «O figliuol mio, non ti dispiaccia
blocks.mandelbaum: And he: “My son, do not mind if Brunetto

blocks.text: se Brunetto Latino un poco teco
blocks.mandelbaum: Latino lingers for a while with you

blocks.text: ritorna ’n dietro e lascia andar la traccia».
blocks.mandelbaum: and lets the file he’s with pass on ahead.”

blocks.text: I’ dissi lui: «Quanto posso, ven preco;
blocks.mandelbaum: I said: “With all my strength I pray you, stay;

blocks.text: e se volete che con voi m’asseggia,
blocks.mandelbaum: and if you’d have me rest awhile with you,

blocks.text: faròl, se piace a costui che vo seco».
blocks.mandelbaum: I shall, if that please him with whom I go.”

blocks.text: «O figliuol», disse, «qual di questa greggia
blocks.mandelbaum: “O son,” he said, “whoever of this flock

blocks.text: s’arresta punto, giace poi cent’ anni
blocks.mandelbaum: stops but a moment, stays a hundred years

blocks.text: sanz’ arrostarsi quando ’l foco il feggia.
blocks.mandelbaum: and cannot shield himself when fire strikes.

blocks.text: Però va oltre: i’ ti verrò a’ panni;
blocks.mandelbaum: Therefore move on; below—but close—I’ll follow;

blocks.text: e poi rigiugnerò la mia masnada,
blocks.mandelbaum: and then I shall rejoin my company,

blocks.text: che va piangendo i suoi etterni danni».
blocks.mandelbaum: who go lamenting their eternal sorrows.”

blocks.text: Io non osava scender de la strada
blocks.mandelbaum: I did not dare to leave my path for his

blocks.text: per andar par di lui; ma ’l capo chino
blocks.mandelbaum: own level; but I walked with head bent low

blocks.text: tenea com’ uom che reverente vada.
blocks.mandelbaum: as does a man who goes in reverence.

blocks.text: El cominciò: «Qual fortuna o destino
blocks.mandelbaum: And he began: “What destiny or chance

blocks.text: anzi l’ultimo dì qua giù ti mena?
blocks.mandelbaum: has led you here below before your last

blocks.text: e chi è questi che mostra ’l cammino?».
blocks.mandelbaum: day came, and who is he who shows the way?”

blocks.text: «Là sù di sopra, in la vita serena»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “There, in the sunlit life above,” I answered,

blocks.text: rispuos’ io lui, «mi smarri’ in una valle,
blocks.mandelbaum: “before my years were full, I went astray

blocks.text: avanti che l’età mia fosse piena.
blocks.mandelbaum: within a valley. Only yesterday

blocks.text: Pur ier mattina le volsi le spalle:
blocks.mandelbaum: at dawn I turned my back upon it—but

blocks.text: questi m’apparve, tornand’ ïo in quella,
blocks.mandelbaum: when I was newly lost, he here appeared,

blocks.text: e reducemi a ca per questo calle».
blocks.mandelbaum: to guide me home again along this path.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Se tu segui tua stella,
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “If you pursue your star,

blocks.text: non puoi fallire a glorïoso porto,
blocks.mandelbaum: you cannot fail to reach a splendid harbor,

blocks.text: se ben m’accorsi ne la vita bella;
blocks.mandelbaum: if in fair life, I judged you properly;

blocks.text: e s’io non fossi sì per tempo morto,
blocks.mandelbaum: and if I had not died too soon for this,

blocks.text: veggendo il cielo a te così benigno,
blocks.mandelbaum: on seeing Heaven was so kind to you,

blocks.text: dato t’avrei a l’opera conforto.
blocks.mandelbaum: I should have helped sustain you in your work.

blocks.text: Ma quello ingrato popolo maligno
blocks.mandelbaum: But that malicious, that ungrateful people

blocks.text: che discese di Fiesole ab antico,
blocks.mandelbaum: come down, in ancient times, from Fiesole—

blocks.text: e tiene ancor del monte e del macigno,
blocks.mandelbaum: still keeping something of the rock and mountain—

blocks.text: ti si farà, per tuo ben far, nimico;
blocks.mandelbaum: for your good deeds, will be your enemy:

blocks.text: ed è ragion, ché tra li lazzi sorbi
blocks.mandelbaum: and there is cause—among the sour sorbs,

blocks.text: si disconvien fruttare al dolce fico.
blocks.mandelbaum: the sweet fig is not meant to bear its fruit.

blocks.text: Vecchia fama nel mondo li chiama orbi;
blocks.mandelbaum: The world has long since called them blind, a people

blocks.text: gent’ è avara, invidiosa e superba:
blocks.mandelbaum: presumptuous, avaricious, envious;

blocks.text: dai lor costumi fa che tu ti forbi.
blocks.mandelbaum: be sure to cleanse yourself of their foul ways.

blocks.text: La tua fortuna tanto onor ti serba,
blocks.mandelbaum: Your fortune holds in store such honor for you,

blocks.text: che l’una parte e l’altra avranno fame
blocks.mandelbaum: one party and the other will be hungry

blocks.text: di te; ma lungi fia dal becco l’erba.
blocks.mandelbaum: for you—but keep the grass far from the goat.

blocks.text: Faccian le bestie fiesolane strame
blocks.mandelbaum: For let the beasts of Fiesole find forage

blocks.text: di lor medesme, e non tocchin la pianta,
blocks.mandelbaum: among themselves, and leave the plant alone—

blocks.text: s’alcuna surge ancora in lor letame,
blocks.mandelbaum: if still, among their dung, it rises up—

blocks.text: in cui riviva la sementa santa
blocks.mandelbaum: in which there lives again the sacred seed

blocks.text: di que’ Roman che vi rimaser quando
blocks.mandelbaum: of those few Romans who remained in Florence

blocks.text: fu fatto il nido di malizia tanta».
blocks.mandelbaum: when such a nest of wickedness was built.”

blocks.text: «Se fosse tutto pieno il mio dimando»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “If my desire were answered totally,”

blocks.text: rispuos’ io lui, «voi non sareste ancora
blocks.mandelbaum: I said to Ser Brunetto, “you’d still be

blocks.text: de l’umana natura posto in bando;
blocks.mandelbaum: among, not banished from, humanity.

blocks.text: ché ’n la mente m’è fitta, e or m’accora,
blocks.mandelbaum: Within my memory is fixed—and now

blocks.text: la cara e buona imagine paterna
blocks.mandelbaum: moves me—your dear, your kind paternal image

blocks.text: di voi quando nel mondo ad ora ad ora
blocks.mandelbaum: when, in the world above, from time to time

blocks.text: m’insegnavate come l’uom s’etterna:
blocks.mandelbaum: you taught me how man makes himself eternal;

blocks.text: e quant’ io l’abbia in grado, mentr’ io vivo
blocks.mandelbaum: and while I live, my gratitude for that

blocks.text: convien che ne la mia lingua si scerna.
blocks.mandelbaum: must always be apparent in my words.

blocks.text: Ciò che narrate di mio corso scrivo,
blocks.mandelbaum: What you have told me of my course, I write;

blocks.text: e serbolo a chiosar con altro testo
blocks.mandelbaum: I keep it with another text, for comment

blocks.text: a donna che saprà, s’a lei arrivo.
blocks.mandelbaum: by one who’ll understand, if I may reach her.

blocks.text: Tanto vogl’ io che vi sia manifesto,
blocks.mandelbaum: One thing alone I’d have you plainly see:

blocks.text: pur che mia coscïenza non mi garra,
blocks.mandelbaum: so long as I am not rebuked by conscience,

blocks.text: ch’a la Fortuna, come vuol, son presto.
blocks.mandelbaum: I stand prepared for Fortune, come what may.

blocks.text: Non è nuova a li orecchi miei tal arra:
blocks.mandelbaum: My ears find no new pledge in that prediction;

blocks.text: però giri Fortuna la sua rota
blocks.mandelbaum: therefore, let Fortune turn her wheel as she

blocks.text: come le piace, e ’l villan la sua marra».
blocks.mandelbaum: may please, and let the peasant turn his mattock.”

blocks.text: Lo mio maestro allora in su la gota
blocks.mandelbaum: At this, my master turned his head around

blocks.text: destra si volse in dietro e riguardommi;
blocks.mandelbaum: and toward the right, and looked at me and said:

blocks.text: poi disse: «Bene ascolta chi la nota».
blocks.mandelbaum: “He who takes note of this has listened well.”

blocks.text: Né per tanto di men parlando vommi
blocks.mandelbaum: But nonetheless, my talk with Ser Brunetto

blocks.text: con ser Brunetto, e dimando chi sono
blocks.mandelbaum: continues, and I ask of him who are

blocks.text: li suoi compagni più noti e più sommi.
blocks.mandelbaum: his comrades of repute and excellence.

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Saper d’alcuno è buono;
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “To know of some is good;

blocks.text: de li altri fia laudabile tacerci,
blocks.mandelbaum: but for the rest, silence is to be praised;

blocks.text: ché ’l tempo saria corto a tanto suono.
blocks.mandelbaum: the time we have is short for so much talk.

blocks.text: In somma sappi che tutti fur cherci
blocks.mandelbaum: In brief, know that my company has clerics

blocks.text: e litterati grandi e di gran fama,
blocks.mandelbaum: and men of letters and of fame—and all

blocks.text: d’un peccato medesmo al mondo lerci.
blocks.mandelbaum: were stained by one same sin upon the earth.

blocks.text: Priscian sen va con quella turba grama,
blocks.mandelbaum: That sorry crowd holds Priscian and Francesco

blocks.text: e Francesco d’Accorso anche; e vedervi,
blocks.mandelbaum: d’Accorso; and among them you can see,

blocks.text: s’avessi avuto di tal tigna brama,
blocks.mandelbaum: if you have any longing for such scurf,

blocks.text: colui potei che dal servo de’ servi
blocks.mandelbaum: the one the Servant of His Servants sent

blocks.text: fu trasmutato d’Arno in Bacchiglione,
blocks.mandelbaum: from the Arno to the Bacchiglione’s banks,

blocks.text: dove lasciò li mal protesi nervi.
blocks.mandelbaum: and there he left his tendons strained by sin.

blocks.text: Di più direi; ma ’l venire e ’l sermone
blocks.mandelbaum: I would say more; but both my walk and words

blocks.text: più lungo esser non può, però ch’i’ veggio
blocks.mandelbaum: must not be longer, for—beyond—I see

blocks.text: là surger nuovo fummo del sabbione.
blocks.mandelbaum: new smoke emerging from the sandy bed.

blocks.text: Gente vien con la quale esser non deggio.
blocks.mandelbaum: Now people come with whom I must not be.

blocks.text: Sieti raccomandato il mio Tesoro,
blocks.mandelbaum: Let my Tesoro, in which I still live,

blocks.text: nel qual io vivo ancora, e più non cheggio».
blocks.mandelbaum: be precious to you; and I ask no more.”

blocks.text: Poi si rivolse, e parve di coloro
blocks.mandelbaum: And then he turned and seemed like one of those

blocks.text: che corrono a Verona il drappo verde
blocks.mandelbaum: who race across the fields to win the green

blocks.text: per la campagna; e parve di costoro
blocks.mandelbaum: cloth at Verona; of those runners, he

blocks.text: quelli che vince, non colui che perde.
blocks.mandelbaum: appeared to be the winner, not the loser.

blocks.text: Già era in loco onde s’udia ’l rimbombo
blocks.mandelbaum: No sooner had I reached the place where one

blocks.text: de l’acqua che cadea ne l’altro giro,
blocks.mandelbaum: could hear a murmur, like a beehive’s hum,

blocks.text: simile a quel che l’arnie fanno rombo,
blocks.mandelbaum: of waters as they fell to the next circle,

blocks.text: quando tre ombre insieme si partiro,
blocks.mandelbaum: when, setting out together, three shades ran,

blocks.text: correndo, d’una torma che passava
blocks.mandelbaum: leaving another company that passed

blocks.text: sotto la pioggia de l’aspro martiro.
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath the rain of bitter punishment.

blocks.text: Venian ver’ noi, e ciascuna gridava:
blocks.mandelbaum: They came toward us, and each of them cried out:

blocks.text: «Sòstati tu ch’a l’abito ne sembri
blocks.mandelbaum: “Stop, you who by your clothing seem to be

blocks.text: esser alcun di nostra terra prava».
blocks.mandelbaum: someone who comes from our indecent country!”

blocks.text: Ahimè, che piaghe vidi ne’ lor membri,
blocks.mandelbaum: Ah me, what wounds I saw upon their limbs,

blocks.text: ricenti e vecchie, da le fiamme incese!
blocks.mandelbaum: wounds new and old, wounds that the flames seared in!

blocks.text: Ancor men duol pur ch’i’ me ne rimembri.
blocks.mandelbaum: It pains me still as I remember it.

blocks.text: A le lor grida il mio dottor s’attese;
blocks.mandelbaum: When they cried out, my master paid attention;

blocks.text: volse ’l viso ver’ me, e «Or aspetta»,
blocks.mandelbaum: he turned his face toward me and then he said:

blocks.text: disse, «a costor si vuole esser cortese.
blocks.mandelbaum: “Now wait: to these one must show courtesy.

blocks.text: E se non fosse il foco che saetta
blocks.mandelbaum: And were it not the nature of this place

blocks.text: la natura del loco, i’ dicerei
blocks.mandelbaum: for shafts of fire to fall, I’d say that haste

blocks.text: che meglio stesse a te che a lor la fretta».
blocks.mandelbaum: was seemlier for you than for those three.”

blocks.text: Ricominciar, come noi restammo, ei
blocks.mandelbaum: As soon as we stood still, they started up

blocks.text: l’antico verso; e quando a noi fuor giunti,
blocks.mandelbaum: their ancient wail again; and when they reached us,

blocks.text: fenno una rota di sé tutti e trei.
blocks.mandelbaum: they formed a wheel, all three of them together.

blocks.text: Qual sogliono i campion far nudi e unti,
blocks.mandelbaum: As champions, naked, oiled, will always do,

blocks.text: avvisando lor presa e lor vantaggio,
blocks.mandelbaum: each studying the grip that serves him best

blocks.text: prima che sien tra lor battuti e punti,
blocks.mandelbaum: before the blows and wounds begin to fall,

blocks.text: così rotando, ciascuno il visaggio
blocks.mandelbaum: while wheeling so, each one made sure his face

blocks.text: drizzava a me, sì che ’n contraro il collo
blocks.mandelbaum: was turned to me, so that their necks opposed

blocks.text: faceva ai piè continüo vïaggio.
blocks.mandelbaum: their feet in one uninterrupted flow.

blocks.text: E «Se miseria d’esto loco sollo
blocks.mandelbaum: And, “If the squalor of this shifting sand,

blocks.text: rende in dispetto noi e nostri prieghi»,
blocks.mandelbaum: together with our baked and barren features,

blocks.text: cominciò l’uno, «e ’l tinto aspetto e brollo,
blocks.mandelbaum: makes us and our requests contemptible,”

blocks.text: la fama nostra il tuo animo pieghi
blocks.mandelbaum: one said, “then may our fame incline your mind

blocks.text: a dirne chi tu se’, che i vivi piedi
blocks.mandelbaum: to tell us who you are, whose living feet

blocks.text: così sicuro per lo ’nferno freghi.
blocks.mandelbaum: can make their way through Hell with such assurance.

blocks.text: Questi, l’orme di cui pestar mi vedi,
blocks.mandelbaum: He in whose steps you see me tread, although

blocks.text: tutto che nudo e dipelato vada,
blocks.mandelbaum: he now must wheel about both peeled and naked,

blocks.text: fu di grado maggior che tu non credi:
blocks.mandelbaum: was higher in degree than you believe:

blocks.text: nepote fu de la buona Gualdrada;
blocks.mandelbaum: he was a grandson of the good Gualdrada,

blocks.text: Guido Guerra ebbe nome, e in sua vita
blocks.mandelbaum: and Guido Guerra was his name; in life

blocks.text: fece col senno assai e con la spada.
blocks.mandelbaum: his sword and his good sense accomplished much.

blocks.text: L’altro, ch’appresso me la rena trita,
blocks.mandelbaum: The other who, behind me, tramples sand—

blocks.text: è Tegghiaio Aldobrandi, la cui voce
blocks.mandelbaum: Tegghiaio Aldobrandi, one whose voice

blocks.text: nel mondo sù dovria esser gradita.
blocks.mandelbaum: should have been heeded in the world above.

blocks.text: E io, che posto son con loro in croce,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, who share this punishment with them,

blocks.text: Iacopo Rusticucci fui, e certo
blocks.mandelbaum: was Jacopo Rusticucci; certainly,

blocks.text: la fiera moglie più ch’altro mi nuoce».
blocks.mandelbaum: more than all else, my savage wife destroyed me.”

blocks.text: S’i’ fossi stato dal foco coperto,
blocks.mandelbaum: If I’d had shield and shelter from the fire,

blocks.text: gittato mi sarei tra lor di sotto,
blocks.mandelbaum: I should have thrown myself down there among them—

blocks.text: e credo che ’l dottor l’avria sofferto;
blocks.mandelbaum: I think my master would have sanctioned that;

blocks.text: ma perch’ io mi sarei brusciato e cotto,
blocks.mandelbaum: but since that would have left me burned and baked,

blocks.text: vinse paura la mia buona voglia
blocks.mandelbaum: my fear won out against the good intention

blocks.text: che di loro abbracciar mi facea ghiotto.
blocks.mandelbaum: that made me so impatient to embrace them.

blocks.text: Poi cominciai: «Non dispetto, ma doglia
blocks.mandelbaum: Then I began: “Your present state had fixed

blocks.text: la vostra condizion dentro mi fisse,
blocks.mandelbaum: not scorn but sorrow in me—and so deeply

blocks.text: tanta che tardi tutta si dispoglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: that it will only disappear slowly—

blocks.text: tosto che questo mio segnor mi disse
blocks.mandelbaum: as soon as my lord spoke to me with words

blocks.text: parole per le quali i’ mi pensai
blocks.mandelbaum: that made me understand what kind of men

blocks.text: che qual voi siete, tal gente venisse.
blocks.mandelbaum: were coming toward us, men of worth like yours.

blocks.text: Di vostra terra sono, e sempre mai
blocks.mandelbaum: For I am of your city; and with fondness,

blocks.text: l’ovra di voi e li onorati nomi
blocks.mandelbaum: I’ve always told and heard the others tell

blocks.text: con affezion ritrassi e ascoltai.
blocks.mandelbaum: of both your actions and your honored names.

blocks.text: Lascio lo fele e vo per dolci pomi
blocks.mandelbaum: I leave the gall and go for the sweet apples

blocks.text: promessi a me per lo verace duca;
blocks.mandelbaum: that I was promised by my truthful guide;

blocks.text: ma ’nfino al centro pria convien ch’i’ tomi».
blocks.mandelbaum: but first I must descend into the center.”

blocks.text: «Se lungamente l’anima conduca
blocks.mandelbaum: “So may your soul long lead your limbs and may

blocks.text: le membra tue», rispuose quelli ancora,
blocks.mandelbaum: your fame shine after you,” he answered then,

blocks.text: «e se la fama tua dopo te luca,
blocks.mandelbaum: “tell us if courtesy and valor still

blocks.text: cortesia e valor dì se dimora
blocks.mandelbaum: abide within our city as they did

blocks.text: ne la nostra città sì come suole,
blocks.mandelbaum: when we were there, or have they disappeared

blocks.text: o se del tutto se n’è gita fora;
blocks.mandelbaum: completely; for Guiglielmo Borsiere,

blocks.text: ché Guiglielmo Borsiere, il qual si duole
blocks.mandelbaum: who only recently has come to share

blocks.text: con noi per poco e va là coi compagni,
blocks.mandelbaum: our torments, and goes there with our companions,

blocks.text: assai ne cruccia con le sue parole».
blocks.mandelbaum: has caused us much affliction with his words.”

blocks.text: «La gente nuova e i sùbiti guadagni
blocks.mandelbaum: “Newcomers to the city and quick gains

blocks.text: orgoglio e dismisura han generata,
blocks.mandelbaum: have brought excess and arrogance to you,

blocks.text: Fiorenza, in te, sì che tu già ten piagni».
blocks.mandelbaum: o Florence, and you weep for it already!”

blocks.text: Così gridai con la faccia levata;
blocks.mandelbaum: So I cried out with face upraised; the three

blocks.text: e i tre, che ciò inteser per risposta,
blocks.mandelbaum: looked at each other when they heard my answer

blocks.text: guardar l’un l’altro com’ al ver si guata.
blocks.mandelbaum: as men will stare when they have heard the truth.

blocks.text: «Se l’altre volte sì poco ti costa»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “If you can always offer a reply

blocks.text: rispuoser tutti, «il satisfare altrui,
blocks.mandelbaum: so readily to others,” said all three,

blocks.text: felice te se sì parli a tua posta!
blocks.mandelbaum: “then happy you who speak, at will, so clearly.

blocks.text: Però, se campi d’esti luoghi bui
blocks.mandelbaum: So, if you can escape these lands of darkness

blocks.text: e torni a riveder le belle stelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: and see the lovely stars on your return,

blocks.text: quando ti gioverà dicere “I’ fui”,
blocks.mandelbaum: when you repeat with pleasure, ‘I was there,’

blocks.text: fa che di noi a la gente favelle».
blocks.mandelbaum: be sure that you remember us to men.”

blocks.text: Indi rupper la rota, e a fuggirsi
blocks.mandelbaum: At this they broke their wheel; and as they fled,

blocks.text: ali sembiar le gambe loro isnelle.
blocks.mandelbaum: their swift legs seemed to be no less than wings.

blocks.text: Un amen non saria possuto dirsi
blocks.mandelbaum: The time it took for them to disappear—

blocks.text: tosto così com’ e’ fuoro spariti;
blocks.mandelbaum: more brief than time it takes to say “amen”;

blocks.text: per ch’al maestro parve di partirsi.
blocks.mandelbaum: and so, my master thought it right to leave.

blocks.text: Io lo seguiva, e poco eravam iti,
blocks.mandelbaum: I followed him. We’d only walked a little

blocks.text: che ’l suon de l’acqua n’era sì vicino,
blocks.mandelbaum: when roaring water grew so near to us

blocks.text: che per parlar saremmo a pena uditi.
blocks.mandelbaum: we hardly could have heard each other speak.

blocks.text: Come quel fiume c’ha proprio cammino
blocks.mandelbaum: And even as the river that is first

blocks.text: prima dal Monte Viso ’nver’ levante,
blocks.mandelbaum: to take its own course eastward from Mount Viso,

blocks.text: da la sinistra costa d’Apennino,
blocks.mandelbaum: along the left flank of the Apennines

blocks.text: che si chiama Acquacheta suso, avante
blocks.mandelbaum: (which up above is called the Acquacheta,

blocks.text: che si divalli giù nel basso letto,
blocks.mandelbaum: before it spills into its valley bed

blocks.text: e a Forlì di quel nome è vacante,
blocks.mandelbaum: and flows without that name beyond Forli),

blocks.text: rimbomba là sovra San Benedetto
blocks.mandelbaum: reverberates above San Benedetto

blocks.text: de l’Alpe per cadere ad una scesa
blocks.mandelbaum: dell’Alpe as it cascades in one leap,

blocks.text: ove dovea per mille esser recetto;
blocks.mandelbaum: where there is space enough to house a thousand;

blocks.text: così, giù d’una ripa discoscesa,
blocks.mandelbaum: so did we hear that blackened water roar

blocks.text: trovammo risonar quell’ acqua tinta,
blocks.mandelbaum: as it plunged down a steep and craggy bank,

blocks.text: sì che ’n poc’ ora avria l’orecchia offesa.
blocks.mandelbaum: enough to deafen us in a few hours.

blocks.text: Io avea una corda intorno cinta,
blocks.mandelbaum: Around my waist I had a cord as girdle,

blocks.text: e con essa pensai alcuna volta
blocks.mandelbaum: and with it once I thought I should be able

blocks.text: prender la lonza a la pelle dipinta.
blocks.mandelbaum: to catch the leopard with the painted hide.

blocks.text: Poscia ch’io l’ebbi tutta da me sciolta,
blocks.mandelbaum: And after I had loosened it completely,

blocks.text: sì come ’l duca m’avea comandato,
blocks.mandelbaum: just as my guide commanded me to do,

blocks.text: porsila a lui aggroppata e ravvolta.
blocks.mandelbaum: I handed it to him, knotted and coiled.

blocks.text: Ond’ ei si volse inver’ lo destro lato,
blocks.mandelbaum: At this, he wheeled around upon his right

blocks.text: e alquanto di lunge da la sponda
blocks.mandelbaum: and cast it, at some distance from the edge,

blocks.text: la gittò giuso in quell’ alto burrato.
blocks.mandelbaum: straight down into the depth of the ravine.

blocks.text: ‘E’ pur convien che novità risponda’,
blocks.mandelbaum: “And surely something strange must here reply,”

blocks.text: dicea fra me medesmo, ‘al novo cenno
blocks.mandelbaum: I said within myself, “to this strange sign—

blocks.text: che ’l maestro con l’occhio sì seconda’.
blocks.mandelbaum: the sign my master follows with his eye.”

blocks.text: Ahi quanto cauti li uomini esser dienno
blocks.mandelbaum: Ah, how much care men ought to exercise

blocks.text: presso a color che non veggion pur l’ovra,
blocks.mandelbaum: with those whose penetrating intellect

blocks.text: ma per entro i pensier miran col senno!
blocks.mandelbaum: can see our thoughts—not just our outer act!

blocks.text: El disse a me: «Tosto verrà di sovra
blocks.mandelbaum: He said to me: “Now there will soon emerge

blocks.text: ciò ch’io attendo e che il tuo pensier sogna;
blocks.mandelbaum: what I await and what your thought has conjured:

blocks.text: tosto convien ch’al tuo viso si scovra».
blocks.mandelbaum: it soon must be discovered to your sight.”

blocks.text: Sempre a quel ver c’ha faccia di menzogna
blocks.mandelbaum: Faced with that truth which seems a lie, a man

blocks.text: de’ l’uom chiuder le labbra fin ch’el puote,
blocks.mandelbaum: should always close his lips as long as he can—

blocks.text: però che sanza colpa fa vergogna;
blocks.mandelbaum: to tell it shames him, even though he’s blameless;

blocks.text: ma qui tacer nol posso; e per le note
blocks.mandelbaum: but here I can’t be still; and by the lines

blocks.text: di questa comedìa, lettor, ti giuro,
blocks.mandelbaum: of this my Comedy, reader, I swear—

blocks.text: s’elle non sien di lunga grazia vòte,
blocks.mandelbaum: and may my verse find favor for long years—

blocks.text: ch’i’ vidi per quell’ aere grosso e scuro
blocks.mandelbaum: that through the dense and darkened air I saw

blocks.text: venir notando una figura in suso,
blocks.mandelbaum: a figure swimming, rising up, enough

blocks.text: maravigliosa ad ogne cor sicuro,
blocks.mandelbaum: to bring amazement to the firmest heart,

blocks.text: sì come torna colui che va giuso
blocks.mandelbaum: like one returning from the waves where he

blocks.text: talora a solver l’àncora ch’aggrappa
blocks.mandelbaum: went down to loose an anchor snagged upon

blocks.text: o scoglio o altro che nel mare è chiuso,
blocks.mandelbaum: a reef or something else hid in the sea,

blocks.text: che ’n sù si stende e da piè si rattrappa.
blocks.mandelbaum: who stretches upward and draws in his feet.

blocks.text: «Ecco la fiera con la coda aguzza,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Behold the beast who bears the pointed tail,

blocks.text: che passa i monti e rompe i muri e l’armi!
blocks.mandelbaum: who crosses mountains, shatters weapons, walls!

blocks.text: Ecco colei che tutto ’l mondo appuzza!».
blocks.mandelbaum: Behold the one whose stench fills all the world!”

blocks.text: Sì cominciò lo mio duca a parlarmi;
blocks.mandelbaum: So did my guide begin to speak to me,

blocks.text: e accennolle che venisse a proda,
blocks.mandelbaum: and then he signaled him to come ashore

blocks.text: vicino al fin d’i passeggiati marmi.
blocks.mandelbaum: close to the end of those stone passageways.

blocks.text: E quella sozza imagine di froda
blocks.mandelbaum: And he came on, that filthy effigy

blocks.text: sen venne, e arrivò la testa e ’l busto,
blocks.mandelbaum: of fraud, and landed with his head and torso

blocks.text: ma ’n su la riva non trasse la coda.
blocks.mandelbaum: but did not draw his tail onto the bank.

blocks.text: La faccia sua era faccia d’uom giusto,
blocks.mandelbaum: The face he wore was that of a just man,

blocks.text: tanto benigna avea di fuor la pelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: so gracious was his features’ outer semblance;

blocks.text: e d’un serpente tutto l’altro fusto;
blocks.mandelbaum: and all his trunk, the body of a serpent;

blocks.text: due branche avea pilose insin l’ascelle;
blocks.mandelbaum: he had two paws, with hair up to the armpits;

blocks.text: lo dosso e ’l petto e ambedue le coste
blocks.mandelbaum: his back and chest as well as both his flanks

blocks.text: dipinti avea di nodi e di rotelle.
blocks.mandelbaum: had been adorned with twining knots and circlets.

blocks.text: Con più color, sommesse e sovraposte
blocks.mandelbaum: No Turks or Tartars ever fashioned fabrics

blocks.text: non fer mai drappi Tartari né Turchi,
blocks.mandelbaum: more colorful in background and relief,

blocks.text: né fuor tai tele per Aragne imposte.
blocks.mandelbaum: nor had Arachne ever loomed such webs.

blocks.text: Come talvolta stanno a riva i burchi,
blocks.mandelbaum: As boats will sometimes lie along the shore,

blocks.text: che parte sono in acqua e parte in terra,
blocks.mandelbaum: with part of them on land and part in water,

blocks.text: e come là tra li Tedeschi lurchi
blocks.mandelbaum: and just as there, among the guzzling Germans,

blocks.text: lo bivero s’assetta a far sua guerra,
blocks.mandelbaum: the beaver sets himself when he means war,

blocks.text: così la fiera pessima si stava
blocks.mandelbaum: so did that squalid beast lie on the margin

blocks.text: su l’orlo ch’è di pietra e ’l sabbion serra.
blocks.mandelbaum: of stone that serves as border for the sand.

blocks.text: Nel vano tutta sua coda guizzava,
blocks.mandelbaum: And all his tail was quivering in the void

blocks.text: torcendo in sù la venenosa forca
blocks.mandelbaum: while twisting upward its envenomed fork,

blocks.text: ch’a guisa di scorpion la punta armava.
blocks.mandelbaum: which had a tip just like a scorpion’s.

blocks.text: Lo duca disse: «Or convien che si torca
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide said: “Now we’d better bend our path

blocks.text: la nostra via un poco insino a quella
blocks.mandelbaum: a little, till we reach as far as that

blocks.text: bestia malvagia che colà si corca».
blocks.mandelbaum: malicious beast which crouches over there.”

blocks.text: Però scendemmo a la destra mammella,
blocks.mandelbaum: Thus we descended on the right hand side

blocks.text: e diece passi femmo in su lo stremo,
blocks.mandelbaum: and moved ten paces on the stony brink

blocks.text: per ben cessar la rena e la fiammella.
blocks.mandelbaum: in order to avoid the sand and fire.

blocks.text: E quando noi a lei venuti semo,
blocks.mandelbaum: When we had reached the sprawling beast, I saw—

blocks.text: poco più oltre veggio in su la rena
blocks.mandelbaum: a little farther on, upon the sand—

blocks.text: gente seder propinqua al loco scemo.
blocks.mandelbaum: some sinners sitting near the fissured rock.

blocks.text: Quivi ’l maestro «Acciò che tutta piena
blocks.mandelbaum: And here my master said to me: “So that

blocks.text: esperïenza d’esto giron porti»,
blocks.mandelbaum: you may experience this ring in full,

blocks.text: mi disse, «va, e vedi la lor mena.
blocks.mandelbaum: go now, and see the state in which they are.

blocks.text: Li tuoi ragionamenti sian là corti;
blocks.mandelbaum: But keep your conversation with them brief;

blocks.text: mentre che torni, parlerò con questa,
blocks.mandelbaum: till you return, I’ll parley with this beast,

blocks.text: che ne conceda i suoi omeri forti».
blocks.mandelbaum: to see if he can lend us his strong shoulders.”

blocks.text: Così ancor su per la strema testa
blocks.mandelbaum: So I went on alone and even farther

blocks.text: di quel settimo cerchio tutto solo
blocks.mandelbaum: along the seventh circle’s outer margin,

blocks.text: andai, dove sedea la gente mesta.
blocks.mandelbaum: to where the melancholy people sat.

blocks.text: Per li occhi fora scoppiava lor duolo;
blocks.mandelbaum: Despondency was bursting from their eyes;

blocks.text: di qua, di là soccorrien con le mani
blocks.mandelbaum: this side, then that, their hands kept fending off,

blocks.text: quando a’ vapori, e quando al caldo suolo:
blocks.mandelbaum: at times the flames, at times the burning soil:

blocks.text: non altrimenti fan di state i cani
blocks.mandelbaum: not otherwise do dogs in summer—now

blocks.text: or col ceffo or col piè, quando son morsi
blocks.mandelbaum: with muzzle, now with paw—when they are bitten

blocks.text: o da pulci o da mosche o da tafani.
blocks.mandelbaum: by fleas or gnats or by the sharp gadfly.

blocks.text: Poi che nel viso a certi li occhi porsi,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I had set my eyes upon the faces

blocks.text: ne’ quali ’l doloroso foco casca,
blocks.mandelbaum: of some on whom that painful fire falls,

blocks.text: non ne conobbi alcun; ma io m’accorsi
blocks.mandelbaum: I recognized no one; but I did notice

blocks.text: che dal collo a ciascun pendea una tasca
blocks.mandelbaum: that from the neck of each a purse was hung

blocks.text: ch’avea certo colore e certo segno,
blocks.mandelbaum: that had a special color and an emblem,

blocks.text: e quindi par che ’l loro occhio si pasca.
blocks.mandelbaum: and their eyes seemed to feast upon these pouches.

blocks.text: E com’ io riguardando tra lor vegno,
blocks.mandelbaum: Looking about—when I had come among them—

blocks.text: in una borsa gialla vidi azzurro
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw a yellow purse with azure on it

blocks.text: che d’un leone avea faccia e contegno.
blocks.mandelbaum: that had the face and manner of a lion.

blocks.text: Poi, procedendo di mio sguardo il curro,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then, as I let my eyes move farther on,

blocks.text: vidine un’altra come sangue rossa,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw another purse that was bloodred,

blocks.text: mostrando un’oca bianca più che burro.
blocks.mandelbaum: and it displayed a goose more white than butter.

blocks.text: E un che d’una scrofa azzurra e grossa
blocks.mandelbaum: And one who had an azure, pregnant sow

blocks.text: segnato avea lo suo sacchetto bianco,
blocks.mandelbaum: inscribed as emblem on his white pouch, said

blocks.text: mi disse: «Che fai tu in questa fossa?
blocks.mandelbaum: to me: “What are you doing in this pit?

blocks.text: Or te ne va; e perché se’ vivo anco,
blocks.mandelbaum: Now you be off; and since you’re still alive,

blocks.text: sappi che ’l mio vicin Vitalïano
blocks.mandelbaum: remember that my neighbor Vitaliano

blocks.text: sederà qui dal mio sinistro fianco.
blocks.mandelbaum: shall yet sit here, upon my left hand side.

blocks.text: Con questi Fiorentin son padoano:
blocks.mandelbaum: Among these Florentines, I’m Paduan;

blocks.text: spesse fïate mi ’ntronan li orecchi
blocks.mandelbaum: I often hear them thunder in my ears,

blocks.text: gridando: “Vegna ’l cavalier sovrano,
blocks.mandelbaum: shouting, ‘Now let the sovereign cavalier,

blocks.text: che recherà la tasca con tre becchi!”».
blocks.mandelbaum: the one who’ll bring the purse with three goats, come!'”

blocks.text: Qui distorse la bocca e di fuor trasse
blocks.mandelbaum: At this he slewed his mouth, and then he stuck

blocks.text: la lingua, come bue che ’l naso lecchi.
blocks.mandelbaum: his tongue out, like an ox that licks its nose.

blocks.text: E io, temendo no ’l più star crucciasse
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, afraid that any longer stay

blocks.text: lui che di poco star m’avea ’mmonito,
blocks.mandelbaum: might anger him who’d warned me to be brief,

blocks.text: torna’mi in dietro da l’anime lasse.
blocks.mandelbaum: made my way back from those exhausted souls.

blocks.text: Trova’ il duca mio ch’era salito
blocks.mandelbaum: I found my guide, who had already climbed

blocks.text: già su la groppa del fiero animale,
blocks.mandelbaum: upon the back of that brute animal,

blocks.text: e disse a me: «Or sie forte e ardito.
blocks.mandelbaum: and he told me: “Be strong and daring now,

blocks.text: Omai si scende per sì fatte scale;
blocks.mandelbaum: for our descent is by this kind of stairs:

blocks.text: monta dinanzi, ch’i’ voglio esser mezzo,
blocks.mandelbaum: you mount in front; I want to be between,

blocks.text: sì che la coda non possa far male».
blocks.mandelbaum: so that the tail can’t do you any harm.”

blocks.text: Qual è colui che sì presso ha ’l riprezzo
blocks.mandelbaum: As one who feels the quartan fever near

blocks.text: de la quartana, c’ha già l’unghie smorte,
blocks.mandelbaum: and shivers, with his nails already blue,

blocks.text: e triema tutto pur guardando ’l rezzo,
blocks.mandelbaum: the sight of shade enough to make him shudder,

blocks.text: tal divenn’ io a le parole porte;
blocks.mandelbaum: so I became when I had heard these words;

blocks.text: ma vergogna mi fé le sue minacce,
blocks.mandelbaum: but then I felt the threat of shame, which makes

blocks.text: che innanzi a buon segnor fa servo forte.
blocks.mandelbaum: a servant—in his kind lord’s presence—brave.

blocks.text: I’ m’assettai in su quelle spallacce;
blocks.mandelbaum: I settled down on those enormous shoulders;

blocks.text: sì volli dir, ma la voce non venne
blocks.mandelbaum: I wished to say (and yet my voice did not

blocks.text: com’ io credetti: ‘Fa che tu m’abbracce’.
blocks.mandelbaum: come as I thought): “See that you hold me tight.”

blocks.text: Ma esso, ch’altra volta mi sovvenne
blocks.mandelbaum: But he who—other times, in other dangers—

blocks.text: ad altro forse, tosto ch’i’ montai
blocks.mandelbaum: sustained me, just as soon as I had mounted,

blocks.text: con le braccia m’avvinse e mi sostenne;
blocks.mandelbaum: clasped me within his arms and propped me up,

blocks.text: e disse: «Gerïon, moviti omai:
blocks.mandelbaum: and said: “Now, Geryon, move on; take care

blocks.text: le rote larghe, e lo scender sia poco;
blocks.mandelbaum: to keep your circles wide, your landing slow;

blocks.text: pensa la nova soma che tu hai».
blocks.mandelbaum: remember the new weight you’re carrying.”

blocks.text: Come la navicella esce di loco
blocks.mandelbaum: Just like a boat that, starting from its moorings,

blocks.text: in dietro in dietro, sì quindi si tolse;
blocks.mandelbaum: moves backward, backward, so that beast took off;

blocks.text: e poi ch’al tutto si sentì a gioco,
blocks.mandelbaum: and when he felt himself completely clear,

blocks.text: là ’v’ era ’l petto, la coda rivolse,
blocks.mandelbaum: he turned his tail to where his chest had been

blocks.text: e quella tesa, come anguilla, mosse,
blocks.mandelbaum: and, having stretched it, moved it like an eel,

blocks.text: e con le branche l’aere a sé raccolse.
blocks.mandelbaum: and with his paws he gathered in the air.

blocks.text: Maggior paura non credo che fosse
blocks.mandelbaum: I do not think that there was greater fear

blocks.text: quando Fetonte abbandonò li freni,
blocks.mandelbaum: in Phaethon when he let his reins go free—

blocks.text: per che ’l ciel, come pare ancor, si cosse;
blocks.mandelbaum: for which the sky, as one still sees, was scorched—

blocks.text: né quando Icaro misero le reni
blocks.mandelbaum: nor in poor Icarus when he could feel,

blocks.text: sentì spennar per la scaldata cera,
blocks.mandelbaum: his sides unwinged because the wax was melting,

blocks.text: gridando il padre a lui «Mala via tieni!»,
blocks.mandelbaum: his father shouting to him, “That way’s wrong!”

blocks.text: che fu la mia, quando vidi ch’i’ era
blocks.mandelbaum: than was in me when, on all sides, I saw

blocks.text: ne l’aere d’ogne parte, e vidi spenta
blocks.mandelbaum: that I was in the air, and everything

blocks.text: ogne veduta fuor che de la fera.
blocks.mandelbaum: had faded from my sight—except the beast.

blocks.text: Ella sen va notando lenta lenta;
blocks.mandelbaum: Slowly, slowly, swimming, he moves on;

blocks.text: rota e discende, ma non me n’accorgo
blocks.mandelbaum: he wheels and he descends, but I feel only

blocks.text: se non che al viso e di sotto mi venta.
blocks.mandelbaum: the wind upon my face and the wind rising.

blocks.text: Io sentia già da la man destra il gorgo
blocks.mandelbaum: Already, on our right, I heard the torrent

blocks.text: far sotto noi un orribile scroscio,
blocks.mandelbaum: resounding, there beneath us, horribly,

blocks.text: per che con li occhi ’n giù la testa sporgo.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that I stretched my neck and looked below.

blocks.text: Allor fu’ io più timido a lo stoscio,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then I was more afraid of falling off,

blocks.text: però ch’i’ vidi fuochi e senti’ pianti;
blocks.mandelbaum: for I saw fires and I heard laments,

blocks.text: ond’ io tremando tutto mi raccoscio.
blocks.mandelbaum: at which I tremble, crouching, and hold fast.

blocks.text: E vidi poi, ché nol vedea davanti,
blocks.mandelbaum: And now I saw what I had missed before:

blocks.text: lo scendere e ’l girar per li gran mali
blocks.mandelbaum: his wheeling and descent—because great torments

blocks.text: che s’appressavan da diversi canti.
blocks.mandelbaum: were drawing closer to us on all sides.

blocks.text: Come ’l falcon ch’è stato assai su l’ali,
blocks.mandelbaum: Just as a falcon long upon the wing—

blocks.text: che sanza veder logoro o uccello
blocks.mandelbaum: who, seeing neither lure nor bird, compels

blocks.text: fa dire al falconiere «Omè, tu cali!»,
blocks.mandelbaum: the falconer to cry, “Ah me, you fall!”—

blocks.text: discende lasso onde si move isnello,
blocks.mandelbaum: descends, exhausted, in a hundred circles,

blocks.text: per cento rote, e da lunge si pone
blocks.mandelbaum: where he had once been swift, and sets himself,

blocks.text: dal suo maestro, disdegnoso e fello;
blocks.mandelbaum: embittered and enraged, far from his master;

blocks.text: così ne puose al fondo Gerïone
blocks.mandelbaum: such, at the bottom of the jagged rock,

blocks.text: al piè al piè de la stagliata rocca,
blocks.mandelbaum: was Geryon, when he had set us down.

blocks.text: e, discarcate le nostre persone,
blocks.mandelbaum: And once our weight was lifted from his back,

blocks.text: si dileguò come da corda cocca.
blocks.mandelbaum: he vanished like an arrow from a bow.

blocks.text: Luogo è in inferno detto Malebolge,
blocks.mandelbaum: There is a place in Hell called Malebolge,

blocks.text: tutto di pietra di color ferrigno,
blocks.mandelbaum: made all of stone the color of crude iron,

blocks.text: come la cerchia che dintorno il volge.
blocks.mandelbaum: as is the wall that makes its way around it.

blocks.text: Nel dritto mezzo del campo maligno
blocks.mandelbaum: Right in the middle of this evil field

blocks.text: vaneggia un pozzo assai largo e profondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: is an abyss, a broad and yawning pit,

blocks.text: di cui suo loco dicerò l’ordigno.
blocks.mandelbaum: whose structure I shall tell in its due place.

blocks.text: Quel cinghio che rimane adunque è tondo
blocks.mandelbaum: The belt, then, that extends between the pit

blocks.text: tra ’l pozzo e ’l piè de l’alta ripa dura,
blocks.mandelbaum: and that hard, steep wall’s base is circular;

blocks.text: e ha distinto in dieci valli il fondo.
blocks.mandelbaum: its bottom has been split into ten valleys.

blocks.text: Quale, dove per guardia de le mura
blocks.mandelbaum: Just as, where moat on moat surrounds a castle

blocks.text: più e più fossi cingon li castelli,
blocks.mandelbaum: in order to keep guard upon the walls,

blocks.text: la parte dove son rende figura,
blocks.mandelbaum: the ground they occupy will form a pattern,

blocks.text: tale imagine quivi facean quelli;
blocks.mandelbaum: so did the valleys here form a design;

blocks.text: e come a tai fortezze da’ lor sogli
blocks.mandelbaum: and as such fortresses have bridges running

blocks.text: a la ripa di fuor son ponticelli,
blocks.mandelbaum: right from their thresholds toward the outer bank,

blocks.text: così da imo de la roccia scogli
blocks.mandelbaum: so here, across the banks and ditches, ridges

blocks.text: movien che ricidien li argini e ’ fossi
blocks.mandelbaum: ran from the base of that rock wall until

blocks.text: infino al pozzo che i tronca e raccogli.
blocks.mandelbaum: the pit that cuts them short and joins them all.

blocks.text: In questo luogo, de la schiena scossi
blocks.mandelbaum: This was the place in which we found ourselves

blocks.text: di Gerïon, trovammoci; e ’l poeta
blocks.mandelbaum: when Geryon had put us down; the poet

blocks.text: tenne a sinistra, e io dietro mi mossi.
blocks.mandelbaum: held to the left, and I walked at his back.

blocks.text: A la man destra vidi nova pieta,
blocks.mandelbaum: Upon the right I saw new misery,

blocks.text: novo tormento e novi frustatori,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw new tortures and new torturers,

blocks.text: di che la prima bolgia era repleta.
blocks.mandelbaum: filling the first of Malebolge’s moats.

blocks.text: Nel fondo erano ignudi i peccatori;
blocks.mandelbaum: Along its bottom, naked sinners moved,

blocks.text: dal mezzo in qua ci venien verso ’l volto,
blocks.mandelbaum: to our side of the middle, facing us;

blocks.text: di là con noi, ma con passi maggiori,
blocks.mandelbaum: beyond that, they moved with us, but more quickly—

blocks.text: come i Roman per l’essercito molto,
blocks.mandelbaum: as, in the year of Jubilee, the Romans,

blocks.text: l’anno del giubileo, su per lo ponte
blocks.mandelbaum: confronted by great crowds, contrived a plan

blocks.text: hanno a passar la gente modo colto,
blocks.mandelbaum: that let the people pass across the bridge,

blocks.text: che da l’un lato tutti hanno la fronte
blocks.mandelbaum: for to one side went all who had their eyes

blocks.text: verso ’l castello e vanno a Santo Pietro,
blocks.mandelbaum: upon the Castle, heading toward St. Peter’s,

blocks.text: da l’altra sponda vanno verso ’l monte.
blocks.mandelbaum: and to the other, those who faced the Mount.

blocks.text: Di qua, di là, su per lo sasso tetro
blocks.mandelbaum: Both left and right, along the somber rock,

blocks.text: vidi demon cornuti con gran ferze,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw horned demons with enormous whips,

blocks.text: che li battien crudelmente di retro.
blocks.mandelbaum: who lashed those spirits cruelly from behind.

blocks.text: Ahi come facean lor levar le berze
blocks.mandelbaum: Ah, how their first strokes made those sinners lift

blocks.text: a le prime percosse! già nessuno
blocks.mandelbaum: their heels! Indeed no sinner waited for

blocks.text: le seconde aspettava né le terze.
blocks.mandelbaum: a second stroke to fall—or for a third.

blocks.text: Mentr’ io andava, li occhi miei in uno
blocks.mandelbaum: And as I moved ahead, my eyes met those

blocks.text: furo scontrati; e io sì tosto dissi:
blocks.mandelbaum: of someone else, and suddenly I said:

blocks.text: «Già di veder costui non son digiuno».
blocks.mandelbaum: “I was not spared the sight of him before.”

blocks.text: Per ch’ïo a figurarlo i piedi affissi;
blocks.mandelbaum: And so I stayed my steps, to study him;

blocks.text: e ’l dolce duca meco si ristette,
blocks.mandelbaum: my gentle guide had stopped together with me

blocks.text: e assentio ch’alquanto in dietro gissi.
blocks.mandelbaum: and gave me leave to take a few steps back.

blocks.text: E quel frustato celar si credette
blocks.mandelbaum: That scourged soul thought that he could hide himself

blocks.text: bassando ’l viso; ma poco li valse,
blocks.mandelbaum: by lowering his face; it helped him little,

blocks.text: ch’io dissi: «O tu che l’occhio a terra gette,
blocks.mandelbaum: for I said: “You, who cast your eyes upon

blocks.text: se le fazion che porti non son false,
blocks.mandelbaum: the ground, if these your features are not false,

blocks.text: Venedico se’ tu Caccianemico.
blocks.mandelbaum: must be Venedico Caccianemico;

blocks.text: Ma che ti mena a sì pungenti salse?».
blocks.mandelbaum: but what brings you to sauces so piquant?”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Mal volontier lo dico;
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “I speak unwillingly;

blocks.text: ma sforzami la tua chiara favella,
blocks.mandelbaum: but your plain speech, that brings the memory

blocks.text: che mi fa sovvenir del mondo antico.
blocks.mandelbaum: of the old world to me, is what compels me;

blocks.text: I’ fui colui che la Ghisolabella
blocks.mandelbaum: For it was I who led Ghisolabella

blocks.text: condussi a far la voglia del marchese,
blocks.mandelbaum: to do as the Marquis would have her do—

blocks.text: come che suoni la sconcia novella.
blocks.mandelbaum: however they retell that filthy tale.

blocks.text: E non pur io qui piango bolognese;
blocks.mandelbaum: I’m not the only Bolognese who weeps here;

blocks.text: anzi n’è questo loco tanto pieno,
blocks.mandelbaum: indeed, this place is so crammed full of us

blocks.text: che tante lingue non son ora apprese
blocks.mandelbaum: that not so many tongues have learned to say

blocks.text: a dicer ‘sipa’ tra Sàvena e Reno;
blocks.mandelbaum: sipa between the Savena and Reno;

blocks.text: e se di ciò vuoi fede o testimonio,
blocks.mandelbaum: if you want faith and testament of that,

blocks.text: rècati a mente il nostro avaro seno».
blocks.mandelbaum: just call to mind our avaricious hearts.”

blocks.text: Così parlando il percosse un demonio
blocks.mandelbaum: And as he spoke, a demon cudgeled him

blocks.text: de la sua scurïada, e disse: «Via,
blocks.mandelbaum: with his horsewhip and cried: “Be off, you pimp,

blocks.text: ruffian! qui non son femmine da conio».
blocks.mandelbaum: there are no women here for you to trick.”

blocks.text: I’ mi raggiunsi con la scorta mia;
blocks.mandelbaum: I joined my escort once again; and then

blocks.text: poscia con pochi passi divenimmo
blocks.mandelbaum: with but few steps, we came upon a place

blocks.text: là ’v’ uno scoglio de la ripa uscia.
blocks.mandelbaum: where, from the bank, a rocky ridge ran out.

blocks.text: Assai leggeramente quel salimmo;
blocks.mandelbaum: We climbed quite easily along that height;

blocks.text: e vòlti a destra su per la sua scheggia,
blocks.mandelbaum: and turning right upon its jagged back,

blocks.text: da quelle cerchie etterne ci partimmo.
blocks.mandelbaum: we took our leave of those eternal circlings.

blocks.text: Quando noi fummo là dov’ el vaneggia
blocks.mandelbaum: When we had reached the point where that ridge opens

blocks.text: di sotto per dar passo a li sferzati,

blocks.text: E quei: «S’i’ fossi di piombato vetro,
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “Were I a leaded mirror,

blocks.text: l’imagine di fuor tua non trarrei

blocks.mandelbaum: For even now your thoughts have joined my own;

blocks.text: con simile atto e con simile faccia,

blocks.text: noi fuggirem l’imaginata caccia».

blocks.text: Già non compié di tal consiglio rendere,
blocks.mandelbaum: He’d hardly finished telling me his plan

blocks.text: tanto che solo una camiscia vesta;

blocks.text: e giù dal collo de la ripa dura

blocks.mandelbaum: And one of them replied: “The yellow cloaks

blocks.text: son di piombo sì grosse, che li pesi

blocks.mandelbaum: is still to be observed around Gardingo.”
