blocks.text: né tante pestilenzie né sì ree
blocks.mandelbaum: she never showed—with all of Ethiopia

blocks.text: mostrò già mai con tutta l’Etïopia
blocks.mandelbaum: or all the land that borders the Red Sea—

blocks.text: né con ciò che di sopra al Mar Rosso èe.
blocks.mandelbaum: so many, such malignant, pestilences.

blocks.text: Tra questa cruda e tristissima copia
blocks.mandelbaum: Among this cruel and depressing swarm,

blocks.text: corrëan genti nude e spaventate,
blocks.mandelbaum: ran people who were naked, terrified,

blocks.text: sanza sperar pertugio o elitropia:
blocks.mandelbaum: with no hope of a hole or heliotrope.

blocks.text: con serpi le man dietro avean legate;
blocks.mandelbaum: Their hands were tied behind by serpents; these

blocks.text: quelle ficcavan per le ren la coda
blocks.mandelbaum: had thrust their head and tail right through the loins,

blocks.text: e ’l capo, ed eran dinanzi aggroppate.
blocks.mandelbaum: and then were knotted on the other side.

blocks.text: Ed ecco a un ch’era da nostra proda,
blocks.mandelbaum: And—there!—a serpent sprang with force at one

blocks.text: s’avventò un serpente che ’l trafisse
blocks.mandelbaum: who stood upon our shore, transfixing him

blocks.text: là dove ’l collo a le spalle s’annoda.
blocks.mandelbaum: just where the neck and shoulders form a knot.

blocks.text: Né O sì tosto mai né I si scrisse,
blocks.mandelbaum: No o or i has ever been transcribed

blocks.text: com’ el s’accese e arse, e cener tutto
blocks.mandelbaum: so quickly as that soul caught fire and burned

blocks.text: convenne che cascando divenisse;
blocks.mandelbaum: and, as he fell, completely turned to ashes;

blocks.text: e poi che fu a terra sì distrutto,
blocks.mandelbaum: and when he lay, undone, upon the ground,

blocks.text: la polver si raccolse per sé stessa
blocks.mandelbaum: the dust of him collected by itself

blocks.text: e ’n quel medesmo ritornò di butto.
blocks.mandelbaum: and instantly returned to what it was:

blocks.text: Così per li gran savi si confessa
blocks.mandelbaum: just so, it is asserted by great sages,

blocks.text: che la fenice more e poi rinasce,
blocks.mandelbaum: that, when it reaches its five—hundredth year,

blocks.text: quando al cinquecentesimo anno appressa;
blocks.mandelbaum: the phoenix dies and then is born again;

blocks.text: erba né biado in sua vita non pasce,
blocks.mandelbaum: lifelong it never feeds on grass or grain,

blocks.text: ma sol d’incenso lagrime e d’amomo,
blocks.mandelbaum: only on drops of incense and amomum;

blocks.text: e nardo e mirra son l’ultime fasce.
blocks.mandelbaum: its final winding sheets are nard and myrrh.

blocks.text: E qual è quel che cade, e non sa como,
blocks.mandelbaum: And just as he who falls, and knows not how—

blocks.text: per forza di demon ch’a terra il tira,
blocks.mandelbaum: by demon’s force that drags him to the ground

blocks.text: o d’altra oppilazion che lega l’omo,
blocks.mandelbaum: or by some other hindrance that binds man—

blocks.text: quando si leva, che ’ntorno si mira
blocks.mandelbaum: who, when he rises, stares about him, all

blocks.text: tutto smarrito de la grande angoscia
blocks.mandelbaum: bewildered by the heavy anguish he

blocks.text: ch’elli ha sofferta, e guardando sospira:
blocks.mandelbaum: has suffered, sighing as he looks around;

blocks.text: tal era ’l peccator levato poscia.
blocks.mandelbaum: so did this sinner stare when he arose.

blocks.text: Oh potenza di Dio, quant’ è severa,
blocks.mandelbaum: Oh, how severe it is, the power of God

blocks.text: che cotai colpi per vendetta croscia!
blocks.mandelbaum: that, as its vengeance, showers down such blows!

blocks.text: Lo duca il domandò poi chi ello era;
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide then asked that sinner who he was;

blocks.text: per ch’ei rispuose: «Io piovvi di Toscana,
blocks.mandelbaum: to this he answered: “Not long since, I rained

blocks.text: poco tempo è, in questa gola fiera.
blocks.mandelbaum: from Tuscany into this savage maw.

blocks.text: Vita bestial mi piacque e non umana,
blocks.mandelbaum: Mule that I was, the bestial life pleased me

blocks.text: sì come a mul ch’i’ fui; son Vanni Fucci
blocks.mandelbaum: and not the human; I am Vanni Fucci,

blocks.text: bestia, e Pistoia mi fu degna tana».
blocks.mandelbaum: beast; and the den that suited me—Pistoia.”

blocks.text: E ïo al duca: «Dilli che non mucci,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I to Virgil: “Tell him not to slip

blocks.text: e domanda che colpa qua giù ’l pinse;
blocks.mandelbaum: away, and ask what sin has thrust him here;

blocks.text: ch’io ’l vidi uomo di sangue e di crucci».
blocks.mandelbaum: I knew him as a man of blood and anger.”

blocks.text: E ’l peccator, che ’ntese, non s’infinse,
blocks.mandelbaum: The sinner heard and did not try to feign

blocks.text: ma drizzò verso me l’animo e ’l volto,
blocks.mandelbaum: but turned his mind and face, intent, toward me;

blocks.text: e di trista vergogna si dipinse;
blocks.mandelbaum: and coloring with miserable shame,

blocks.text: poi disse: «Più mi duol che tu m’hai colto
blocks.mandelbaum: he said: “I suffer more because you’ve caught me

blocks.text: ne la miseria dove tu mi vedi,
blocks.mandelbaum: in this, the misery you see, than I

blocks.text: che quando fui de l’altra vita tolto.
blocks.mandelbaum: suffered when taken from the other life.

blocks.text: Io non posso negar quel che tu chiedi;
blocks.mandelbaum: I can’t refuse to answer what you ask:

blocks.text: in giù son messo tanto perch’ io fui
blocks.mandelbaum: I am set down so far because I robbed

blocks.text: ladro a la sagrestia d’i belli arredi,
blocks.mandelbaum: the sacristy of its fair ornaments,

blocks.text: e falsamente già fu apposto altrui.
blocks.mandelbaum: and someone else was falsely blamed for that.

blocks.text: Ma perché di tal vista tu non godi,
blocks.mandelbaum: But lest this sight give you too much delight,

blocks.text: se mai sarai di fuor da’ luoghi bui,
blocks.mandelbaum: if you can ever leave these lands of darkness,

blocks.text: apri li orecchi al mio annunzio, e odi.
blocks.mandelbaum: open your ears to my announcement, hear:

blocks.text: Pistoia in pria d’i Neri si dimagra;
blocks.mandelbaum: Pistoia first will strip herself of Blacks,

blocks.text: poi Fiorenza rinova gente e modi.
blocks.mandelbaum: then Florence will renew her men and manners.

blocks.text: Tragge Marte vapor di Val di Magra
blocks.mandelbaum: From Val di Magra, Mars will draw a vapor

blocks.text: ch’è di torbidi nuvoli involuto;
blocks.mandelbaum: which turbid clouds will try to wrap; the clash

blocks.text: e con tempesta impetüosa e agra
blocks.mandelbaum: between them will be fierce, impetuous,

blocks.text: sovra Campo Picen fia combattuto;
blocks.mandelbaum: a tempest, fought upon Campo Piceno,

blocks.text: ond’ ei repente spezzerà la nebbia,
blocks.mandelbaum: until that vapor, vigorous, shall crack

blocks.text: sì ch’ogne Bianco ne sarà feruto.
blocks.mandelbaum: the mist, and every White be struck by it.

blocks.text: E detto l’ho perché doler ti debbia!».
blocks.mandelbaum: And I have told you this to make you grieve.”

blocks.text: Al fine de le sue parole il ladro
blocks.mandelbaum: When he had finished with his words, the thief

blocks.text: le mani alzò con amendue le fiche,
blocks.mandelbaum: raised high his fists with both figs cocked and cried:

blocks.text: gridando: «Togli, Dio, ch’a te le squadro!».
blocks.mandelbaum: “Take that, o God; I square them off for you!”

blocks.text: Da indi in qua mi fuor le serpi amiche,
blocks.mandelbaum: From that time on, those serpents were my friends,

blocks.text: perch’ una li s’avvolse allora al collo,
blocks.mandelbaum: for one of them coiled then around his neck,

blocks.text: come dicesse ‘Non vo’ che più diche’;
blocks.mandelbaum: as if to say, “I’ll have you speak no more”;

blocks.text: e un’altra a le braccia, e rilegollo,
blocks.mandelbaum: another wound about his arms and bound him

blocks.text: ribadendo sé stessa sì dinanzi,
blocks.mandelbaum: again and wrapped itself in front so firmly,

blocks.text: che non potea con esse dare un crollo.
blocks.mandelbaum: he could not even make them budge an inch.

blocks.text: Ahi Pistoia, Pistoia, ché non stanzi
blocks.mandelbaum: Pistoia, ah, Pistoia, must you last:

blocks.text: d’incenerarti sì che più non duri,
blocks.mandelbaum: why not decree your self-incineration,

blocks.text: poi che ’n mal fare il seme tuo avanzi?
blocks.mandelbaum: since you surpass your seed in wickedness?

blocks.text: Per tutt’ i cerchi de lo ’nferno scuri
blocks.mandelbaum: Throughout the shadowed circles of deep Hell,

blocks.text: non vidi spirto in Dio tanto superbo,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw no soul against God so rebel,

blocks.text: non quel che cadde a Tebe giù da’ muri.
blocks.mandelbaum: not even he who fell from Theban walls.

blocks.text: El si fuggì che non parlò più verbo;
blocks.mandelbaum: He fled and could not say another word;

blocks.text: e io vidi un centauro pien di rabbia
blocks.mandelbaum: and then I saw a Centaur full of anger,

blocks.text: venir chiamando: «Ov’ è, ov’ è l’acerbo?».
blocks.mandelbaum: shouting: “Where is he, where’s that bitter one?”

blocks.text: Maremma non cred’ io che tante n’abbia,
blocks.mandelbaum: I do not think Maremma has the number

blocks.text: quante bisce elli avea su per la groppa
blocks.mandelbaum: of snakes that Centaur carried on his haunch

blocks.text: infin ove comincia nostra labbia.
blocks.mandelbaum: until the part that takes our human form.

blocks.text: Sovra le spalle, dietro da la coppa,
blocks.mandelbaum: Upon his shoulders and behind his nape

blocks.text: con l’ali aperte li giacea un draco;
blocks.mandelbaum: there lay a dragon with its wings outstretched;

blocks.text: e quello affuoca qualunque s’intoppa.
blocks.mandelbaum: it sets ablaze all those it intercepts.

blocks.text: Lo mio maestro disse: «Questi è Caco,
blocks.mandelbaum: My master said: “That Centaur there is Cacus,

blocks.text: che, sotto ’l sasso di monte Aventino,
blocks.mandelbaum: who often made a lake of blood within

blocks.text: di sangue fece spesse volte laco.
blocks.mandelbaum: a grotto underneath Mount Aventine.

blocks.text: Non va co’ suoi fratei per un cammino,
blocks.mandelbaum: He does not ride the same road as his brothers

blocks.text: per lo furto che frodolente fece
blocks.mandelbaum: because he stole—and most deceitfully—

blocks.text: del grande armento ch’elli ebbe a vicino;
blocks.mandelbaum: from the great herd nearby; his crooked deeds

blocks.text: onde cessar le sue opere biece
blocks.mandelbaum: ended beneath the club of Hercules,

blocks.text: sotto la mazza d’Ercule, che forse
blocks.mandelbaum: who may have given him a hundred blows—

blocks.text: gliene diè cento, e non sentì le diece».
blocks.mandelbaum: but he was not alive to feel the tenth.”

blocks.text: Mentre che sì parlava, ed el trascorse,
blocks.mandelbaum: While he was talking so, Cacus ran by

blocks.text: e tre spiriti venner sotto noi,
blocks.mandelbaum: and, just beneath our ledge, three souls arrived;

blocks.text: de’ quai né io né ’l duca mio s’accorse,
blocks.mandelbaum: but neither I nor my guide noticed them

blocks.text: se non quando gridar: «Chi siete voi?»;
blocks.mandelbaum: until they had cried out: “And who are you?”

blocks.text: per che nostra novella si ristette,
blocks.mandelbaum: At this the words we shared were interrupted,

blocks.text: e intendemmo pur ad essi poi.
blocks.mandelbaum: and we attended only to those spirits.

blocks.text: Io non li conoscea; ma ei seguette,
blocks.mandelbaum: I did not recognize them, but it happened,

blocks.text: come suol seguitar per alcun caso,
blocks.mandelbaum: as chance will usually bring about,

blocks.text: che l’un nomar un altro convenette,
blocks.mandelbaum: that one of them called out the other’s name,

blocks.text: dicendo: «Cianfa dove fia rimaso?»;
blocks.mandelbaum: exclaiming: “Where was Cianfa left behind?”

blocks.text: per ch’io, acciò che ’l duca stesse attento,
blocks.mandelbaum: At this, so that my guide might be alert,

blocks.text: mi puosi ’l dito su dal mento al naso.
blocks.mandelbaum: I raised my finger up from chin to nose.

blocks.text: Se tu se’ or, lettore, a creder lento
blocks.mandelbaum: If, reader, you are slow now to believe

blocks.text: ciò ch’io dirò, non sarà maraviglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: what I shall tell, that is no cause for wonder,

blocks.text: ché io che ’l vidi, a pena il mi consento.
blocks.mandelbaum: for I who saw it hardly can accept it.

blocks.text: Com’ io tenea levate in lor le ciglia,
blocks.mandelbaum: As I kept my eyes fixed upon those sinners,

blocks.text: e un serpente con sei piè si lancia
blocks.mandelbaum: a serpent with six feet springs out against

blocks.text: dinanzi a l’uno, e tutto a lui s’appiglia.
blocks.mandelbaum: one of the three, and clutches him completely.

blocks.text: Co’ piè di mezzo li avvinse la pancia
blocks.mandelbaum: It gripped his belly with its middle feet,

blocks.text: e con li anterïor le braccia prese;
blocks.mandelbaum: and with its forefeet grappled his two arms;

blocks.text: poi li addentò e l’una e l’altra guancia;
blocks.mandelbaum: and then it sank its teeth in both his cheeks;

blocks.text: li diretani a le cosce distese,
blocks.mandelbaum: it stretched its rear feet out along his thighs

blocks.text: e miseli la coda tra ’mbedue
blocks.mandelbaum: and ran its tail along between the two,

blocks.text: e dietro per le ren sù la ritese.
blocks.mandelbaum: then straightened it again behind his loins.

blocks.text: Ellera abbarbicata mai non fue
blocks.mandelbaum: No ivy ever gripped a tree so fast

blocks.text: ad alber sì, come l’orribil fiera
blocks.mandelbaum: as when that horrifying monster clasped

blocks.text: per l’altrui membra avviticchiò le sue.
blocks.mandelbaum: and intertwined the other’s limbs with its.

blocks.text: Poi s’appiccar, come di calda cera
blocks.mandelbaum: Then just as if their substance were warm wax,

blocks.text: fossero stati, e mischiar lor colore,
blocks.mandelbaum: they stuck together and they mixed their colors,

blocks.text: né l’un né l’altro già parea quel ch’era:
blocks.mandelbaum: so neither seemed what he had been before;

blocks.text: come procede innanzi da l’ardore,
blocks.mandelbaum: just as, when paper’s kindled, where it still

blocks.text: per lo papiro suso, un color bruno
blocks.mandelbaum: has not caught flame in full, its color’s dark

blocks.text: che non è nero ancora e ’l bianco more.
blocks.mandelbaum: though not yet black, while white is dying off.

blocks.text: Li altri due ’l riguardavano, e ciascuno
blocks.mandelbaum: The other two souls stared, and each one cried:

blocks.text: gridava: «Omè, Agnel, come ti muti!
blocks.mandelbaum: “Ah me, Agnello, how you change! Just see,

blocks.text: Vedi che già non se’ né due né uno».
blocks.mandelbaum: you are already neither two nor one!”

blocks.text: Già eran li due capi un divenuti,
blocks.mandelbaum: Then two heads were already joined in one,

blocks.text: quando n’apparver due figure miste
blocks.mandelbaum: when in one face where two had been dissolved,

blocks.text: in una faccia, ov’ eran due perduti.
blocks.mandelbaum: two intermingled shapes appeared to us.

blocks.text: Fersi le braccia due di quattro liste;
blocks.mandelbaum: Two arms came into being from four lengths;

blocks.text: le cosce con le gambe e ’l ventre e ’l casso
blocks.mandelbaum: the thighs and legs, the belly and the chest

blocks.text: divenner membra che non fuor mai viste.
blocks.mandelbaum: became such limbs as never had been seen.

blocks.text: Ogne primaio aspetto ivi era casso:
blocks.mandelbaum: And every former shape was canceled there:

blocks.text: due e nessun l’imagine perversa
blocks.mandelbaum: that perverse image seemed to share in both—

blocks.text: parea; e tal sen gio con lento passo.
blocks.mandelbaum: and none; and so, and slowly, it moved on.

blocks.text: Come ’l ramarro sotto la gran fersa
blocks.mandelbaum: Just as the lizard, when it darts from hedge

blocks.text: dei dì canicular, cangiando sepe,
blocks.mandelbaum: to hedge, beneath the dog days’ giant lash,

blocks.text: folgore par se la via attraversa,
blocks.mandelbaum: seems, if it cross one’s path, a lightning flash,

blocks.text: sì pareva, venendo verso l’epe
blocks.mandelbaum: so seemed a blazing little serpent moving

blocks.text: de li altri due, un serpentello acceso,
blocks.mandelbaum: against the bellies of the other two,

blocks.text: livido e nero come gran di pepe;
blocks.mandelbaum: as black and livid as a peppercorn.

blocks.text: e quella parte onde prima è preso
blocks.mandelbaum: Attacking one of therm, it pierced right through

blocks.text: nostro alimento, a l’un di lor trafisse;
blocks.mandelbaum: the part where we first take our nourishment;

blocks.text: poi cadde giuso innanzi lui disteso.
blocks.mandelbaum: and then it fell before him at full length.

blocks.text: Lo trafitto ’l mirò, ma nulla disse;
blocks.mandelbaum: The one it had transfixed stared but said nothing;

blocks.text: anzi, co’ piè fermati, sbadigliava
blocks.mandelbaum: in fact he only stood his ground and yawned

blocks.text: pur come sonno o febbre l’assalisse.
blocks.mandelbaum: as one whom sleep or fever has undone.

blocks.text: Elli ’l serpente e quei lui riguardava;
blocks.mandelbaum: The serpent stared at him, he at the serpent;

blocks.text: l’un per la piaga e l’altro per la bocca
blocks.mandelbaum: one through his wound, the other through his mouth

blocks.text: fummavan forte, e ’l fummo si scontrava.
blocks.mandelbaum: were smoking violently; their smoke met.

blocks.text: Taccia Lucano ormai là dov’ e’ tocca
blocks.mandelbaum: Let Lucan now be silent, where he sings

blocks.text: del misero Sabello e di Nasidio,
blocks.mandelbaum: of sad Sabellus and Nasidius,

blocks.text: e attenda a udir quel ch’or si scocca.
blocks.mandelbaum: and wait to hear what flies off from my bow.

blocks.text: Taccia di Cadmo e d’Aretusa Ovidio,
blocks.mandelbaum: Let Ovid now be silent, where he tells

blocks.text: ché se quello in serpente e quella in fonte
blocks.mandelbaum: of Cadmus, Arethusa; if his verse

blocks.text: converte poetando, io non lo ’nvidio;
blocks.mandelbaum: has made of one a serpent, one a fountain,

blocks.text: ché due nature mai a fronte a fronte
blocks.mandelbaum: I do not envy him; he never did

blocks.text: non trasmutò sì ch’amendue le forme
blocks.mandelbaum: transmute two natures, face to face, so that

blocks.text: a cambiar lor matera fosser pronte.
blocks.mandelbaum: both forms were ready to exchange their matter.

blocks.text: Insieme si rispuosero a tai norme,
blocks.mandelbaum: These were the ways they answered to each other:

blocks.text: che ’l serpente la coda in forca fesse,
blocks.mandelbaum: the serpent split its tail into a fork;

blocks.text: e ’l feruto ristrinse insieme l’orme.
blocks.mandelbaum: the wounded sinner drew his steps together.

blocks.text: Le gambe con le cosce seco stesse
blocks.mandelbaum: The legs and then the thighs along with them

blocks.text: s’appiccar sì, che ’n poco la giuntura
blocks.mandelbaum: so fastened to each other that the juncture

blocks.text: non facea segno alcun che si paresse.
blocks.mandelbaum: soon left no sign that was discernible.

blocks.text: Togliea la coda fessa la figura
blocks.mandelbaum: Meanwhile the cleft tail took upon itself

blocks.text: che si perdeva là, e la sua pelle
blocks.mandelbaum: the form the other gradually lost;

blocks.text: si facea molle, e quella di là dura.
blocks.mandelbaum: its skin grew soft, the other’s skin grew hard.

blocks.text: Io vidi intrar le braccia per l’ascelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw the arms that drew in at his armpits

blocks.text: e i due piè de la fiera, ch’eran corti,
blocks.mandelbaum: and also saw the monster’s two short feet

blocks.text: tanto allungar quanto accorciavan quelle.
blocks.mandelbaum: grow long for just as much as those were shortened.

blocks.text: Poscia li piè di rietro, insieme attorti,
blocks.mandelbaum: The serpent’s hind feet, twisted up together,

blocks.text: diventaron lo membro che l’uom cela,
blocks.mandelbaum: became the member that man hides; just as

blocks.text: e ’l misero del suo n’avea due porti.
blocks.mandelbaum: the wretch put out two hind paws from his member.

blocks.text: Mentre che ’l fummo l’uno e l’altro vela
blocks.mandelbaum: And while the smoke veils each with a new color,

blocks.text: di color novo, e genera ’l pel suso
blocks.mandelbaum: and now breeds hair upon the skin of one,

blocks.text: per l’una parte e da l’altra il dipela,
blocks.mandelbaum: just as it strips the hair from off the other,

blocks.text: l’un si levò e l’altro cadde giuso,

blocks.text: non torcendo però le lucerne empie,
blocks.mandelbaum: they never turned aside their impious eyelamps,

blocks.text: sotto le quai ciascun cambiava muso.
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath which each of them transformed his snout:

blocks.text: Quel ch’era dritto, il trasse ver’ le tempie,

blocks.text: e di troppa matera ch’in là venne

blocks.mandelbaum: came ears upon the cheeks that had been bare;

blocks.text: ciò che non corse in dietro e si ritenne
blocks.mandelbaum: whatever had not been pulled back but kept,

blocks.text: di quel soverchio, fé naso a la faccia
blocks.mandelbaum: superfluous, then made his face a nose

blocks.text: e le labbra ingrossò quanto convenne.
blocks.mandelbaum: and thickened out his lips appropriately.

blocks.text: Quel che giacëa, il muso innanzi caccia,
blocks.mandelbaum: He who was lying down thrust out his snout;

blocks.text: e li orecchi ritira per la testa
blocks.mandelbaum: and even as the snail hauls in its horns,

blocks.text: come face le corna la lumaccia;
blocks.mandelbaum: he drew his ears straight back into his head;

blocks.text: e la lingua, ch’avëa unita e presta

blocks.text: L’anima ch’era fiera divenuta,
blocks.mandelbaum: The soul that had become an animal,

blocks.mandelbaum: now hissing, hurried off along the valley;

blocks.text: e l’altro dietro a lui parlando sputa.

blocks.text: Poscia li volse le novelle spalle,
blocks.mandelbaum: And then he turned aside his new-made shoulders

blocks.text: e disse a l’altro: «I’ vo’ che Buoso corra,
blocks.mandelbaum: and told the third soul: “I’d have Buoso run

blocks.text: com’ ho fatt’ io, carpon per questo calle».

blocks.text: Così vid’ io la settima zavorra
blocks.mandelbaum: And so I saw the seventh ballast change

blocks.text: mutare e trasmutare; e qui mi scusi
blocks.mandelbaum: and rechange; may the strangeness plead for me

blocks.text: la novità se fior la penna abborra.
blocks.mandelbaum: if there’s been some confusion in my pen.

blocks.text: E avvegna che li occhi miei confusi
blocks.mandelbaum: And though my eyes were somewhat blurred, my mind

blocks.mandelbaum: bewildered, those three sinners did not flee

blocks.text: non poter quei fuggirsi tanto chiusi,

blocks.text: ch’i’ non scorgessi ben Puccio Sciancato;

blocks.text: ed era quel che sol, di tre compagni
blocks.mandelbaum: the only soul who’d not been changed among

blocks.text: che venner prima, non era mutato;
blocks.mandelbaum: the three companions we had met at first;

blocks.text: l’altr’ era quel che tu, Gaville, piagni.
blocks.mandelbaum: the other one made you, Gaville, grieve.

blocks.text: Godi, Fiorenza, poi che se’ sì grande
blocks.mandelbaum: Be joyous, Florence, you are great indeed,

blocks.text: che per mare e per terra batti l’ali,
blocks.mandelbaum: for over sea and land you beat your wings;

blocks.text: e per lo ’nferno tuo nome si spande!
blocks.mandelbaum: through every part of Hell your name extends!

blocks.text: Tra li ladron trovai cinque cotali
blocks.mandelbaum: Among the thieves I found five citizens

blocks.text: tuoi cittadini onde mi ven vergogna,
blocks.mandelbaum: of yours—and such, that shame has taken me;

blocks.text: e tu in grande orranza non ne sali.
blocks.mandelbaum: with them, you can ascend to no high honor.

blocks.text: Ma se presso al mattin del ver si sogna,
blocks.mandelbaum: But if the dreams dreamt close to dawn are true,

blocks.text: tu sentirai, di qua da picciol tempo,
blocks.mandelbaum: then little time will pass before you feel

blocks.text: di quel che Prato, non ch’altri, t’agogna.
blocks.mandelbaum: what Prato and the others crave for you.

blocks.text: E se già fosse, non saria per tempo.
blocks.mandelbaum: Were that already come, it would not be

blocks.text: Così foss’ ei, da che pur esser dee!
blocks.mandelbaum: too soon—and let it come, since it must be!

blocks.text: ché più mi graverà, com’ più m’attempo.
blocks.mandelbaum: As I grow older, it will be more heavy.

blocks.text: Noi ci partimmo, e su per le scalee
blocks.mandelbaum: We left that deep and, by protruding stones

blocks.text: che n’avea fatto iborni a scender pria,
blocks.mandelbaum: that served as stairs for our descent before,

blocks.text: rimontò ’l duca mio e trasse mee;
blocks.mandelbaum: my guide climbed up again and drew me forward;

blocks.text: e proseguendo la solinga via,
blocks.mandelbaum: and as we took our solitary path

blocks.text: tra le schegge e tra ’ rocchi de lo scoglio
blocks.mandelbaum: among the ridge’s jagged spurs and rocks,

blocks.text: lo piè sanza la man non si spedia.
blocks.mandelbaum: our feet could not make way without our hands.

blocks.text: Allor mi dolsi, e ora mi ridoglio
blocks.mandelbaum: It grieved me then and now grieves me again

blocks.text: quando drizzo la mente a ciò ch’io vidi,
blocks.mandelbaum: when I direct my mind to what I saw;

blocks.text: e più lo ’ngegno affreno ch’i’ non soglio,
blocks.mandelbaum: and more than usual, I curb my talent,

blocks.text: perché non corra che virtù nol guidi;
blocks.mandelbaum: that it not run where virtue does not guide;

blocks.text: sì che, se stella bona o miglior cosa
blocks.mandelbaum: so that, if my kind star or something better

blocks.text: m’ha dato ’l ben, ch’io stessi nol m’invidi.
blocks.mandelbaum: has given me that gift, I not abuse it.

blocks.text: Quante ’l villan ch’al poggio si riposa,
blocks.mandelbaum: As many as the fireflies the peasant

blocks.text: nel tempo che colui che ’l mondo schiara
blocks.mandelbaum: (while resting on a hillside in the season

blocks.text: la faccia sua a noi tien meno ascosa,
blocks.mandelbaum: when he who lights the world least hides his face),

blocks.text: come la mosca cede a la zanzara,
blocks.mandelbaum: just when the fly gives way to the mosquito,

blocks.text: vede lucciole giù per la vallea,
blocks.mandelbaum: sees glimmering below, down in the valley,

blocks.text: forse colà dov’ e’ vendemmia e ara:
blocks.mandelbaum: there where perhaps he gathers grapes and tills—

blocks.text: di tante fiamme tutta risplendea
blocks.mandelbaum: so many were the flames that glittered in

blocks.text: l’ottava bolgia, sì com’ io m’accorsi
blocks.mandelbaum: the eighth abyss; I made this out as soon

blocks.text: tosto che fui là ’ve ’l fondo parea.
blocks.mandelbaum: as I had come to where one sees the bottom.

blocks.text: E qual colui che si vengiò con li orsi
blocks.mandelbaum: Even as he who was avenged by bears

blocks.text: vide ’l carro d’Elia al dipartire,
blocks.mandelbaum: saw, as it left, Elijah’s chariot—

blocks.text: quando i cavalli al cielo erti levorsi,
blocks.mandelbaum: its horses rearing, rising right to heaven—

blocks.text: che nol potea sì con li occhi seguire,
blocks.mandelbaum: when he could not keep track of it except

blocks.text: ch’el vedesse altro che la fiamma sola,
blocks.mandelbaum: by watching one lone flame in its ascent,

blocks.text: sì come nuvoletta, in sù salire:
blocks.mandelbaum: just like a little cloud that climbs on high:

blocks.text: tal si move ciascuna per la gola
blocks.mandelbaum: so, through the gullet of that ditch, each flame

blocks.text: del fosso, ché nessuna mostra ’l furto,
blocks.mandelbaum: must make its way; no flame displays its prey,

blocks.text: e ogne fiamma un peccatore invola.
blocks.mandelbaum: though every flame has carried off a sinner.

blocks.text: Io stava sovra ’l ponte a veder surto,
blocks.mandelbaum: I stood upon the bridge and leaned straight out

blocks.text: sì che s’io non avessi un ronchion preso,
blocks.mandelbaum: to see; and if I had not gripped a rock,

blocks.text: caduto sarei giù sanz’ esser urto.
blocks.mandelbaum: I should have fallen off—without a push.

blocks.text: E ’l duca che mi vide tanto atteso,
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide, who noted how intent I was,

blocks.text: disse: «Dentro dai fuochi son li spirti;
blocks.mandelbaum: told me: “Within those fires there are souls;

blocks.text: catun si fascia di quel ch’elli è inceso».
blocks.mandelbaum: each one is swathed in that which scorches him.”

blocks.text: «Maestro mio», rispuos’ io, «per udirti
blocks.mandelbaum: “My master,” I replied, “on hearing you,

blocks.text: son io più certo; ma già m’era avviso
blocks.mandelbaum: I am more sure; but I’d already thought

blocks.text: che così fosse, e già voleva dirti:
blocks.mandelbaum: that it was so, and I had meant to ask:

blocks.text: chi è ’n quel foco che vien sì diviso
blocks.mandelbaum: Who is within the flame that comes so twinned

blocks.text: di sopra, che par surger de la pira
blocks.mandelbaum: above that it would seem to rise out of

blocks.text: dov’ Eteòcle col fratel fu miso?».
blocks.mandelbaum: the pyre Eteocles shared with his brother?”

blocks.text: Rispuose a me: «Là dentro si martira
blocks.mandelbaum: He answered me: “Within that flame, Ulysses

blocks.text: Ulisse e Dïomede, e così insieme
blocks.mandelbaum: and Diomedes suffer; they, who went

blocks.text: a la vendetta vanno come a l’ira;
blocks.mandelbaum: as one to rage, now share one punishment.

blocks.text: e dentro da la lor fiamma si geme
blocks.mandelbaum: And there, together in their flame, they grieve

blocks.text: l’agguato del caval che fé la porta
blocks.mandelbaum: over the horse’s fraud that caused a breach—

blocks.text: onde uscì de’ Romani il gentil seme.
blocks.mandelbaum: the gate that let Rome’s noble seed escape.

blocks.text: Piangevisi entro l’arte per che, morta,
blocks.mandelbaum: There they regret the guile that makes the dead

blocks.text: Deïdamìa ancor si duol d’Achille,
blocks.mandelbaum: Deidamia still lament Achilles;

blocks.text: e del Palladio pena vi si porta».
blocks.mandelbaum: and there, for the Palladium, they pay.”

blocks.text: «S’ei posson dentro da quelle faville
blocks.mandelbaum: “If they can speak within those sparks,” I said,

blocks.text: parlar», diss’ io, «maestro, assai ten priego
blocks.mandelbaum: “I pray you and repray and, master, may

blocks.text: e ripriego, che ’l priego vaglia mille,
blocks.mandelbaum: my prayer be worth a thousand pleas, do not

blocks.text: che non mi facci de l’attender niego
blocks.mandelbaum: forbid my waiting here until the flame

blocks.text: fin che la fiamma cornuta qua vegna;
blocks.mandelbaum: with horns approaches us; for you can see

blocks.text: vedi che del disio ver’ lei mi piego!».
blocks.mandelbaum: how, out of my desire, I bend toward it.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «La tua preghiera è degna
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “What you have asked is worthy

blocks.text: di molta loda, e io però l’accetto;
blocks.mandelbaum: of every praise; therefore, I favor it.

blocks.text: ma fa che la tua lingua si sostegna.
blocks.mandelbaum: I only ask you this: refrain from talking.

blocks.text: Lascia parlare a me, ch’i’ ho concetto
blocks.mandelbaum: Let me address them—I have understood

blocks.text: ciò che tu vuoi; ch’ei sarebbero schivi,
blocks.mandelbaum: what you desire of them. Since they were Greek,

blocks.text: perch’ e’ fuor greci, forse del tuo detto».
blocks.mandelbaum: perhaps they’d be disdainful of your speech.”

blocks.text: Poi che la fiamma fu venuta quivi
blocks.mandelbaum: And when my guide adjudged the flame had reached

blocks.text: dove parve al mio duca tempo e loco,
blocks.mandelbaum: a point where time and place were opportune,

blocks.text: in questa forma lui parlare audivi:
blocks.mandelbaum: this was the form I heard his words assume:

blocks.text: «O voi che siete due dentro ad un foco,
blocks.mandelbaum: “You two who move as one within the flame,

blocks.text: s’io meritai di voi mentre ch’io vissi,
blocks.mandelbaum: if I deserved of you while I still lived,

blocks.text: s’io meritai di voi assai o poco
blocks.mandelbaum: if I deserved of you much or a little

blocks.text: quando nel mondo li alti versi scrissi,
blocks.mandelbaum: when in the world I wrote my noble lines,

blocks.text: non vi movete; ma l’un di voi dica
blocks.mandelbaum: do not move on; let one of you retell

blocks.text: dove, per lui, perduto a morir gissi».
blocks.mandelbaum: where, having gone astray, he found his death.”

blocks.text: Lo maggior corno de la fiamma antica
blocks.mandelbaum: The greater horn within that ancient flame

blocks.text: cominciò a crollarsi mormorando,
blocks.mandelbaum: began to sway and tremble, murmuring

blocks.text: pur come quella cui vento affatica;
blocks.mandelbaum: just like a fire that struggles in the wind;

blocks.text: indi la cima qua e là menando,
blocks.mandelbaum: and then he waved his flame—tip back and forth

blocks.text: come fosse la lingua che parlasse,
blocks.mandelbaum: as if it were a tongue that tried to speak,

blocks.text: gittò voce di fuori e disse: «Quando
blocks.mandelbaum: and flung toward us a voice that answered: “When

blocks.text: mi diparti’ da Circe, che sottrasse
blocks.mandelbaum: I sailed away from Circe, who’d beguiled me

blocks.text: me più d’un anno là presso a Gaeta,
blocks.mandelbaum: to stay more than a year there, near Gaeta—

blocks.text: prima che sì Enëa la nomasse,
blocks.mandelbaum: before Aeneas gave that place a name—

blocks.text: né dolcezza di figlio, né la pieta
blocks.mandelbaum: neither my fondness for my son nor pity

blocks.text: del vecchio padre, né ’l debito amore
blocks.mandelbaum: for my old father nor the love I owed

blocks.text: lo qual dovea Penelopè far lieta,
blocks.mandelbaum: Penelope, which would have gladdened her,

blocks.text: vincer potero dentro a me l’ardore
blocks.mandelbaum: was able to defeat in me the longing

blocks.text: ch’i’ ebbi a divenir del mondo esperto
blocks.mandelbaum: I had to gain experience of the world

blocks.text: e de li vizi umani e del valore;
blocks.mandelbaum: and of the vices and the worth of men.

blocks.text: ma misi me per l’alto mare aperto
blocks.mandelbaum: Therefore, I set out on the open sea

blocks.text: sol con un legno e con quella compagna
blocks.mandelbaum: with but one ship and that small company

blocks.text: picciola da la qual non fui diserto.
blocks.mandelbaum: of those who never had deserted me.

blocks.text: L’un lito e l’altro vidi infin la Spagna,
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw as far as Spain, far as Morocco,

blocks.text: fin nel Morrocco, e l’isola d’i Sardi,
blocks.mandelbaum: along both shores; I saw Sardinia

blocks.text: e l’altre che quel mare intorno bagna.
blocks.mandelbaum: and saw the other islands that sea bathes.

blocks.text: Io e ’ compagni eravam vecchi e tardi
blocks.mandelbaum: And I and my companions were already

blocks.text: quando venimmo a quella foce stretta
blocks.mandelbaum: old and slow, when we approached the narrows

blocks.text: dov’ Ercule segnò li suoi riguardi
blocks.mandelbaum: where Hercules set up his boundary stones

blocks.text: acciò che l’uom più oltre non si metta;
blocks.mandelbaum: that men might heed and never reach beyond:

blocks.text: da la man destra mi lasciai Sibilia,
blocks.mandelbaum: upon my right, I had gone past Seville,

blocks.text: da l’altra già m’avea lasciata Setta.
blocks.mandelbaum: and on the left, already passed Ceuta.

blocks.text: “O frati”, dissi “che per cento milia
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘Brothers,’ I said, ‘o you, who having crossed

blocks.text: perigli siete giunti a l’occidente,
blocks.mandelbaum: a hundred thousand dangers, reach the west,

blocks.text: a questa tanto picciola vigilia
blocks.mandelbaum: to this brief waking—time that still is left

blocks.text: d’i nostri sensi ch’è del rimanente
blocks.mandelbaum: unto your senses, you must not deny

blocks.text: non vogliate negar l’esperïenza,
blocks.mandelbaum: experience of that which lies beyond

blocks.text: di retro al sol, del mondo sanza gente.
blocks.mandelbaum: the sun, and of the world that is unpeopled.

blocks.text: Considerate la vostra semenza:
blocks.mandelbaum: Consider well the seed that gave you birth:

blocks.text: fatti non foste a viver come bruti,
blocks.mandelbaum: you were not made to live your lives as brutes,

blocks.text: ma per seguir virtute e canoscenza”.
blocks.mandelbaum: but to be followers of worth and knowledge.’

blocks.text: Li miei compagni fec’ io sì aguti,
blocks.mandelbaum: I spurred my comrades with this brief address

blocks.text: con questa orazion picciola, al cammino,
blocks.mandelbaum: to meet the journey with such eagerness

blocks.text: che a pena poscia li avrei ritenuti;
blocks.mandelbaum: that I could hardly, then, have held them back;

blocks.text: e volta nostra poppa nel mattino,
blocks.mandelbaum: and having turned our stern toward morning, we

blocks.text: de’ remi facemmo ali al folle volo,
blocks.mandelbaum: made wings out of our oars in a wild flight

blocks.text: sempre acquistando dal lato mancino.
blocks.mandelbaum: and always gained upon our left—hand side.

blocks.text: Tutte le stelle già de l’altro polo
blocks.mandelbaum: At night I now could see the other pole

blocks.text: vedea la notte, e ’l nostro tanto basso,
blocks.mandelbaum: and all its stars; the star of ours had fallen

blocks.text: che non surgëa fuor del marin suolo.
blocks.mandelbaum: and never rose above the plain of the ocean.

blocks.text: Cinque volte racceso e tante casso
blocks.mandelbaum: Five times the light beneath the moon had been

blocks.text: lo lume era di sotto da la luna,
blocks.mandelbaum: rekindled, and, as many times, was spent,

blocks.text: poi che ’ntrati eravam ne l’alto passo,
blocks.mandelbaum: since that hard passage faced our first attempt,

blocks.text: quando n’apparve una montagna, bruna
blocks.mandelbaum: when there before us rose a mountain, dark

blocks.text: per la distanza, e parvemi alta tanto
blocks.mandelbaum: because of distance, and it seemed to me

blocks.text: quanto veduta non avëa alcuna.
blocks.mandelbaum: the highest mountain I had ever seen.

blocks.text: Noi ci allegrammo, e tosto tornò in pianto;
blocks.mandelbaum: And we were glad, but this soon turned to sorrow,

blocks.text: ché de la nova terra un turbo nacque
blocks.mandelbaum: for out of that new land a whirlwind rose

blocks.text: e percosse del legno il primo canto.
blocks.mandelbaum: and hammered at our ship, against her bow.

blocks.text: Tre volte il fé girar con tutte l’acque;
blocks.mandelbaum: Three times it turned her round with all the waters;

blocks.text: a la quarta levar la poppa in suso
blocks.mandelbaum: and at the fourth, it lifted up the stern

blocks.text: e la prora ire in giù, com’ altrui piacque,
blocks.mandelbaum: so that our prow plunged deep, as pleased an Other,

blocks.text: infin che ’l mar fu sovra noi richiuso».
blocks.mandelbaum: until the sea again closed—over us.”

blocks.text: Già era dritta in sù la fiamma e queta
blocks.mandelbaum: The flame already was erect and silent —

blocks.text: per non dir più, e già da noi sen gia
blocks.mandelbaum: it had no more to say. Now it had left us

blocks.text: con la licenza del dolce poeta,
blocks.mandelbaum: with the permission of the gentle poet,

blocks.text: quand’ un’altra, che dietro a lei venìa,
blocks.mandelbaum: when, just behind it, came another flame

blocks.text: ne fece volger li occhi a la sua cima
blocks.mandelbaum: that drew our eyes to watch its tip because

blocks.text: per un confuso suon che fuor n’uscia.
blocks.mandelbaum: of the perplexing sound that it sent forth.

blocks.text: Come ’l bue cicilian che mugghiò prima
blocks.mandelbaum: Even as the Sicilian bull (that first

blocks.text: col pianto di colui, e ciò fu dritto,
blocks.mandelbaum: had bellowed with the cry — and this was just —

blocks.text: che l’avea temperato con sua lima,
blocks.mandelbaum: of him who shaped it with his instruments)

blocks.text: mugghiava con la voce de l’afflitto,
blocks.mandelbaum: would always bellow with its victim’s voice,

blocks.text: sì che, con tutto che fosse di rame,
blocks.mandelbaum: so that, although that bull was only brass,

blocks.text: pur el pareva dal dolor trafitto;
blocks.mandelbaum: it seemed as if it were pierced through by pain;

blocks.text: così, per non aver via né forame
blocks.mandelbaum: so were the helpless words that, from the first,

blocks.text: dal principio nel foco, in suo linguaggio
blocks.mandelbaum: had found no path or exit from the flame,

blocks.text: si convertïan le parole grame.
blocks.mandelbaum: transformed into the language of the fire.

blocks.text: Ma poscia ch’ebber colto lor vïaggio
blocks.mandelbaum: But after they had found their way up toward

blocks.text: su per la punta, dandole quel guizzo
blocks.mandelbaum: the tip, and given it that movement which

blocks.text: che dato avea la lingua in lor passaggio,
blocks.mandelbaum: the tongue had given them along their passage,

blocks.text: udimmo dire: «O tu a cu’ io drizzo
blocks.mandelbaum: we heard: “O you to whom I turn my voice,

blocks.text: la voce e che parlavi mo lombardo,
blocks.mandelbaum: who only now were talking Lombard, saying,

blocks.text: dicendo “Istra ten va, più non t’adizzo”,
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘Now you may leave — I’ll not provoke more speech,’

blocks.text: perch’ io sia giunto forse alquanto tardo,
blocks.mandelbaum: though I have come perhaps a little late,

blocks.text: non t’incresca restare a parlar meco;
blocks.mandelbaum: may it not trouble you to stop and speak

blocks.text: vedi che non incresce a me, e ardo!
blocks.mandelbaum: with me; see how I stay — and I am burning!

blocks.text: Se tu pur mo in questo mondo cieco
blocks.mandelbaum: If you have fallen into this blind world

blocks.text: caduto se’ di quella dolce terra
blocks.mandelbaum: but recently, out of the sweet Italian

blocks.text: latina ond’ io mia colpa tutta reco,
blocks.mandelbaum: country from which I carry all my guilt,

blocks.text: dimmi se Romagnuoli han pace o guerra;
blocks.mandelbaum: do tell me if the Romagnoles have peace

blocks.text: ch’io fui d’i monti là intra Orbino
blocks.mandelbaum: or war; I was from there — the hills between

blocks.text: e ’l giogo di che Tever si diserra».
blocks.mandelbaum: Urbino and the ridge where Tiber springs.”

blocks.text: Io era in giuso ancora attento e chino,
blocks.mandelbaum: I still was bent, attentive, over him,

blocks.text: quando il mio duca mi tentò di costa,
blocks.mandelbaum: when my guide nudged me lightly at the side

blocks.text: dicendo: «Parla tu; questi è latino».
blocks.mandelbaum: and said: “You speak; he is Italian.”

blocks.text: E io, ch’avea già pronta la risposta,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, who had my answer set already,

blocks.text: sanza indugio a parlare incominciai:
blocks.mandelbaum: without delay began to speak to him:

blocks.text: «O anima che se’ là giù nascosta,
blocks.mandelbaum: “O soul that is concealed below in flame,

blocks.text: Romagna tua non è, e non fu mai,
blocks.mandelbaum: Romagna is not now and never was

blocks.text: sanza guerra ne’ cuor de’ suoi tiranni;
blocks.mandelbaum: quite free of war inside its tyrants’ hearts;

blocks.text: ma ’n palese nessuna or vi lasciai.
blocks.mandelbaum: but when I left her, none had broken out.

blocks.text: Ravenna sta come stata è molt’ anni:
blocks.mandelbaum: Ravenna stands as it has stood for years;

blocks.text: l’aguglia da Polenta la si cova,
blocks.mandelbaum: the eagle of Polenta shelters it

blocks.text: sì che Cervia ricuopre co’ suoi vanni.
blocks.mandelbaum: and also covers Cervia with his wings.

blocks.text: La terra che fé già la lunga prova
blocks.mandelbaum: The city that already stood long trial

blocks.text: e di Franceschi sanguinoso mucchio,
blocks.mandelbaum: and made a bloody heap out of the French,

blocks.text: sotto le branche verdi si ritrova.
blocks.mandelbaum: now finds itself again beneath green paws.

blocks.text: E ’l mastin vecchio e ’l nuovo da Verrucchio,
blocks.mandelbaum: Both mastiffs of Verruchio, old and new,

blocks.text: che fecer di Montagna il mal governo,
blocks.mandelbaum: who dealt so badly with Montagna, use

blocks.text: là dove soglion fan d’i denti succhio.
blocks.mandelbaum: their teeth to bore where they have always gnawed.

blocks.text: Le città di Lamone e di Santerno
blocks.mandelbaum: The cities on Lamone and Santerno

blocks.text: conduce il lïoncel dal nido bianco,
blocks.mandelbaum: are led by the young lion of the white lair;

blocks.text: che muta parte da la state al verno.
blocks.mandelbaum: from summer unto winter, he shifts factions.

blocks.text: E quella cu’ il Savio bagna il fianco,
blocks.mandelbaum: That city with its side bathed by the Savio,

blocks.text: così com’ ella sie’ tra ’l piano e ’l monte,
blocks.mandelbaum: just as it lies between the plain and mountain,

blocks.text: tra tirannia si vive e stato franco.
blocks.mandelbaum: lives somewhere between tyranny and freedom.

blocks.text: Ora chi se’, ti priego che ne conte;
blocks.mandelbaum: And now, I pray you, tell me who you are:

blocks.text: non esser duro più ch’altri sia stato,
blocks.mandelbaum: do not be harder than I’ve been with you,

blocks.text: se ’l nome tuo nel mondo tegna fronte».
blocks.mandelbaum: that in the world your name may still endure.”

blocks.text: Poscia che ’l foco alquanto ebbe rugghiato
blocks.mandelbaum: After the flame, in customary fashion,

blocks.text: al modo suo, l’aguta punta mosse
blocks.mandelbaum: had roared awhile, it moved its pointed tip

blocks.text: di qua, di là, e poi diè cotal fiato:
blocks.mandelbaum: this side and that and then set free this breath:

blocks.text: «S’i’ credesse che mia risposta fosse
blocks.mandelbaum: “If I thought my reply were meant for one

blocks.text: a persona che mai tornasse al mondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: who ever could return into the world,

blocks.text: questa fiamma staria sanza più scosse;
blocks.mandelbaum: this flame would stir no more; and yet, since none —

blocks.text: ma però che già mai di questo fondo
blocks.mandelbaum: if what I hear is true — ever returned

blocks.text: non tornò vivo alcun, s’i’ odo il vero,
blocks.mandelbaum: alive from this abyss, then without fear

blocks.text: sanza tema d’infamia ti rispondo.
blocks.mandelbaum: of facing infamy, I answer you.

blocks.text: Io fui uom d’arme, e poi fui cordigliero,
blocks.mandelbaum: I was a man of arms, then wore the cord,

blocks.text: credendomi, sì cinto, fare ammenda;
blocks.mandelbaum: believing that, so girt, I made amends;

blocks.text: e certo il creder mio venìa intero,
blocks.mandelbaum: and surely what I thought would have been true

blocks.text: se non fosse il gran prete, a cui mal prenda!,
blocks.mandelbaum: had not the Highest Priest — may he be damned! —

blocks.text: che mi rimise ne le prime colpe;
blocks.mandelbaum: made me fall back into my former sins;

blocks.text: e come e quare, voglio che m’intenda.
blocks.mandelbaum: and how and why, I’d have you hear from me.

blocks.text: Mentre ch’io forma fui d’ossa e di polpe
blocks.mandelbaum: While I still had the form of bones and flesh

blocks.text: che la madre mi diè, l’opere mie
blocks.mandelbaum: my mother gave to me, my deeds were not

blocks.text: non furon leonine, ma di volpe.
blocks.mandelbaum: those of the lion but those of the fox.

blocks.text: Li accorgimenti e le coperte vie
blocks.mandelbaum: The wiles and secret ways — I knew them all

blocks.text: io seppi tutte, e sì menai lor arte,
blocks.mandelbaum: and so employed their arts that my renown

blocks.text: ch’al fine de la terra il suono uscie.
blocks.mandelbaum: had reached the very boundaries of earth.

blocks.text: Quando mi vidi giunto in quella parte
blocks.mandelbaum: But when I saw myself come to that part

blocks.text: di mia etade ove ciascun dovrebbe
blocks.mandelbaum: of life when it is fitting for all men

blocks.text: calar le vele e raccoglier le sarte,
blocks.mandelbaum: to lower sails and gather in their ropes,

blocks.text: ciò che pria mi piacëa, allor m’increbbe,
blocks.mandelbaum: what once had been my joy was now dejection;

blocks.text: e pentuto e confesso mi rendei;
blocks.mandelbaum: repenting and confessing, I became

blocks.text: ahi miser lasso! e giovato sarebbe.
blocks.mandelbaum: a friar; and — poor me — it would have helped.

blocks.text: Lo principe d’i novi Farisei,
blocks.mandelbaum: The prince of the new Pharisees, who then

blocks.text: avendo guerra presso a Laterano,
blocks.mandelbaum: was waging war so near the Lateran —

blocks.text: e non con Saracin né con Giudei,
blocks.mandelbaum: and not against the Jews or Saracens,

blocks.text: ché ciascun suo nimico era cristiano,
blocks.mandelbaum: for every enemy of his was Christian,

blocks.text: e nessun era stato a vincer Acri
blocks.mandelbaum: and none of them had gone to conquer Acre

blocks.text: né mercatante in terra di Soldano,
blocks.mandelbaum: or been a trader in the Sultan’s lands —

blocks.text: né sommo officio né ordini sacri
blocks.mandelbaum: took no care for the highest office or

blocks.text: guardò in sé, né in me quel capestro
blocks.mandelbaum: the holy orders that were his, or for

blocks.text: che solea fare i suoi cinti più macri.
blocks.mandelbaum: my cord, which used to make its wearers leaner.

blocks.text: Ma come Costantin chiese Silvestro
blocks.mandelbaum: But just as Constantine, on Mount Soracte,

blocks.text: d’entro Siratti a guerir de la lebbre,
blocks.mandelbaum: to cure his leprosy, sought out Sylvester,

blocks.text: così mi chiese questi per maestro
blocks.mandelbaum: so this one sought me out as his instructor,

blocks.text: a guerir de la sua superba febbre;
blocks.mandelbaum: to ease the fever of his arrogance.

blocks.text: domandommi consiglio, e io tacetti
blocks.mandelbaum: He asked me to give counsel. I was silent —

blocks.text: perché le sue parole parver ebbre.
blocks.mandelbaum: his words had seemed to me delirious.

blocks.text: E’ poi ridisse: “Tuo cuor non sospetti;
blocks.mandelbaum: And then he said: ‘Your heart must not mistrust:

blocks.text: finor t’assolvo, e tu m’insegna fare
blocks.mandelbaum: I now absolve you in advance — teach me

blocks.text: sì come Penestrino in terra getti.
blocks.mandelbaum: to batter Penestrino to the ground.

blocks.text: Lo ciel poss’ io serrare e diserrare,
blocks.mandelbaum: You surely know that I possess the power

blocks.text: come tu sai; però son due le chiavi
blocks.mandelbaum: to lock and unlock Heaven; for the keys

blocks.text: che ’l mio antecessor non ebbe care”.
blocks.mandelbaum: my predecessor did not prize are two.’

blocks.text: Allor mi pinser li argomenti gravi
blocks.mandelbaum: Then his grave arguments compelled me so,

blocks.text: là ’ve ’l tacer mi fu avviso ’l peggio,
blocks.mandelbaum: my silence seemed a worse offense than speech,

blocks.text: e dissi: “Padre, da che tu mi lavi
blocks.mandelbaum: and I said: ‘Since you cleanse me of the sin

blocks.text: lunga promessa con l’attender corto
blocks.mandelbaum: long promises and very brief fulfillments

blocks.text: ti farà trïunfar ne l’alto seggio”.
blocks.mandelbaum: will bring a victory to your high throne.’

blocks.mandelbaum: Then Francis came, as soon as I was dead,

blocks.text: per me; ma un d’i neri cherubini
blocks.mandelbaum: for me; but one of the black cherubim

blocks.text: li disse: “Non portar: non mi far torto.
blocks.mandelbaum: told him: ‘Don’t bear him off; do not cheat me.

blocks.text: Venir se ne dee giù tra ’ miei meschini
blocks.mandelbaum: He must come down among my menials;

blocks.text: perché diede ’l consiglio frodolente,

blocks.text: dal quale in qua stato li sono a’ crini;
blocks.mandelbaum: since then, I’ve kept close track, to snatch his scalp;

blocks.text: per la contradizion che nol consente”.
blocks.mandelbaum: the law of contradiction won’t allow it.’

blocks.text: Oh me dolente! come mi riscossi

blocks.text: quando mi prese dicendomi: “Forse
blocks.mandelbaum: when he took hold of me and said: ‘Perhaps

blocks.text: tu non pensavi ch’io löico fossi!”.
blocks.mandelbaum: you did not think that I was a logician!’

blocks.mandelbaum: He carried me to Minos; and that monster

blocks.text: otto volte la coda al dosso duro;
blocks.mandelbaum: twisted his tail eight times around his hide

blocks.mandelbaum: and then, when he had bit it in great anger,

blocks.text: e sì vestito, andando, mi rancuro».
blocks.mandelbaum: and in this garb I move in bitterness.”

blocks.text: Quand’ elli ebbe ’l suo dir così compiuto,
blocks.mandelbaum: And when, with this, his words were at an end,

blocks.text: la fiamma dolorando si partio,
blocks.mandelbaum: the flame departed, sorrowing and writhing

blocks.text: torcendo e dibattendo ’l corno aguto.
blocks.mandelbaum: and tossing its sharp horn. We moved beyond;

blocks.text: Noi passamm’ oltre, e io e ’l duca mio,
blocks.mandelbaum: I went together with my guide, along

blocks.text: su per lo scoglio infino in su l’altr’ arco
blocks.mandelbaum: the ridge until the other arch that bridges

blocks.text: che cuopre ’l fosso in che si paga il fio
blocks.mandelbaum: the ditch where payment is imposed on those

blocks.text: a quei che scommettendo acquistan carco.
blocks.mandelbaum: who, since they brought such discord, bear such loads.

blocks.text: Chi poria mai pur con parole sciolte
blocks.mandelbaum: Who, even with untrammeled words and many

blocks.text: dicer del sangue e de le piaghe a pieno
blocks.mandelbaum: attempts at telling, ever could recount

blocks.mandelbaum: in full the blood and wounds that I now saw?

blocks.text: Ogne lingua per certo verria meno
blocks.mandelbaum: Each tongue that tried would certainly fall short

blocks.text: per lo nostro sermone e per la mente
blocks.mandelbaum: because the shallowness of both our speech

blocks.mandelbaum: his chest and said: “See how I split myself!

blocks.mandelbaum: who walks and weeps before me is Ali,

blocks.mandelbaum: Behind us here, a devil decks us out

blocks.mandelbaum: the other circling crags converge and rest,

blocks.mandelbaum: the juice of my conception would be pressed

blocks.text: più pienamente; ma perch’ io non l’abbo,
blocks.mandelbaum: more fully; but because I feel their lack,

blocks.text: non sanza tema a dicer mi conduco;
blocks.mandelbaum: I bring myself to speak, yet speak in fear;

blocks.text: ché non è impresa da pigliare a gabbo
blocks.mandelbaum: for it is not a task to take in jest,

blocks.text: discriver fondo a tutto l’universo,
blocks.mandelbaum: to show the base of all the universe—

blocks.text: né da lingua che chiami mamma o babbo.
blocks.mandelbaum: nor for a tongue that cries out, “mama,” “papa.”

blocks.text: Ma quelle donne aiutino il mio verso
blocks.mandelbaum: But may those ladies now sustain my verse

blocks.text: ch’aiutaro Anfïone a chiuder Tebe,
blocks.mandelbaum: who helped Amphion when he walled up Thebes,

blocks.text: sì che dal fatto il dir non sia diverso.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that my tale not differ from the fact.

blocks.text: Oh sovra tutte mal creata plebe
blocks.mandelbaum: O rabble, miscreated past all others,

blocks.text: che stai nel loco onde parlare è duro,
blocks.mandelbaum: there in the place of which it’s hard to speak,

blocks.text: mei foste state qui pecore o zebe!
blocks.mandelbaum: better if here you had been goats or sheep!

blocks.text: Come noi fummo giù nel pozzo scuro
blocks.mandelbaum: When we were down below in the dark well,

blocks.text: sotto i piè del gigante assai più bassi,
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath the giant’s feet and lower yet,

blocks.text: e io mirava ancora a l’alto muro,
blocks.mandelbaum: with my eyes still upon the steep embankment,

blocks.text: dicere udi’mi: «Guarda come passi:
blocks.mandelbaum: I heard this said to me: “Watch how you pass;

blocks.text: va sì, che tu non calchi con le piante
blocks.mandelbaum: walk so that you not trample with your soles

blocks.text: le teste de’ fratei miseri lassi».
blocks.mandelbaum: the heads of your exhausted, wretched brothers.”

blocks.text: Per ch’io mi volsi, e vidimi davante
blocks.mandelbaum: At this I turned and saw in front of me,

blocks.text: e sotto i piedi un lago che per gelo
blocks.mandelbaum: beneath my feet, a lake that, frozen fast,

blocks.text: avea di vetro e non d’acqua sembiante.
blocks.mandelbaum: had lost the look of water and seemed glass.

blocks.text: Non fece al corso suo sì grosso velo
blocks.mandelbaum: The Danube where it flows in Austria,

blocks.text: di verno la Danoia in Osterlicchi,
blocks.mandelbaum: the Don beneath its frozen sky, have never

blocks.text: né Tanaï là sotto ’l freddo cielo,
blocks.mandelbaum: made for their course so thick a veil in winter

blocks.text: com’ era quivi; che se Tambernicchi
blocks.mandelbaum: as there was here; for had Mount Tambernic

blocks.text: vi fosse sù caduto, o Pietrapana,
blocks.mandelbaum: or Pietrapana’s mountain crashed upon it,

blocks.text: non avria pur da l’orlo fatto cricchi.
blocks.mandelbaum: not even at the edge would it have creaked.

blocks.text: E come a gracidar si sta la rana
blocks.mandelbaum: And as the croaking frog sits with its muzzle

blocks.text: col muso fuor de l’acqua, quando sogna
blocks.mandelbaum: above the water, in the season when

blocks.text: di spigolar sovente la villana,
blocks.mandelbaum: the peasant woman often dreams of gleaning,

blocks.text: livide, insin là dove appar vergogna
blocks.mandelbaum: so, livid in the ice, up to the place

blocks.text: eran l’ombre dolenti ne la ghiaccia,
blocks.mandelbaum: where shame can show itself, were those sad shades,

blocks.text: mettendo i denti in nota di cicogna.
blocks.mandelbaum: whose teeth were chattering with notes like storks’.

blocks.text: Ognuna in giù tenea volta la faccia;
blocks.mandelbaum: Each kept his face bent downward steadily;

blocks.text: da bocca il freddo, e da li occhi il cor tristo
blocks.mandelbaum: their mouths bore witness to the cold they felt,

blocks.text: tra lor testimonianza si procaccia.
blocks.mandelbaum: just as their eyes proclaimed their sorry hearts.

blocks.text: Quand’ io m’ebbi dintorno alquanto visto,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I had looked around a while, my eyes

blocks.text: volsimi a’ piedi, e vidi due sì stretti,
blocks.mandelbaum: turned toward my feet and saw two locked so close,

blocks.text: che ’l pel del capo avieno insieme misto.
blocks.mandelbaum: the hair upon their heads had intermingled.

blocks.text: «Ditemi, voi che sì strignete i petti»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “Do tell me, you whose chests are pressed so tight,”

blocks.text: diss’ io, «chi siete?». E quei piegaro i colli;
blocks.mandelbaum: I said, “who are you?” They bent back their necks,

blocks.text: e poi ch’ebber li visi a me eretti,
blocks.mandelbaum: and when they’d lifted up their faces toward me,

blocks.text: li occhi lor, ch’eran pria pur dentro molli,
blocks.mandelbaum: their eyes, which wept upon the ground before,

blocks.text: gocciar su per le labbra, e ’l gelo strinse
blocks.mandelbaum: shed tears down on their lips until the cold

blocks.text: le lagrime tra essi e riserrolli.
blocks.mandelbaum: held fast the tears and locked their lids still more.

blocks.text: Con legno legno spranga mai non cinse
blocks.mandelbaum: No clamp has ever fastened plank to plank

blocks.text: forte così; ond’ ei come due becchi
blocks.mandelbaum: so tightly; and because of this, they butted

blocks.text: cozzaro insieme, tanta ira li vinse.
blocks.mandelbaum: each other like two rams, such was their fury.

blocks.text: E un ch’avea perduti ambo li orecchi
blocks.mandelbaum: And one from whom the cold had taken both

blocks.text: per la freddura, pur col viso in giùe,
blocks.mandelbaum: his ears, who kept his face bent low, then said:

blocks.text: disse: «Perché cotanto in noi ti specchi?
blocks.mandelbaum: “Why do you keep on staring so at us?

blocks.text: Se vuoi saper chi son cotesti due,
blocks.mandelbaum: If you would like to know who these two are:

blocks.text: la valle onde Bisenzo si dichina
blocks.mandelbaum: that valley where Bisenzio descends,

blocks.text: del padre loro Alberto e di lor fue.
blocks.mandelbaum: belonged to them and to their father Alberto.

blocks.text: D’un corpo usciro; e tutta la Caina
blocks.mandelbaum: They came out of one body; and you can

blocks.text: potrai cercare, e non troverai ombra
blocks.mandelbaum: search all Caina, you will never find

blocks.text: degna più d’esser fitta in gelatina:
blocks.mandelbaum: a shade more fit to sit within this ice—

blocks.text: non quelli a cui fu rotto il petto e l’ombra
blocks.mandelbaum: not him who, at one blow, had chest and shadow

blocks.text: con esso un colpo per la man d’Artù;
blocks.mandelbaum: shattered by Arthur’s hand; and not Focaccia;

blocks.text: non Focaccia; non questi che m’ingombra
blocks.mandelbaum: and not this sinner here who so impedes

blocks.text: col capo sì, ch’i’ non veggio oltre più,
blocks.mandelbaum: my vision with his head, I can’t see past him;

blocks.text: e fu nomato Sassol Mascheroni;
blocks.mandelbaum: his name was Sassol Mascheroni; if

blocks.text: se tosco se’, ben sai omai chi fu.
blocks.mandelbaum: you’re Tuscan, now you know who he has been.

blocks.text: E perché non mi metti in più sermoni,
blocks.mandelbaum: And lest you keep me talking any longer,

blocks.text: sappi ch’i’ fu’ il Camiscion de’ Pazzi;
blocks.mandelbaum: know that I was Camiscion de’ Pazzi;

blocks.text: e aspetto Carlin che mi scagioni».
blocks.mandelbaum: I’m waiting for Carlino to absolve me.”

blocks.text: Poscia vid’ io mille visi cagnazzi
blocks.mandelbaum: And after that I saw a thousand faces

blocks.text: fatti per freddo; onde mi vien riprezzo,
blocks.mandelbaum: made doglike by the cold; for which I shudder—

blocks.text: e verrà sempre, de’ gelati guazzi.
blocks.mandelbaum: and always will—when I face frozen fords.

blocks.text: E mentre ch’andavamo inver’ lo mezzo
blocks.mandelbaum: And while we were advancing toward the center

blocks.text: al quale ogne gravezza si rauna,
blocks.mandelbaum: to which all weight is drawn—I, shivering

blocks.text: e io tremava ne l’etterno rezzo;
blocks.mandelbaum: in that eternally cold shadow—I

blocks.text: se voler fu o destino o fortuna,
blocks.mandelbaum: know not if it was will or destiny

blocks.text: non so; ma, passeggiando tra le teste,
blocks.mandelbaum: or chance, but as I walked among the heads,

blocks.text: forte percossi ’l piè nel viso ad una.
blocks.mandelbaum: I struck my foot hard in the face of one.

blocks.text: Piangendo mi sgridò: «Perché mi peste?
blocks.mandelbaum: Weeping, he chided then: “Why trample me?

blocks.text: se tu non vieni a crescer la vendetta
blocks.mandelbaum: If you’ve not come to add to the revenge

blocks.text: di Montaperti, perché mi moleste?».
blocks.mandelbaum: of Montaperti, why do you molest me?”

blocks.text: E io: «Maestro mio, or qui m’aspetta,
blocks.mandelbaum: And I: “My master, now wait here for me,

blocks.text: sì ch’io esca d’un dubbio per costui;
blocks.mandelbaum: that I may clear up just one doubt about him;

blocks.text: poi mi farai, quantunque vorrai, fretta».
blocks.mandelbaum: then you can make me hurry as you will.”

blocks.text: Lo duca stette, e io dissi a colui
blocks.mandelbaum: My guide stood fast, and I went on to ask

blocks.text: che bestemmiava duramente ancora:
blocks.mandelbaum: of him who still was cursing bitterly:

blocks.text: «Qual se’ tu che così rampogni altrui?».
blocks.mandelbaum: “Who are you that rebukes another so?”

blocks.text: «Or tu chi se’ che vai per l’Antenora,
blocks.mandelbaum: “And who are you who go through Antenora,

blocks.text: percotendo», rispuose, «altrui le gote,
blocks.mandelbaum: striking the cheeks of others,” he replied,

blocks.text: sì che, se fossi vivo, troppo fora?».
blocks.mandelbaum: “too roughly—even if you were alive?”

blocks.text: «Vivo son io, e caro esser ti puote»,
blocks.mandelbaum: “I am alive, and can be precious to you

blocks.text: fu mia risposta, «se dimandi fama,
blocks.mandelbaum: if you want fame,” was my reply, “for I

blocks.text: ch’io metta il nome tuo tra l’altre note».
blocks.mandelbaum: can set your name among my other notes.”

blocks.text: Ed elli a me: «Del contrario ho io brama.
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “I want the contrary;

blocks.text: Lèvati quinci e non mi dar più lagna,
blocks.mandelbaum: so go away and do not harass me—

blocks.text: ché mal sai lusingar per questa lama!».
blocks.mandelbaum: your flattery is useless in this valley.”

blocks.text: Allor lo presi per la cuticagna
blocks.mandelbaum: At that I grabbed him by the scruff and said:

blocks.text: e dissi: «El converrà che tu ti nomi,
blocks.mandelbaum: “You’ll have to name yourself to me or else

blocks.text: o che capel qui sù non ti rimagna».
blocks.mandelbaum: you won’t have even one hair left up here.”

blocks.text: Ond’ elli a me: «Perché tu mi dischiomi,
blocks.mandelbaum: And he to me: “Though you should strip me bald,

blocks.text: né ti dirò ch’io sia, né mosterrolti,
blocks.mandelbaum: I shall not tell you who I am or show it,

blocks.text: se mille fiate in sul capo mi tomi».
blocks.mandelbaum: not if you pound my head a thousand times.”

blocks.text: Io avea già i capelli in mano avvolti,
blocks.mandelbaum: His hairs were wound around my hand already,

blocks.text: e tratti glien’ avea più d’una ciocca,
blocks.mandelbaum: and I had plucked from him more than one tuft

blocks.text: latrando lui con li occhi in giù raccolti,
blocks.mandelbaum: while he was barking and his eyes stared down,

blocks.text: quando un altro gridò: «Che hai tu, Bocca?
blocks.mandelbaum: when someone else cried out: “What is it, Bocca?

blocks.text: non ti basta sonar con le mascelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: Isn’t the music of your jaws enough

blocks.text: se tu non latri? qual diavol ti tocca?».
blocks.mandelbaum: for you without your bark? What devil’s at you?”

blocks.text: «Omai», diss’ io, «non vo’ che più favelle,
blocks.mandelbaum: “And now,” I said, “you traitor bent on evil,

blocks.text: malvagio traditor; ch’a la tua onta
blocks.mandelbaum: I do not need your talk, for I shall carry

blocks.text: io porterò di te vere novelle».
blocks.mandelbaum: true news of you, and that will bring you shame.”

blocks.text: «Va via», rispuose, «e ciò che tu vuoi conta;
blocks.mandelbaum: “Be off,” he answered; “tell them what you like,

blocks.text: ma non tacer, se tu di qua entro eschi,
blocks.mandelbaum: but don’t be silent, if you make it back,

blocks.text: di quel ch’ebbe or così la lingua pronta.
blocks.mandelbaum: about the one whose tongue was now so quick.

blocks.text: El piange qui l’argento de’ Franceschi:
blocks.mandelbaum: Here he laments the silver of the Frenchmen;

blocks.text: “Io vidi”, potrai dir, “quel da Duera
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘I saw,’ you then can say, ‘him of Duera,

blocks.text: là dove i peccatori stanno freschi”.
blocks.mandelbaum: down there, where all the sinners are kept cool.’

blocks.text: Se fossi domandato “Altri chi v’era?”,
blocks.mandelbaum: And if you’re asked who else was there in ice,

blocks.text: tu hai dallato quel di Beccheria
blocks.mandelbaum: one of the Beccheria is beside you—

blocks.text: di cui segò Fiorenza la gorgiera.
blocks.mandelbaum: he had his gullet sliced right through by Florence.

blocks.text: Gianni de’ Soldanier credo che sia
blocks.mandelbaum: Gianni de’ Soldanieri, I believe,

blocks.text: più là con Ganellone e Tebaldello,
blocks.mandelbaum: lies there with Ganelon and Tebaldello,

blocks.text: ch’aprì Faenza quando si dormia».
blocks.mandelbaum: he who unlocked Faenza while it slept.”

blocks.text: Noi eravam partiti già da ello,
blocks.mandelbaum: We had already taken leave of him,

blocks.text: ch’io vidi due ghiacciati in una buca,
blocks.mandelbaum: when I saw two shades frozen in one hole,

blocks.text: sì che l’un capo a l’altro era cappello;
blocks.mandelbaum: so that one’s head served as the other’s cap;

blocks.text: e come ’l pan per fame si manduca,
blocks.mandelbaum: and just as he who’s hungry chews his bread,

blocks.text: così ’l sovran li denti a l’altro pose
blocks.mandelbaum: one sinner dug his teeth into the other

blocks.text: là ’ve ’l cervel s’aggiugne con la nuca:
blocks.mandelbaum: right at the place where brain is joined to nape:

blocks.text: non altrimenti Tidëo si rose
blocks.mandelbaum: no differently had Tydeus gnawed the temples

blocks.text: le tempie a Menalippo per disdegno,
blocks.mandelbaum: of Menalippus, out of indignation,

blocks.text: che quei faceva il teschio e l’altre cose.
blocks.mandelbaum: than this one chewed the skull and other parts.

blocks.text: «O tu che mostri per sì bestial segno
blocks.mandelbaum: “O you who show, with such a bestial sign,

blocks.text: odio sovra colui che tu ti mangi,
blocks.mandelbaum: your hatred for the one on whom you feed,

blocks.text: dimmi ’l perché», diss’ io, «per tal convegno,
blocks.mandelbaum: tell me the cause,” I said; “we can agree

blocks.text: che se tu a ragion di lui ti piangi,
blocks.mandelbaum: that if your quarrel with him is justified,

blocks.text: sappiendo chi voi siete e la sua pecca,
blocks.mandelbaum: then knowing who you are and what’s his sin,

blocks.text: nel mondo suso ancora io te ne cangi,
blocks.mandelbaum: I shall repay you yet on earth above,

blocks.text: se quella con ch’io parlo non si secca».
blocks.mandelbaum: if that with which I speak does not dry up.”

blocks.text: La bocca sollevò dal fiero pasto
blocks.mandelbaum: That sinner raised his mouth from his fierce meal,

blocks.text: quel peccator, forbendola a’ capelli
blocks.mandelbaum: then used the head that he had ripped apart

blocks.text: del capo ch’elli avea di retro guasto.
blocks.mandelbaum: in back: he wiped his lips upon its hair.

blocks.text: Poi cominciò: «Tu vuo’ ch’io rinovelli
blocks.mandelbaum: Then he began: “You want me to renew

blocks.text: disperato dolor che ’l cor mi preme
blocks.mandelbaum: despairing pain that presses at my heart

blocks.text: già pur pensando, pria ch’io ne favelli.
blocks.mandelbaum: even as I think back, before I speak.

blocks.text: Ma se le mie parole esser dien seme
blocks.mandelbaum: But if my words are seed from which the fruit

blocks.text: che frutti infamia al traditor ch’i’ rodo,
blocks.mandelbaum: is infamy for this betrayer whom

blocks.text: parlar e lagrimar vedrai insieme.
blocks.mandelbaum: I gnaw, you’ll see me speak and weep at once.

blocks.text: Io non so chi tu se’ né per che modo
blocks.mandelbaum: I don’t know who you are or in what way

blocks.text: venuto se’ qua giù; ma fiorentino
blocks.mandelbaum: you’ve come down here; and yet you surely seem—

blocks.text: mi sembri veramente quand’ io t’odo.
blocks.mandelbaum: from what I hear—to be a Florentine.

blocks.text: Tu dei saper ch’i’ fui conte Ugolino,
blocks.mandelbaum: You are to know I was Count Ugolino,

blocks.text: e questi è l’arcivescovo Ruggieri:
blocks.mandelbaum: and this one here, Archbishop Ruggieri;

blocks.text: or ti dirò perché i son tal vicino.
blocks.mandelbaum: and now I’ll tell you why I am his neighbor.

blocks.text: Che per l’effetto de’ suo’ mai pensieri,
blocks.mandelbaum: There is no need to tell you that, because

blocks.text: fidandomi di lui, io fossi preso
blocks.mandelbaum: of his malicious tricks, I first was taken

blocks.text: e poscia morto, dir non è mestieri;
blocks.mandelbaum: and then was killed—since I had trusted him;

blocks.text: però quel che non puoi avere inteso,
blocks.mandelbaum: however, that which you cannot have heard—

blocks.text: cioè come la morte mia fu cruda,
blocks.mandelbaum: that is, the cruel death devised for me—

blocks.text: udirai, e saprai s’e’ m’ha offeso.
blocks.mandelbaum: you now shall hear and know if he has wronged me.

blocks.text: Breve pertugio dentro da la Muda,
blocks.mandelbaum: A narrow window in the Eagles’ Tower,

blocks.text: la qual per me ha ’l titol de la fame,
blocks.mandelbaum: which now, through me, is called the Hunger Tower,

blocks.text: e che conviene ancor ch’altrui si chiuda,
blocks.mandelbaum: a cage in which still others will be locked,

blocks.text: m’avea mostrato per lo suo forame
blocks.mandelbaum: had, through its opening, already showed me

blocks.text: più lune già, quand’ io feci ’l mal sonno
blocks.mandelbaum: several moons, when I dreamed that bad dream

blocks.text: che del futuro mi squarciò ’l velame.
blocks.mandelbaum: which rent the curtain of the future for me.

blocks.text: Questi pareva a me maestro e donno,
blocks.mandelbaum: This man appeared to me as lord and master;

blocks.text: cacciando il lupo e ’ lupicini al monte
blocks.mandelbaum: he hunted down the wolf and its young whelps

blocks.text: per che i Pisan veder Lucca non ponno.
blocks.mandelbaum: upon the mountain that prevents the Pisans

blocks.text: Con cagne magre, studïose e conte
blocks.mandelbaum: from seeing Lucca; and with lean and keen

blocks.text: Gualandi con Sismondi e con Lanfranchi
blocks.mandelbaum: and practiced hounds, he’d sent up front, before him,

blocks.text: s’avea messi dinanzi da la fronte.
blocks.mandelbaum: Gualandi and Sismondi and Lanfranchi.

blocks.text: In picciol corso mi parieno stanchi
blocks.mandelbaum: But after a brief course, it seemed to me

blocks.text: lo padre e ’ figli, e con l’agute scane
blocks.mandelbaum: that both the father and the sons were weary;

blocks.text: mi parea lor veder fender li fianchi.
blocks.mandelbaum: I seemed to see their flanks torn by sharp fangs.

blocks.text: Quando fui desto innanzi la dimane,
blocks.mandelbaum: When I awoke at daybreak, I could hear

blocks.text: pianger senti’ fra ’l sonno i miei figliuoli
blocks.mandelbaum: my sons, who were together with me there,

blocks.text: ch’eran con meco, e dimandar del pane.
blocks.mandelbaum: weeping within their sleep, asking for bread.

blocks.text: Ben se’ crudel, se tu già non ti duoli
blocks.mandelbaum: You would be cruel indeed if, thinking what

blocks.text: pensando ciò che ’l mio cor s’annunziava;
blocks.mandelbaum: my heart foresaw, you don’t already grieve;

blocks.text: e se non piangi, di che pianger suoli?
blocks.mandelbaum: and if you don’t weep now, when would you weep?

blocks.text: Già eran desti, e l’ora s’appressava
blocks.mandelbaum: They were awake by now; the hour drew near

blocks.text: che ’l cibo ne solëa essere addotto,
blocks.mandelbaum: at which our food was usually brought,

blocks.text: e per suo sogno ciascun dubitava;
blocks.mandelbaum: and each, because of what he’d dreamed, was anxious;

blocks.text: e io senti’ chiavar l’uscio di sotto
blocks.mandelbaum: below, I heard them nailing up the door

blocks.text: a l’orribile torre; ond’ io guardai
blocks.mandelbaum: of that appalling tower; without a word,

blocks.text: nel viso a’ mie’ figliuoi sanza far motto.
blocks.mandelbaum: I looked into the faces of my sons.

blocks.text: Io non piangëa, sì dentro impetrai:
blocks.mandelbaum: I did not weep; within, I turned to stone.

blocks.text: piangevan elli; e Anselmuccio mio
blocks.mandelbaum: They wept; and my poor little Anselm said:

blocks.text: disse: “Tu guardi sì, padre! che hai?”.
blocks.mandelbaum: ‘Father, you look so . . . What is wrong with you?’

blocks.text: Perciò non lagrimai né rispuos’ io
blocks.mandelbaum: At that I shed no tears and-all day long

blocks.text: tutto quel giorno né la notte appresso,
blocks.mandelbaum: and through the night that followed—did not answer

blocks.text: infin che l’altro sol nel mondo uscìo.
blocks.mandelbaum: until another sun had touched the world.

blocks.text: Come un poco di raggio si fu messo
blocks.mandelbaum: As soon as a thin ray had made its way

blocks.text: nel doloroso carcere, e io scorsi
blocks.mandelbaum: into that sorry prison, and I saw,

blocks.text: per quattro visi il mio aspetto stesso,
blocks.mandelbaum: reflected in four faces, my own gaze,

blocks.text: ambo le man per lo dolor mi morsi;
blocks.mandelbaum: out of my grief, I bit at both my hands;

blocks.text: ed ei, pensando ch’io ’l fessi per voglia
blocks.mandelbaum: and they, who thought I’d done that out of hunger,

blocks.text: di manicar, di sùbito levorsi
blocks.mandelbaum: immediately rose and told me: ‘Father,

blocks.text: e disser: “Padre, assai ci fia men doglia
blocks.mandelbaum: it would be far less painful for us if

blocks.text: se tu mangi di noi: tu ne vestisti
blocks.mandelbaum: you ate of us; for you clothed us in this

blocks.text: queste misere carni, e tu le spoglia”.
blocks.mandelbaum: sad flesh—it is for you to strip it off.’

blocks.text: Queta’mi allor per non farli più tristi;
blocks.mandelbaum: Then I grew calm, to keep them from more sadness;

blocks.text: lo dì e l’altro stemmo tutti muti;
blocks.mandelbaum: through that day and the next, we all were silent;

blocks.text: ahi dura terra, perché non t’apristi?
blocks.mandelbaum: O hard earth, why did you not open up?

blocks.text: Poscia che fummo al quarto dì venuti,
blocks.mandelbaum: But after we had reached the fourth day, Gaddo,

blocks.text: Gaddo mi si gittò disteso a’ piedi,
blocks.mandelbaum: throwing himself, outstretched, down at my feet,

blocks.text: dicendo: “Padre mio, ché non m’aiuti?”.
blocks.mandelbaum: implored me: ‘Father, why do you not help me?’

blocks.text: Quivi morì; e come tu mi vedi,
blocks.mandelbaum: And there he died; and just as you see me,

blocks.text: vid’ io cascar li tre ad uno ad uno
blocks.mandelbaum: I saw the other three fall one by one

blocks.text: tra ’l quinto dì e ’l sesto; ond’ io mi diedi,
