blocks.text: e cominciò, raggiandomi d’un riso
blocks.mandelbaum: began to smile at me so brightly that,

blocks.text: tal, che nel foco faria l’uom felice:
blocks.mandelbaum: even in fire, a man would still feel glad.

blocks.text: «Secondo mio infallibile avviso,
blocks.mandelbaum: “According to my never-erring judgment,

blocks.text: come giusta vendetta giustamente
blocks.mandelbaum: the question that perplexes you is how

blocks.text: punita fosse, t’ha in pensier miso;
blocks.mandelbaum: just vengeance can deserve just punishment;

blocks.text: ma io ti solverò tosto la mente;
blocks.mandelbaum: but I shall quickly free your mind from doubt;

blocks.text: e tu ascolta, ché le mie parole
blocks.mandelbaum: and listen carefully; the words I speak

blocks.text: di gran sentenza ti faran presente.
blocks.mandelbaum: will bring the gift of a great truth in reach.

blocks.text: Per non soffrire a la virtù che vole
blocks.mandelbaum: Since he could not endure the helpful curb

blocks.text: freno a suo prode, quell’ uom che non nacque,
blocks.mandelbaum: on his willpower, the man who was not born,

blocks.text: dannando sé, dannò tutta sua prole;
blocks.mandelbaum: damning himself, damned all his progeny.

blocks.text: onde l’umana specie inferma giacque
blocks.mandelbaum: For this, mankind lay sick, in the abyss

blocks.text: giù per secoli molti in grande errore,
blocks.mandelbaum: of a great error, for long centuries,

blocks.text: fin ch’al Verbo di Dio discender piacque
blocks.mandelbaum: until the Word of God willed to descend

blocks.text: u’ la natura, che dal suo fattore
blocks.mandelbaum: to where the nature that was sundered from

blocks.text: s’era allungata, unì a sé in persona
blocks.mandelbaum: its Maker was united to His person

blocks.text: con l’atto sol del suo etterno amore.
blocks.mandelbaum: by the sole act of His eternal Love.

blocks.text: Or drizza il viso a quel ch’or si ragiona:
blocks.mandelbaum: Now set your sight on what derives from that.

blocks.text: questa natura al suo fattore unita,
blocks.mandelbaum: This nature, thus united to its Maker,

blocks.text: qual fu creata, fu sincera e buona;
blocks.mandelbaum: was good and pure, even as when created;

blocks.text: ma per sé stessa pur fu ella sbandita
blocks.mandelbaum: but in itself, this nature had been banished

blocks.text: di paradiso, però che si torse
blocks.mandelbaum: from paradise, because it turned aside

blocks.text: da via di verità e da sua vita.
blocks.mandelbaum: from its own path, from truth, from its own life.

blocks.text: La pena dunque che la croce porse
blocks.mandelbaum: Thus, if the penalty the Cross inflicted

blocks.text: s’a la natura assunta si misura,
blocks.mandelbaum: is measured by the nature He assumed,

blocks.text: nulla già mai sì giustamente morse;
blocks.mandelbaum: no one has ever been so justly stung;

blocks.text: e così nulla fu di tanta ingiura,
blocks.mandelbaum: yet none was ever done so great a wrong,

blocks.text: guardando a la persona che sofferse,
blocks.mandelbaum: if we regard the Person made to suffer,

blocks.text: in che era contratta tal natura.
blocks.mandelbaum: He who had gathered in Himself that nature.

blocks.text: Però d’un atto uscir cose diverse:
blocks.mandelbaum: Thus, from one action, issued differing things:

blocks.text: ch’a Dio e a’ Giudei piacque una morte;
blocks.mandelbaum: God and the Jews were pleased by one same death;

blocks.text: per lei tremò la terra e ’l ciel s’aperse.
blocks.mandelbaum: earth trembled for that death and Heaven opened.

blocks.text: Non ti dee oramai parer più forte,
blocks.mandelbaum: You need no longer find it difficult

blocks.text: quando si dice che giusta vendetta
blocks.mandelbaum: to understand when it is said that just

blocks.text: poscia vengiata fu da giusta corte.
blocks.mandelbaum: vengeance was then avenged by a just court.

blocks.text: Ma io veggi’ or la tua mente ristretta
blocks.mandelbaum: But I now see your understanding tangled

blocks.text: di pensiero in pensier dentro ad un nodo,
blocks.mandelbaum: by thought on thought into a knot, from which,

blocks.text: del qual con gran disio solver s’aspetta.
blocks.mandelbaum: with much desire, your mind awaits release.

blocks.text: Tu dici: “Ben discerno ciò ch’i’ odo;
blocks.mandelbaum: You say: ‘What I have heard is clear to me;

blocks.mandelbaum: but this is hidden from me—why God willed

blocks.text: a nostra redenzion pur questo modo”.
blocks.mandelbaum: precisely this pathway for our redemption.’

blocks.text: Questo decreto, frate, sta sepulto
blocks.mandelbaum: Brother, this ordinance is buried from

blocks.text: ne la fiamma d’amor non è adulto.

blocks.text: Veramente, però ch’a questo segno
blocks.mandelbaum: Nevertheless, since there is much attempting

blocks.text: molto si mira e poco si discerne,
blocks.mandelbaum: to find this point, but little understanding,

blocks.text: dirò perché tal modo fu più degno.
blocks.mandelbaum: I shall tell why that way was the most fitting.

blocks.text: sì che dispiega le bellezze etterne.

blocks.text: Ciò che da lei sanza mezzo distilla

blocks.text: Ciò che da essa sanza mezzo piove

blocks.text: libero è tutto, perché non soggiace

blocks.text: a la virtute de le cose nove.

blocks.text: Più l’è conforme, e però più le piace;

blocks.text: ché l’ardor santo ch’ogne cosa raggia,

blocks.mandelbaum: those things most like Itself. The human being

blocks.text: l’umana creatura, e s’una manca,

blocks.text: di sua nobilità convien che caggia.
blocks.mandelbaum: then its nobility has been undone.

blocks.text: Solo il peccato è quel che la disfranca

blocks.text: e falla dissimìle al sommo bene,

blocks.text: per che del lume suo poco s’imbianca;

blocks.text: e in sua dignità mai non rivene,

blocks.mandelbaum: For when your nature sinned so totally

blocks.text: come di paradiso, fu remota;
blocks.mandelbaum: just as from Paradise, that nature parted;

blocks.text: sanza passar per un di questi guadi:

blocks.text: quando la sua semenza è già riposta,
blocks.mandelbaum: its grain is in the granary already,

blocks.text: a batter l’altra dolce amor m’invita.
blocks.mandelbaum: sweet love leads me to thresh the other stalk.

blocks.text: Tu credi che nel petto onde la costa
blocks.mandelbaum: You think that any light which human nature

blocks.text: si trasse per formar la bella guancia
blocks.mandelbaum: can rightfully possess was all infused

blocks.text: il cui palato a tutto ’l mondo costa,

blocks.text: e in quel che, forato da la lancia,
blocks.mandelbaum: the one out of whose chest was drawn the rib

blocks.text: e prima e poscia tanto sodisfece,

blocks.text: che d’ogne colpa vince la bilancia,

blocks.text: quantunque a la natura umana lece
blocks.mandelbaum: and One whose chest was transfixed by the lance,

blocks.text: aver di lume, tutto fosse infuso

blocks.text: da quel valor che l’uno e l’altro fece;
blocks.mandelbaum: outweighing them upon the scales of justice.

blocks.text: e però miri a ciò ch’io dissi suso,
blocks.mandelbaum: Therefore you wondered at my words when I—

blocks.text: quando narrai che non ebbe ’l secondo
blocks.mandelbaum: before—said that no other ever vied

blocks.text: lo ben che ne la quinta luce è chiuso.
blocks.mandelbaum: with that great soul enclosed in the fifth light.

blocks.text: Or apri li occhi a quel ch’io ti rispondo,
blocks.mandelbaum: Now let your eyes hold fast to my reply,

blocks.text: e vedräi il tuo credere e ’l mio dire
blocks.mandelbaum: and you will see: truth centers both my speech

blocks.text: nel vero farsi come centro in tondo.
blocks.mandelbaum: and your belief, just like a circle’s center.

blocks.text: Ciò che non more e ciò che può morire
blocks.mandelbaum: Both that which never dies and that which dies

blocks.text: non è se non splendor di quella idea
blocks.mandelbaum: are only the reflected light of that

blocks.text: che partorisce, amando, il nostro Sire;
blocks.mandelbaum: Idea which our Sire, with Love, begets;

blocks.text: ché quella viva luce che sì mea
blocks.mandelbaum: because the living Light that pours out so

blocks.text: dal suo lucente, che non si disuna
blocks.mandelbaum: from Its bright Source that It does not disjoin

blocks.text: da lui né da l’amor ch’a lor s’intrea,
blocks.mandelbaum: from It or from the Love intrined with them,

blocks.text: per sua bontate il suo raggiare aduna,
blocks.mandelbaum: through Its own goodness gathers up Its rays

blocks.text: quasi specchiato, in nove sussistenze,
blocks.mandelbaum: within nine essences, as in a mirror,

blocks.text: etternalmente rimanendosi una.
blocks.mandelbaum: Itself eternally remaining One.

blocks.text: Quindi discende a l’ultime potenze
blocks.mandelbaum: From there, from act to act, light then descends

blocks.text: giù d’atto in atto, tanto divenendo,

blocks.text: che più non fa che brevi contingenze;
blocks.mandelbaum: where it is such that it engenders nothing

blocks.text: e queste contingenze essere intendo
blocks.mandelbaum: but brief contingent things, by which I mean

blocks.text: le cose generate, che produce

blocks.text: con seme e sanza seme il ciel movendo.
blocks.mandelbaum: bring into being, with or without seed.

blocks.text: La cera di costoro e chi la duce
blocks.mandelbaum: The wax of such things and what shapes that wax

blocks.text: non sta d’un modo; e però sotto ’l segno
blocks.mandelbaum: are not immutable; and thus, beneath

blocks.text: idëale poi più e men traluce.

blocks.text: Ond’ elli avvien ch’un medesimo legno,
blocks.mandelbaum: Thus it can be that, in the selfsame species,

blocks.text: secondo specie, meglio e peggio frutta;

blocks.text: e voi nascete con diverso ingegno.

blocks.text: Se fosse a punto la cera dedutta
blocks.mandelbaum: For were the wax appropriately readied,

blocks.text: sì ch’io commendo tua oppinïone,

blocks.text: comincerebber le parole tue.

blocks.text: trïangol sì ch’un retto non avesse.

blocks.mandelbaum: Thus, if you note both what I said and say,

blocks.mandelbaum: by ‘matchless vision’ it is kingly prudence

blocks.text: in che lo stral di mia intenzion percuote;
blocks.mandelbaum: my arrow of intention means to strike;

blocks.text: ai regi, che son molti, e ’ buon son rari.
blocks.mandelbaum: kings, who are many, and the good are rare.

blocks.text: Con questa distinzion prendi ’l mio detto;
blocks.mandelbaum: Take what I said with this distinction then;

blocks.mandelbaum: in that way it accords with what you thought

blocks.text: del primo padre e del nostro Diletto.

blocks.text: E questo ti sia sempre piombo a’ piedi,

blocks.text: per farti mover lento com’ uom lasso

blocks.text: e al sì e al no che tu non vedi:
blocks.mandelbaum: to yes or no when you do not see clearly:

blocks.text: ché quelli è tra li stolti bene a basso,

blocks.text: che sanza distinzione afferma e nega

blocks.text: ne l’un così come ne l’altro passo;
blocks.mandelbaum: must be among the most obtuse of men;

blocks.text: e poi l’affetto l’intelletto lega.

blocks.text: Vie più che ’ndarno da riva si parte,

blocks.text: chi pesca per lo vero e non ha l’arte.

blocks.text: E di ciò sono al mondo aperte prove
blocks.mandelbaum: Of this, Parmenides, Melissus, Bryson,

blocks.mandelbaum: so did Sabellius and Arius

blocks.text: Come, da più letizia pinti e tratti,

blocks.text: nel torneare e ne la mira nota.

blocks.mandelbaum: on earth, the Highest Good—from which derives

blocks.text: onde vien la letizia che mi fascia;
blocks.mandelbaum: the joy that now enfolds me—was called I;

blocks.text: e El si chiamò poi: e ciò convene,
blocks.mandelbaum: and then He was called El. Such change must be:

blocks.mandelbaum: the ways that mortals take are as the leaves

blocks.text: in ramo, che sen va e altra vene.
blocks.mandelbaum: upon a branch—one comes, another goes.

blocks.text: Nel monte che si leva più da l’onda,
blocks.mandelbaum: On that peak rising highest from the sea,

blocks.text: fu’ io, con vita pura e disonesta,
blocks.mandelbaum: my life—first pure, then tainted—lasted from

blocks.text: da la prim’ ora a quella che seconda,

blocks.text: come ’l sol muta quadra, l’ora sesta».
blocks.mandelbaum: the sixth, when the sun shifts to a new quadrant.

blocks.text: ‘Al Padre, al Figlio, a lo Spirito Santo’,
blocks.mandelbaum: Unto the Father, Son, and Holy Ghost,

blocks.text: cominciò, ‘gloria!’, tutto ’l paradiso,

blocks.text: Ciò ch’io vedeva mi sembiava un riso

blocks.mandelbaum: the universe had smiled; my rapture had

blocks.text: intrava per l’udire e per lo viso.

blocks.text: Oh gioia! oh ineffabile allegrezza!

blocks.text: oh sanza brama sicura ricchezza!

blocks.mandelbaum: you are a living spring of hope. Lady,

blocks.text: Donna, se’ tanto grande e tanto vali,
blocks.mandelbaum: you are so high, you can so intercede,

blocks.text: che qual vuol grazia e a te non ricorre,
blocks.mandelbaum: that he who would have grace but does not seek

blocks.text: sua disïanza vuol volar sanz’ ali.
blocks.mandelbaum: your aid, may long to fly but has no wings.

blocks.text: La tua benignità non pur soccorre
blocks.mandelbaum: Your loving-kindness does not only answer

blocks.text: a chi domanda, ma molte fïate
blocks.mandelbaum: the one who asks, but it is often ready

blocks.text: liberamente al dimandar precorre.
blocks.mandelbaum: to answer freely long before the asking.

blocks.text: In te misericordia, in te pietate,
blocks.mandelbaum: In you compassion is, in you is pity,

blocks.text: in te magnificenza, in te s’aduna
blocks.mandelbaum: in you is generosity, in you

blocks.text: quantunque in creatura è di bontate.
blocks.mandelbaum: is every goodness found in any creature.

blocks.text: Or questi, che da l’infima lacuna
blocks.mandelbaum: This man—who from the deepest hollow in

blocks.text: de l’universo infin qui ha vedute
blocks.mandelbaum: the universe, up to this height, has seen

blocks.text: le vite spiritali ad una ad una,
blocks.mandelbaum: the lives of spirits, one by one—now pleads

blocks.text: supplica a te, per grazia, di virtute
blocks.mandelbaum: with you, through grace, to grant him so much virtue

blocks.text: tanto, che possa con li occhi levarsi
blocks.mandelbaum: that he may lift his vision higher still—

blocks.text: più alto verso l’ultima salute.
blocks.mandelbaum: may lift it toward the ultimate salvation.

blocks.text: E io, che mai per mio veder non arsi
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, who never burned for my own vision

blocks.text: più ch’i’ fo per lo suo, tutti miei prieghi
blocks.mandelbaum: more than I burn for his, do offer you

blocks.text: ti porgo, e priego che non sieno scarsi,
blocks.mandelbaum: all of my prayers—and pray that they may not

blocks.text: perché tu ogne nube li disleghi
blocks.mandelbaum: fall short—that, with your prayers, you may disperse

blocks.text: di sua mortalità co’ prieghi tuoi,
blocks.mandelbaum: all of the clouds of his mortality

blocks.text: sì che ’l sommo piacer li si dispieghi.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that the Highest Joy be his to see.

blocks.text: Ancor ti priego, regina, che puoi
blocks.mandelbaum: This, too, O Queen, who can do what you would,

blocks.text: ciò che tu vuoli, che conservi sani,
blocks.mandelbaum: I ask of you: that after such a vision,

blocks.text: dopo tanto veder, li affetti suoi.
blocks.mandelbaum: his sentiments preserve their perseverance.

blocks.text: Vinca tua guardia i movimenti umani:
blocks.mandelbaum: May your protection curb his mortal passions.

blocks.text: vedi Beatrice con quanti beati
blocks.mandelbaum: See Beatrice—how many saints with her!

blocks.text: per li miei prieghi ti chiudon le mani!».
blocks.mandelbaum: They join my prayers! They clasp their hands to you!”

blocks.text: Li occhi da Dio diletti e venerati,
blocks.mandelbaum: The eyes that are revered and loved by God,

blocks.text: fissi ne l’orator, ne dimostraro
blocks.mandelbaum: now fixed upon the supplicant, showed us

blocks.text: quanto i devoti prieghi le son grati;
blocks.mandelbaum: how welcome such devotions are to her;

blocks.text: indi a l’etterno lume s’addrizzaro,
blocks.mandelbaum: then her eyes turned to the Eternal Light—

blocks.text: nel qual non si dee creder che s’invii
blocks.mandelbaum: there, do not think that any creature’s eye

blocks.text: per creatura l’occhio tanto chiaro.
blocks.mandelbaum: can find its way as clearly as her sight.

blocks.text: E io ch’al fine di tutt’ i disii
blocks.mandelbaum: And I, who now was nearing Him who is

blocks.text: appropinquava, sì com’ io dovea,
blocks.mandelbaum: the end of all desires, as I ought,

blocks.text: l’ardor del desiderio in me finii.
blocks.mandelbaum: lifted my longing to its ardent limit.

blocks.text: Bernardo m’accennava, e sorridea,
blocks.mandelbaum: Bernard was signaling—he smiled—to me

blocks.text: perch’ io guardassi suso; ma io era
blocks.mandelbaum: to turn my eyes on high; but I, already

blocks.text: già per me stesso tal qual ei volea:
blocks.mandelbaum: was doing what he wanted me to do,

blocks.text: ché la mia vista, venendo sincera,
blocks.mandelbaum: because my sight, becoming pure, was able

blocks.text: e più e più intrava per lo raggio
blocks.mandelbaum: to penetrate the ray of Light more deeply—

blocks.text: de l’alta luce che da sé è vera.
blocks.mandelbaum: that Light, sublime, which in Itself is true.

blocks.text: Da quinci innanzi il mio veder fu maggio
blocks.mandelbaum: From that point on, what I could see was greater

blocks.text: che ’l parlar mostra, ch’a tal vista cede,
blocks.mandelbaum: than speech can show: at such a sight, it fails—

blocks.text: e cede la memoria a tanto oltraggio.
blocks.mandelbaum: and memory fails when faced with such excess.

blocks.text: Qual è colüi che sognando vede,
blocks.mandelbaum: As one who sees within a dream, and, later,

blocks.text: che dopo ’l sogno la passione impressa
blocks.mandelbaum: the passion that had been imprinted stays,

blocks.text: rimane, e l’altro a la mente non riede,
blocks.mandelbaum: but nothing of the rest returns to mind,

blocks.text: cotal son io, ché quasi tutta cessa
blocks.mandelbaum: such am I, for my vision almost fades

blocks.text: mia visïone, e ancor mi distilla
blocks.mandelbaum: completely, yet it still distills within

blocks.text: nel core il dolce che nacque da essa.
blocks.mandelbaum: my heart the sweetness that was born of it.

blocks.text: Così la neve al sol si disigilla;
blocks.mandelbaum: So is the snow, beneath the sun, unsealed;

blocks.text: così al vento ne le foglie levi
blocks.mandelbaum: and so, on the light leaves, beneath the wind,

blocks.text: si perdea la sentenza di Sibilla.
blocks.mandelbaum: the oracles the Sibyl wrote were lost.

blocks.text: O somma luce che tanto ti levi
blocks.mandelbaum: O Highest Light, You, raised so far above

blocks.text: da’ concetti mortali, a la mia mente
blocks.mandelbaum: the minds of mortals, to my memory

blocks.text: ripresta un poco di quel che parevi,
blocks.mandelbaum: give back something of Your epiphany,

blocks.text: e fa la lingua mia tanto possente,
blocks.mandelbaum: and make my tongue so powerful that I

blocks.text: ch’una favilla sol de la tua gloria
blocks.mandelbaum: may leave to people of the future one

blocks.text: possa lasciare a la futura gente;
blocks.mandelbaum: gleam of the glory that is Yours, for by

blocks.text: ché, per tornare alquanto a mia memoria
blocks.mandelbaum: returning somewhat to my memory

blocks.text: e per sonare un poco in questi versi,
blocks.mandelbaum: and echoing awhile within these lines,

blocks.text: più si conceperà di tua vittoria.
blocks.mandelbaum: Your victory will be more understood.

blocks.text: Io credo, per l’acume ch’io soffersi
blocks.mandelbaum: The living ray that I endured was so

blocks.text: del vivo raggio, ch’i’ sarei smarrito,
blocks.mandelbaum: acute that I believe I should have gone

blocks.text: se li occhi miei da lui fossero aversi.
blocks.mandelbaum: astray had my eyes turned away from it.

blocks.text: E’ mi ricorda ch’io fui più ardito
blocks.mandelbaum: I can recall that I, because of this,

blocks.text: per questo a sostener, tanto ch’i’ giunsi
blocks.mandelbaum: was bolder in sustaining it until

blocks.text: l’aspetto mio col valore infinito.
blocks.mandelbaum: my vision reached the Infinite Goodness.

blocks.text: Oh abbondante grazia ond’ io presunsi
blocks.mandelbaum: O grace abounding, through which I presumed

blocks.text: ficcar lo viso per la luce etterna,
blocks.mandelbaum: to set my eyes on the Eternal Light

blocks.text: tanto che la veduta vi consunsi!
blocks.mandelbaum: so long that I spent all my sight on it!

blocks.text: Nel suo profondo vidi che s’interna,
blocks.mandelbaum: In its profundity I saw—ingathered

blocks.text: legato con amore in un volume,
blocks.mandelbaum: and bound by love into one single volume—

blocks.text: ciò che per l’universo si squaderna:
blocks.mandelbaum: what, in the universe, seems separate, scattered:

blocks.text: sustanze e accidenti e lor costume
blocks.mandelbaum: substances, accidents, and dispositions

blocks.text: quasi conflati insieme, per tal modo
blocks.mandelbaum: as if conjoined—in such a way that what

blocks.text: che ciò ch’i’ dico è un semplice lume.
blocks.mandelbaum: I tell is only rudimentary.

blocks.text: La forma universal di questo nodo
blocks.mandelbaum: I think I saw the universal shape

blocks.text: credo ch’i’ vidi, perché più di largo,
blocks.mandelbaum: which that knot takes; for, speaking this, I feel

blocks.text: dicendo questo, mi sento ch’i’ godo.
blocks.mandelbaum: a joy that is more ample. That one moment

blocks.text: Un punto solo m’è maggior letargo
blocks.mandelbaum: brings more forgetfulness to me than twenty-

blocks.text: che venticinque secoli a la ’mpresa
blocks.mandelbaum: five centuries have brought to the endeavor

blocks.text: che fé Nettuno ammirar l’ombra d’Argo.
blocks.mandelbaum: that startled Neptune with the Argo’s shadow!

blocks.text: Così la mente mia, tutta sospesa,
blocks.mandelbaum: So was my mind—completely rapt, intent,

blocks.text: mirava fissa, immobile e attenta,
blocks.mandelbaum: steadfast, and motionless—gazing; and it

blocks.text: e sempre di mirar faceasi accesa.
blocks.mandelbaum: grew ever more enkindled as it watched.

blocks.text: A quella luce cotal si diventa,
blocks.mandelbaum: Whoever sees that Light is soon made such

blocks.text: che volgersi da lei per altro aspetto
blocks.mandelbaum: that it would be impossible for him

blocks.text: è impossibil che mai si consenta;
blocks.mandelbaum: to set that Light aside for other sight;

blocks.text: però che ’l ben, ch’è del volere obietto,
blocks.mandelbaum: because the good, the object of the will,

blocks.text: tutto s’accoglie in lei, e fuor di quella
blocks.mandelbaum: is fully gathered in that Light; outside

blocks.text: è defettivo ciò ch’è lì perfetto.
blocks.mandelbaum: that Light, what there is perfect is defective.

blocks.text: Omai sarà più corta mia favella,
blocks.mandelbaum: What little I recall is to be told,

blocks.text: pur a quel ch’io ricordo, che d’un fante
blocks.mandelbaum: from this point on, in words more weak than those

blocks.text: che bagni ancor la lingua a la mammella.
blocks.mandelbaum: of one whose infant tongue still bathes at the breast.

blocks.text: Non perché più ch’un semplice sembiante
blocks.mandelbaum: And not because more than one simple semblance

blocks.text: fosse nel vivo lume ch’io mirava,
blocks.mandelbaum: was in the Living Light at which I gazed—

blocks.text: che tal è sempre qual s’era davante;
blocks.mandelbaum: for It is always what It was before—

blocks.text: ma per la vista che s’avvalorava
blocks.mandelbaum: but through my sight, which as I gazed grew stronger,

blocks.text: in me guardando, una sola parvenza,
blocks.mandelbaum: that sole appearance, even as I altered,

blocks.text: mutandom’ io, a me si travagliava.
blocks.mandelbaum: seemed to be changing. In the deep and bright

blocks.text: Ne la profonda e chiara sussistenza
blocks.mandelbaum: essence of that exalted Light, three circles

blocks.text: de l’alto lume parvermi tre giri
blocks.mandelbaum: appeared to me; they had three different colors,

blocks.text: di tre colori e d’una contenenza;
blocks.mandelbaum: but all of them were of the same dimension;

blocks.text: e l’un da l’altro come iri da iri
blocks.mandelbaum: one circle seemed reflected by the second,

blocks.text: parea reflesso, e ’l terzo parea foco
blocks.mandelbaum: as rainbow is by rainbow, and the third

blocks.text: che quinci e quindi igualmente si spiri.
blocks.mandelbaum: seemed fire breathed equally by those two circles.

blocks.text: Oh quanto è corto il dire e come fioco
blocks.mandelbaum: How incomplete is speech, how weak, when set

blocks.text: al mio concetto! e questo, a quel ch’i’ vidi,
blocks.mandelbaum: against my thought! And this, to what I saw.

blocks.text: è tanto, che non basta a dicer ‘poco’.
blocks.mandelbaum: is such—to call it little is too much.

blocks.text: O luce etterna che sola in te sidi,
blocks.mandelbaum: Eternal Light, You only dwell within

blocks.text: sola t’intendi, e da te intelletta
blocks.mandelbaum: Yourself, and only You know You; Self-knowing,

blocks.text: e intendente te ami e arridi!
blocks.mandelbaum: Self-known, You love and smile upon Yourself!

blocks.text: Quella circulazion che sì concetta
blocks.mandelbaum: That circle—which, begotten so, appeared

blocks.text: pareva in te come lume reflesso,
blocks.mandelbaum: in You as light reflected—when my eyes

blocks.text: da li occhi miei alquanto circunspetta,
blocks.mandelbaum: had watched it with attention for some time,

blocks.text: dentro da sé, del suo colore stesso,
blocks.mandelbaum: within itself and colored like itself,

blocks.text: mi parve pinta de la nostra effige:
blocks.mandelbaum: to me seemed painted with our effigy,

blocks.text: per che ’l mio viso in lei tutto era messo.
blocks.mandelbaum: so that my sight was set on it completely.

blocks.text: Qual è ’l geomètra che tutto s’affige
blocks.mandelbaum: As the geometer intently seeks

blocks.text: per misurar lo cerchio, e non ritrova,
blocks.mandelbaum: to square the circle, but he cannot reach,

blocks.text: pensando, quel principio ond’ elli indige,
blocks.mandelbaum: through thought on thought, the principle he needs,

blocks.text: tal era io a quella vista nova:
blocks.mandelbaum: so I searched that strange sight: I wished to see

blocks.text: veder voleva come si convenne
blocks.mandelbaum: the way in which our human effigy

blocks.text: l’imago al cerchio e come vi s’indova;
blocks.mandelbaum: suited the circle and found place in it—

blocks.text: ma non eran da ciò le proprie penne:
blocks.mandelbaum: and my own wings were far too weak for that.

blocks.text: se non che la mia mente fu percossa
blocks.mandelbaum: But then my mind was struck by light that flashed

blocks.text: da un fulgore in che sua voglia venne.
blocks.mandelbaum: and, with this light, received what it had asked.

blocks.text: A l’alta fantasia qui mancò possa;
blocks.mandelbaum: Here force failed my high fantasy; but my

blocks.text: ma già volgeva il mio disio e ’l velle,
blocks.mandelbaum: desire and will were moved already—like

blocks.text: sì come rota ch’igualmente è mossa,
blocks.mandelbaum: a wheel revolving uniformly—by

blocks.text: l’amor che move il sole e l’altre stelle.
blocks.mandelbaum: the Love that moves the sun and the other stars.
